Without simultaneously reforming urban planning, parking policy, fuel pricing, and last-mile connectivity, every new rail line is just an expensive band-aid on a systemic failure. Billions are spent on tracks while the system remains one disruption away from collapse. Commuters who try public transport once and get stranded never come back.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government subsidises fuel (keeping driving artificially cheap), allows abundant free parking in urban areas (removing the cost incentive to use public transport), neglects pedestrian infrastructure (making it dangerous to walk to stations), and fails to invest in last-mile solutions (leaving commuters stranded between stations and destinations). Then it announces another RM50 billion rail project and claims it is “solving traffic.” This is not a transport policy — it is an infrastructure spending programme that benefits construction companies and politicians who cut ribbons, not commuters who need to get to work. LRT3 has been delayed repeatedly. MRT3 costs keep escalating. The ECRL’s ridership projections are fantasy. KRI told the government the approach doesn’t work, and the government responded by announcing more of the same.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has been building rail infrastructure for decades — KTM Komuter, LRT, Monorail, MRT1, MRT2 — yet car ownership rates keep climbing and traffic congestion keeps worsening. Each new project is announced with the same promise: this one will finally solve traffic. None has. The reason is structural: Malaysia’s urban planning is car-centric by design. Without changing the design — restricting parking, raising fuel costs, improving walkability, building bus networks — adding rail lines is like building a bigger bucket while the tap keeps running.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your own government-funded think tank says rail alone doesn’t solve traffic. Yet your transport policy is: build more rail. Reform parking policy. Reform fuel pricing. Fund bus networks. Fix pedestrian infrastructure. These are the boring, politically difficult changes that would actually make public transport work. But they don’t generate ribbon-cutting photo ops, so they don’t happen. Stop building expensive monuments to political vanity and start building a transport SYSTEM.

Sources: Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: [BM] Janji Mansuh Tol Dimungkiri — Kerajaan Bayar RM591J Untuk BEKU Kenaikan Sahaja
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-18

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: PH berjanji untuk memansuhkan tol lebuhraya dalam kedua-dua PRU14 dan PRU15. Sebaliknya, pada Disember 2025, kerajaan mengumumkan ia akan membelanjakan RM591 juta semata-mata untuk menghalang kenaikan tol di 10 lebuhraya pada 2026 — membayar syarikat konsesi dengan wang awam supaya tidak menaikkan harga. Menteri Kerja Raya mengakui memansuhkan tol sepenuhnya akan menelan belanja “berbilion.” Gerakan mengejek janji yang dimungkiri. FMT bertanya sama ada Anwar akan “mengulangi ikrar PH untuk memansuhkan tol” menjelang PRU16. Pemandu Malaysia terus membayar tol DAN cukai mereka kini mensubsidi pembekuan itu. Tempoh bebas tol perayaan juga dibatalkan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Pemansuhan tol adalah salah satu janji manifesto PH yang paling konkrit dan boleh diukur — cukup spesifik sehingga pengundi boleh mengesahkan penyampaian dengan mudah. Realitinya: bukan sahaja tol tidak dimansuhkan, kerajaan membayar syarikat konsesi lebuhraya ratusan juta setiap tahun semata-mata untuk membekukan kenaikan kadar. RM591J yang dibelanjakan untuk pembekuan boleh membiayai penambahbaikan infrastruktur sebenar. Sebaliknya, ia mengalir kepada pemegang saham konsesi — syarikat yang mempunyai hubungan politik yang mengaut keuntungan daripada orang awam yang terperangkap.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Pengakuan Menteri Kerja Raya bahawa pemansuhan akan menelan belanja “berbilion” adalah pengakuan bahawa PH membuat janji itu dengan mengetahui ia tidak boleh ditunaikan. Kamu tidak boleh berkempen atas pemansuhan tol dua kali, memenangi kerajaan dua kali, dan kemudian kata ia terlalu mahal. Sama ada kamu tahu kosnya sebelum berjanji (dalam kes itu kamu berbohong kepada pengundi) atau kamu tidak ambil peduli untuk mengiranya (dalam kes itu kamu tidak cekap). Sistem tol mewakili cukai regresif terhadap mobiliti — pekerja berpendapatan rendah yang berulang-alik jarak jauh ke kilang membayar bahagian pendapatan yang tidak seimbang untuk tol. Jawapan PH terhadap ketidaksamaan ini: bayar konsesi dengan wang cukai untuk mengekalkan status quo.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Janji pemansuhan tol telah dibuat oleh setiap gabungan pembangkang sejak awal 2000-an. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) juga tidak memansuhkan tol — walaupun 22 bulan berkuasa. Perjanjian konsesi, yang banyak dianugerahkan di bawah pentadbiran terdahulu melalui proses yang dipersoalkan, distrukturkan untuk menjamin keuntungan swasta dengan belanja awam. Tiada kerajaan yang mempunyai kehendak politik untuk berunding semula atau membeli balik konsesi.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Kamu berjanji untuk memansuhkan tol. Dua kali. Dalam dua pilihan raya umum. Kamu kini sudah berkuasa lebih tiga tahun. Tol masih ada. Kamu membelanjakan RM591J wang awam untuk MEMBEKUKAN mereka — bukan memansuhkan, membekukan. RM591J itu pergi kepada pemegang saham konsesi, bukan kepada rakyat. Berhenti membuat janji yang kamu tahu tidak boleh ditepati. Jika pemansuhan tol tidak mampu ditanggung, katakan secara jujur — dan terangkan pelan alternatif kamu. Atau lebih baik lagi, rundingan semula perjanjian konsesi yang mengunci Malaysia untuk membayar syarikat swasta selama-lamanya.

Sumber: The Edge | FMT

item.title: Toll Abolition Promise Broken — Govt Pays RM591M to FREEZE Hikes Instead
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: PH promised to abolish highway tolls in both GE14 and GE15. Instead, in December 2025, the government announced it would spend RM591 million just to prevent toll increases on 10 highways in 2026 — paying concessionaires with public money to not raise prices. The Works Minister admitted that fully abolishing tolls would cost “billions.” Gerakan mocked the broken promise. FMT asked whether Anwar would “repeat PH’s pledge to abolish tolls” ahead of GE16. Malaysian drivers continue paying tolls AND their taxes now subsidise the freeze. Festive toll-free periods were also cancelled.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Toll abolition was one of PH’s most concrete, measurable manifesto promises — specific enough that voters could easily verify delivery. The reality: not only were tolls not abolished, the government is paying highway concessionaires hundreds of millions annually just to freeze rate hikes. The RM591M spent on freezing could have funded actual infrastructure improvements. Instead, it flows to concessionaire shareholders — politically connected companies that profit from a captive public. This is the worst possible outcome: broken promise, continued tolls, AND public money transferred to private companies.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The Works Minister’s admission that abolition would cost “billions” is an admission that PH made the promise knowing it was undeliverable. You can’t campaign on toll abolition twice, win government twice, and then say it costs too much. Either you knew the cost before promising (in which case you lied to voters) or you didn’t bother calculating it (in which case you’re incompetent). The toll system represents a regressive tax on mobility — lower-income workers who commute long distances to factory jobs pay a disproportionate share of their income on tolls. PH’s response to this inequality: pay concessionaires with tax money to maintain the status quo.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The toll abolition promise has been made by every opposition coalition since the early 2000s. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) didn’t abolish tolls either — despite 22 months in government. The concession agreements, many awarded under previous administrations through questionable processes, are structured to guarantee private profits at public expense. No government has had the political will to renegotiate or buy back concessions. The concessionaires know this, which is why they keep raising toll rates and governments keep paying them not to.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You promised to abolish tolls. Twice. In two general elections. You’ve now been in power for over three years. Tolls are still there. You’re spending RM591M of public money to FREEZE them — not abolish them, freeze them. That RM591M goes to concessionaire shareholders, not to the rakyat. Stop making promises you know you can’t keep. If toll abolition is unaffordable, say so honestly — and explain your alternative plan. Or better yet, renegotiate the concession agreements that lock Malaysia into paying private companies forever. You won’t, because the concessionaires are politically connected. So the rakyat pays twice: once at the toll booth, once through taxes.

Sources: The Edge | FMT

item.title: [BM] LRT3 Ditangguh LAGI — Masalah Perisian, Ujian Gagal, Penalti RM475J Menanti
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Laluan LRT3 dari Bandar Utama ke Johan Setia — projek berbilion ringgit yang bertujuan mengubah ketersambungan di Lembah Klang — telah ditangguhkan berulang kali kerana kegagalan perisian isyarat dan ketidakupayaan menyelesaikan ujian tanpa kerosakan. Menteri Pengangkutan Anthony Loke memberi amaran kontraktor mungkin menghadapi penalti melebihi RM475 juta untuk kelewatan tersebut. Tarikh pembukaan kekal tidak disahkan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: LRT3 sepatutnya beroperasi bertahun-tahun lalu. Kelewatan berulang bermakna penumpang terus bergantung kepada kereta, memburukkan kesesakan dan pencemaran di Lembah Klang. Wang awam terikat dalam sistem yang tiada siapa boleh guna — berbilion dilaburkan dengan pulangan sifar kepada penumpang. Kegagalan perisian isyarat menunjukkan isu lebih mendalam dengan pengurusan projek, keupayaan kontraktor, dan pengawasan kerajaan.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Ancaman penalti Loke kedengaran kosong. Kerajaan mempunyai rekod berunding semula dan bukannya menguatkuasakan akibat terhadap kontraktor projek mega. Penalti diketepikan, tarikh akhir dilanjutkan, lebihan kos diserap oleh orang awam, dan tiada siapa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kelewatan infrastruktur di bawah PH tidak berbeza daripada kelewatan infrastruktur di bawah BN — kerajaan “reformasi” menyampaikan keputusan yang sama dengan kontraktor dan alasan yang sama.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Sejarah projek mega infrastruktur Malaysia adalah katalog kelewatan, lebihan kos, dan kontrak yang dirunding semula. MRT2 ditangguh. ECRL dirunding semula. Pelan Induk Pengangkutan Pulau Pinang terbantut. LRT3 kini menyertai senarai. Corak merentas pentadbiran: umum projek bercita-cita tinggi, anugerah kontrak kepada firma yang mempunyai hubungan politik, terlepas tarikh akhir, ugut penalti, runding semula secara senyap, dan ulang.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Jika penalti RM475J terhutang, kutiplah. Jika kontraktor tidak boleh menyampaikan, gantikan. Jika perisian isyarat tidak berfungsi selepas bertahun-tahun ujian, cari sebabnya dan terbitkan penemuan. Berhenti mengugut akibat yang tidak pernah kamu kuatkuasakan. Kuatkuasakan RM475J itu. Kami cabar kamu.

Sumber: The Edge | Lowyat

item.title: LRT3 Delayed AGAIN — Software Glitches, Failed Test Runs, RM475M Penalties Looming
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The LRT3 line from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia — a multi-billion ringgit project intended to transform connectivity in the Klang Valley — has been postponed repeatedly due to signalling software failures and the inability to complete fault-free test runs. Transport Minister Anthony Loke warned contractors may face penalties exceeding RM475 million for the delays. The opening date remains unconfirmed. The Edge and Lowyat both reported the latest round of testing setbacks as the system failed to achieve the required reliability benchmarks.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: LRT3 was supposed to be operational years ago. Repeated delays mean commuters continue relying on cars, worsening congestion and pollution in the Klang Valley. Public money is tied up in a system nobody can use — billions invested with zero return to commuters. The signalling software failures suggest deeper issues with project management, contractor capability, and government oversight. Meanwhile, commuters who were told LRT3 would change their lives continue sitting in traffic.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Loke’s penalty threats ring hollow. The government has a track record of renegotiating rather than enforcing consequences on mega-project contractors. Penalties get waived, deadlines get extended, cost overruns get absorbed by the public, and nobody is held accountable. Infrastructure delays under PH are no different from infrastructure delays under BN — the “reform” government delivers the same results with the same contractors and the same excuses. If the RM475M in penalties are actually enforced, it would be the first time in Malaysian infrastructure history. Don’t hold your breath.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s mega-infrastructure project history is a catalogue of delays, cost overruns, and renegotiated contracts. MRT2 was delayed. The ECRL was renegotiated (costs ballooned from RM55B to reportedly higher). The Penang Transport Master Plan is stalled. LRT3 now joins the list. The pattern across administrations: announce ambitious projects, award contracts to politically connected firms, miss deadlines, threaten penalties, renegotiate quietly, and repeat. The contractors get paid regardless. The public gets delays regardless. Only the minister cutting the ribbon changes.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: If RM475M in penalties are owed, collect them. If the contractor can’t deliver, replace them. If the signalling software doesn’t work after years of testing, find out why and publish the findings. Stop threatening consequences you never enforce. Infrastructure accountability starts with one simple principle: contracts that aren’t met should have real consequences. Under BN, they didn’t. Under PH, they still don’t. Prove us wrong. Enforce the RM475M. We dare you.

Sources: The Edge | Lowyat

item.title: [BM] Anwar Janji Hapus Kemiskinan Tegar Menjelang Q1 2026 — Sekarang Mac 2026 Dan Tiada Siapa Bercakap Tentangnya
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-07

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 7 Disember 2025, di majlis penutup program Rancakkan Madani di Putrajaya, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim mengisytiharkan bahawa kadar kemiskinan tegar Malaysia telah jatuh kepada 0.09 peratus — melibatkan kira-kira 7,000 isi rumah — dan memberitahu penjawat awam bahawa memandangkan angka itu “terlalu kecil,” kerajaan boleh “menghapuskannya menjelang suku pertama 2026.” Bernama, Malay Mail, The Star, Business Today — semuanya menjadikannya berita utama. Kini pertengahan Mac 2026, jauh masuk ke dalam Q1. Tiada pengisytiharan kejayaan, tiada kemas kini tentang 7,000 isi rumah itu, dan tiada sesiapa dalam kerajaan yang kelihatan membincangkannya.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Ini bukan kenyataan sambil lewa. Anwar menjadikan penghapusan kemiskinan tegar tunjang utama Bajet 2026, memperuntukkan RM15 bilion bantuan langsung, dan menggunakan angka 0.09% dalam pelbagai ucapan sebagai bukti kejayaan MADANI. Ahli ekonomi sudah pun mempersoalkan sama ada garis kemiskinan kerajaan — RM1,169 sebulan untuk kemiskinan tegar — benar-benar menggambarkan kesusahan hidup di negara yang sewa rumah sahaja boleh melebihi jumlah itu. Tarikh akhir Q1 2026 sepatutnya menjadi pencapaian kemuncak. Sebaliknya, ia senyap-senyap berlalu sementara kerajaan sibuk dengan krisis harga minyak, skandal Azam Baki, dan undang-undang sekatan kebebasan bersuara. Kesunyian itu memberitahu segalanya tentang betapa seriusnya janji itu dibuat.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Ini buku panduan PH yang sama: buat janji besar bergegar dengan tarikh akhir yang jelas, harap tiada siapa akan follow up, dan biarkan kitaran berita menguburkannya. Anwar bukan sekadar berkata “kita harap boleh kurangkan kemiskinan” — dia bagi suku tahun, peratusan, dan arahan khusus kepada penjawat awam. Kalau sasaran dah tercapai, mesti ada sidang akhbar, berita Bernama, dan hashtag. Ketiadaan sambutan itu sendiri adalah bukti. Sementara itu, rakyat yang sama yang Anwar janji nak angkat dari kemiskinan kini menyaksikan RON97 cecah RM3.85, diesel naik 26 peratus, dan harga makanan meningkat. 7,000 isi rumah yang berpendapatan bawah RM1,169 sebulan tak jadi lebih baik semata-mata kerana Perdana Menteri sudah beralih ke ucapan seterusnya.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Anwar ada corak menetapkan tarikh akhir yang tamat dalam kesunyian. Pada 2024, dia isytiharkan kemiskinan tegar “dihapuskan sepenuhnya” di KL, Melaka, dan Negeri Sembilan — dakwaan yang dipertikaikan ahli ekonomi yang menyatakan garis kemiskinan itu sendiri terlalu rendah untuk bermakna. Kerajaan kemudian senyap-senyap menyemak semula Garis Kemiskinan Makanan kepada RM1,236 pada 2024, yang sepatutnya menaikkan jumlah itu, bukan menurunkannya. Sebelum itu, reformasi subsidi dijanjikan “dalam masa beberapa bulan” selepas memerintah pada 2022 — kini 2026 dan subsidi bersasar hanya meliputi RON95, dengan reformasi yang lebih luas ditangguhkan selama-lamanya. Coraknya konsisten: umum, tuntut kredit, teruskan, jangan sekali-kali bertanggungjawab atas hasilnya.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Anda beritahu 35 juta rakyat Malaysia bahawa 7,000 isi rumah sahaja yang memisahkan kerajaan anda daripada penghapusan kemiskinan tegar. Anda beri diri sendiri masa sehingga Mac 2026. Sekarang Mac 2026. Mana 7,000 keluarga itu? Sudah keluar dari kemiskinan, atau anda hanya berhenti mengira? Anda jadikan ini tajuk utama Bajet, tunjang Rancakkan Madani, dan bukti MADANI berjaya. Kalau berjaya, tunjukkan resit. Kalau gagal, mengaku. Satu perkara yang anda tidak boleh buat ialah berpura-pura janji itu tidak pernah dibuat — kerana Bernama, Malay Mail, The Star, dan Business Today semuanya ada rekodnya.

Sumber: Malay Mail | Bernama | The Star | Business Today | The Vibes

item.title: Anwar Promised to End Hardcore Poverty by Q1 2026 — It's March 2026 and Nobody's Talking About It
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-07

📌 What Happened

On December 7, 2025, at the closing ceremony of the Rancakkan Madani programme in Putrajaya, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim publicly declared that Malaysia’s hardcore poverty rate had fallen to 0.09 per cent — affecting roughly 7,000 households — and told civil servants that since the number was “so small,” the government could “end it by the first quarter of 2026.” Multiple outlets — Bernama, Malay Mail, The Star, Business Today — ran the claim as headline news. It is now mid-March 2026, deep into Q1. There has been no victory declaration, no update on the 7,000 households, and no one in government appears to be talking about it.

🔥 Why It Matters

This was not an off-the-cuff remark. Anwar made hardcore poverty eradication a centrepiece of Budget 2026, allocated RM15 billion in direct assistance, and used the 0.09% figure in multiple speeches as proof of MADANI’s success. Economists had already questioned whether the government’s poverty line — RM1,169 per month for hardcore poverty — meaningfully captures deprivation in a country where urban rents alone can exceed that threshold. The Q1 2026 deadline was supposed to be the crowning achievement. Instead, it has quietly lapsed while the government is consumed by oil price crises, Azam Baki scandals, and speech-restriction laws. The silence tells you everything about how seriously the promise was made.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

This is the classic PH playbook: make a bold, headline-friendly promise with a specific deadline, count on nobody following up, and hope the news cycle buries it. Anwar didn’t just say “we hope to reduce poverty” — he gave a quarter, a percentage, and a specific instruction to civil servants. If the target had been hit, there would be a press conference, a Bernama wire, and a hashtag. The absence of celebration is the evidence. Meanwhile, the same rakyat Anwar promised to lift out of poverty are now watching RON97 hit RM3.85, diesel surge 26 per cent, and food prices climb. The 7,000 households earning under RM1,169 a month are not better off because the Prime Minister moved on to his next speech.

📜 Previous Cases

Anwar has a pattern of setting deadlines that expire in silence. In 2024, he declared hardcore poverty “totally eliminated” in KL, Melaka, and Negeri Sembilan — a claim economists disputed, noting the poverty line itself was too low to be meaningful. The government later quietly revised the Food Poverty Line to RM1,236 in 2024, which should have increased the count, not decreased it. Before that, subsidy reform was promised “within months” of taking office in 2022 — it is now 2026 and targeted subsidies cover only RON95, with the broader reform indefinitely shelved. The pattern is consistent: announce, claim credit, move on, never account for the outcome.

🎯 PH Callout

You told 35 million Malaysians that 7,000 households stood between your government and the eradication of hardcore poverty. You gave yourself until March 2026. It is March 2026. Where are the 7,000 families? Are they out of poverty, or did you just stop counting? You made this the headline of your Budget, the centrepiece of Rancakkan Madani, and the proof that MADANI works. If it worked, show the receipts. If it didn’t, say so. The one thing you cannot do is pretend the promise was never made — because Bernama, Malay Mail, The Star, and Business Today all have it on record.

Sources: Malay Mail | Bernama | The Star | Business Today | The Vibes

item.title: [BM] Hutang 1MDB Masih RM50 Bilion — Beban Fiskal Terbesar Malaysia Di Bawah Pengawasan Anwar
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-03

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Sehingga Disember 2025, hutang 1MDB Malaysia masih berjumlah kira-kira RM50 bilion — beban fiskal tunggal terbesar dalam sejarah negara. RM42 bilion telah dibayar dalam prinsipal dan faedah. RM29 bilion telah dipulihkan melalui tindakan penguatkuasaan. Kira-kira RM13 bilion masih perlu diselesaikan. Di bawah pengawasan Anwar, Najib Razak — PM yang mengetuai 1MDB — telah dipotong separuh hukuman penjara oleh Lembaga Pengampunan dan seterusnya diberikan tahanan rumah.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Setiap ringgit yang dibelanjakan untuk khidmat hutang 1MDB adalah ringgit yang TIDAK dibelanjakan untuk penjagaan kesihatan, pendidikan, infrastruktur, atau jaring keselamatan sosial. Jumlah RM50 bilion — prinsipal campur faedah — mewakili tindakan kleptokrasi tunggal terbesar dalam sejarah Malaysia. Anwar menjadikan akauntabiliti 1MDB sebagai janji utamanya. Sebaliknya, Najib mendapat hukuman yang dipotong separuh, tahanan rumah, dan bisikan mengenai kembali ke politik.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Anwar membina kerjaya politiknya untuk mempertanggungjawabkan Najib atas 1MDB. “Kerajaan Madani” sepatutnya bermakna tiada impuniti bagi rasuah. Sebaliknya, di bawah kerajaan Anwar: Lembaga Pengampunan memotong separuh hukuman Najib, mahkamah memberikan tahanan rumah, dan sekutu politik secara terbuka membincangkan “sumbangan” Najib kepada UMNO. Orang yang mencuri berbilion hidup selesa sementara 33 juta rakyat Malaysia membayar hutang yang dicipta olehnya. Ini bukan akauntabiliti — ia akomodasi.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: 1MDB telah disiasat di empat benua. DOJ AS memulihkan lebih $1 bilion dalam aset. Singapura memenjarakan bankir. Switzerland mengenakan sanksi terhadap institusi. Goldman Sachs membayar $3.9 bilion dalam penyelesaian. Malaysia — negara yang dirompak — telah melakukan paling sedikit daripada mana-mana bidang kuasa yang terlibat.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Kamu berjanji akauntabiliti 1MDB. Kamu menyampaikan hukuman yang dipotong separuh dan tahanan rumah untuk orang yang mengetuai kecurian itu. RM50 bilion jumlah kos. RM13 bilion masih berhutang. Dan Najib mungkin kembali ke politik. Jika ini rupa akauntabiliti di bawah Kerajaan Madani, perkataan itu telah kehilangan semua makna.

Sumber: Reuters | Malaysiakini

item.title: 1MDB Debt Still RM50 Billion — Malaysia's Largest Fiscal Burden Under Anwar's Watch
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-03

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As of December 2025, Malaysia’s 1MDB debt still stands at approximately RM50 billion — the largest single fiscal burden in the country’s history. RM42 billion has already been paid in principal and interest. RM29 billion has been recovered through enforcement actions. Approximately RM13 billion remains to be settled. Under Anwar’s watch, Najib Razak — the PM who presided over 1MDB — had his jail sentence halved by the Pardons Board and was subsequently granted house arrest. The rakyat continues to pay the debt while the man responsible serves a comfortable sentence.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Every ringgit spent servicing 1MDB debt is a ringgit not spent on schools, hospitals, infrastructure, or social protection. The RM50 billion total — principal plus interest — represents the largest single act of kleptocracy in Malaysian history. Anwar made 1MDB accountability his signature promise. Instead, Najib got a halved sentence, house arrest, and whispers of a political return. The debt remains. The accountability was diluted. The rakyat pays.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar built his political career on holding Najib accountable for 1MDB. “Kerajaan Madani” was supposed to mean no impunity for corruption. Instead, under Anwar’s government: the Pardons Board halved Najib’s sentence, the courts granted house arrest, and political allies openly discuss Najib’s “contribution” to UMNO. The man who stole billions lives comfortably while 33 million Malaysians service the debt he created. This is not accountability — it is accommodation.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: 1MDB has been investigated on four continents. The US DOJ recovered over $1 billion in assets. Singapore jailed bankers. Switzerland sanctioned institutions. Goldman Sachs paid $3.9 billion in settlements. Malaysia — the country that was robbed — has done the least of any jurisdiction involved. Under Najib, the investigation was suppressed. Under Muhyiddin, it continued slowly. Under Anwar, the investigation supposedly concluded but the principal figure serves a reduced, comfortable sentence.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You promised 1MDB accountability. You delivered a halved sentence and house arrest for the man who presided over the theft. RM50 billion in total cost. RM13 billion still owed. And Najib may return to politics. If this is what accountability looks like under Kerajaan Madani, the word has lost all meaning. Ensure every ringgit of 1MDB debt recovery is publicly accounted for. Block any attempt at a full pardon. Or admit that 1MDB accountability was a campaign slogan, not a governance commitment.

Sources: Reuters | Malaysiakini | DOJ

item.title: [BM] Nisbah Hutang-KDNK Masih 64.6% — Anwar Suruh Tunggu Sampai 2028 Untuk Kelegaan
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Nisbah hutang-KDNK Malaysia berada pada 64.6% pada 2024 — melebihi siling sasaran 60% kerajaan sendiri yang ditetapkan di bawah Akta Kewangan Awam dan Tanggungjawab Fiskal. Pada Disember 2025, Anwar memberitahu Bloomberg bahawa tekanan hutang akan “reda menjelang akhir 2028” — enam tahun ke dalam tempoh pemerintahannya sebelum kesihatan fiskal mula bertambah baik. Pinjaman baru dikurangkan kepada RM75 bilion daripada RM100 bilion tetapi kekal besar. Warisan 1MDB sahaja masih menyumbang RM50 bilion dalam liabiliti.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Anwar berkempen atas tanggungjawab fiskal dan menamatkan budaya hutang pentadbiran terdahulu. Sebaliknya, kerajaannya meminjam pada paras hampir rekod. Hutang-KDNK melebihi 60% bermakna penampan fiskal Malaysia terhadap kejutan ekonomi sangat nipis. Memberitahu rakyat Malaysia untuk menunggu sehingga 2028 untuk kelegaan fiskal meminta mereka mempercayai PM yang mungkin tidak berkuasa pada masa itu.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Disiplin fiskal bermakna membuat pilihan sukar sekarang — bukan menolak garis masa ke kitaran pilihan raya berikutnya. Sasaran 2028 dengan selesa jatuh selepas PRU16, bermakna Anwar mahu pengundi memilihnya semula atas janji bahawa penambahbaikan fiskal “sudah hampir” — janji yang sama dibuat pada 2022. Sementara itu, perbelanjaan terus meningkat: gaji perkhidmatan awam (1.6 juta pekerja), subsidi, projek pembangunan, dan khidmat hutang (16% hasil). Defisit struktur bermakna setiap tahun kerajaan berbelanja lebih daripada yang diperoleh, dan meminjam perbezaannya.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Setiap kerajaan Malaysia berjanji tanggungjawab fiskal sambil meminjam besar-besaran. 1MDB Najib mewujudkan warisan RM50 bilion. Perbelanjaan pandemik Muhyiddin perlu tetapi tidak dijejaki dengan baik. Anwar mewarisi keadaan huru-hara — itu benar. Tetapi tiga tahun sudah cukup untuk menunjukkan laluan yang boleh dipercayai ke penyatuan fiskal. Sebaliknya, sasaran terus bergerak: pertama 2025, kemudian 2027, kini 2028.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Kamu sudah berkuasa lebih tiga tahun. Nisbah hutang-KDNK MASIH melebihi sasaran 60% kamu sendiri. Kamu suruh rakyat Malaysia tunggu sehingga 2028 — dengan selesa selepas pilihan raya berikutnya — untuk kelegaan. Itu bukan pelan fiskal, ia strategi pilihan raya. Potong perbelanjaan yang tidak melayani rakyat. Kuatkuasakan pematuhan cukai. Berhenti meminjam untuk membiayai naungan politik.

Sumber: Bloomberg | CNA

item.title: Debt-to-GDP Still at 64.6% — Anwar Says Wait Until 2028 for Relief
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s debt-to-GDP ratio stood at 64.6% in 2024 — above the government’s own 60% target ceiling set under the Public Finance and Fiscal Responsibility Act. In December 2025, Anwar told Bloomberg that debt pressure would “ease from end-2028” — six years into his tenure before fiscal health even begins to improve. New borrowings were reduced to RM75 billion from RM100 billion but remain massive. The 1MDB legacy alone still accounts for RM50 billion in liabilities. CNA reported on Malaysia’s “increased spending” alongside the “low debt ratio” aspiration that remains unfulfilled.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Anwar campaigned on fiscal responsibility and ending the debt culture of previous administrations. Instead, his government is borrowing at near-record levels while 1MDB alone still accounts for RM50 billion in liabilities. Debt-to-GDP above 60% means Malaysia’s fiscal buffer against economic shocks is dangerously thin. If a global recession hits, or commodity prices crash, or a pandemic returns, the government has limited room to respond. Telling Malaysians to wait until 2028 for fiscal relief is asking them to trust a PM who may not even be in power by then.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Fiscal discipline means making hard choices now — not pushing timelines to the next election cycle. The 2028 target conveniently falls after GE16, meaning Anwar wants voters to re-elect him on the promise that fiscal improvement is “just around the corner” — the same promise he made in 2022. Meanwhile, spending continues to rise: civil service wages (1.6 million employees), subsidies (even after “rationalisation”), development projects (many driven by political rather than economic logic), and debt servicing (16% of revenue). The structural deficit means every year the government spends more than it earns, and borrows the difference. PH has not fundamentally changed this equation — it has just changed the rhetoric around it.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every Malaysian government has promised fiscal responsibility while borrowing heavily. Najib’s 1MDB created the RM50 billion legacy that still haunts the books. Muhyiddin’s pandemic spending was necessary but poorly tracked. Anwar inherited a mess — that’s true. But three years is enough time to demonstrate a credible path to fiscal consolidation. Instead, the target keeps moving: first it was 2025, then 2027, now 2028. The can gets kicked, the debt compounds, and the next government inherits an even bigger mess.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You’ve been in power for over three years. The debt-to-GDP ratio is STILL above your own 60% target. You’re telling Malaysians to wait until 2028 — conveniently after the next election — for relief. That’s not a fiscal plan, it’s an electoral strategy. Cut spending that doesn’t serve the public (238 political appointments, RM85.5M J-Kom propaganda, RM2.6B JAKIM). Enforce tax compliance on the wealthy and corporations. Stop borrowing to fund political patronage. Or admit that fiscal responsibility, like every other reform promise, is permanently “coming soon.”

Sources: Bloomberg | CNA

item.title: [BM] Khidmat Hutang Negara Makan 16% Hasil — Setiap Ringgit Keenam Pergi Bayar Faedah
item.description:
Said by: Ministry of Finance / Bloomberg

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Kos khidmat hutang Malaysia memakan kira-kira 16% daripada hasil kerajaan persekutuan — bermakna kira-kira setiap ringgit keenam yang dikutip dalam cukai pergi terus untuk membayar faedah hutang terkumpul dan bukannya membiayai sekolah, hospital, atau infrastruktur. Dengan nisbah hutang-KDNK pada 64.6% — melebihi sasaran 60% kerajaan sendiri — dan hutang warisan 1MDB masih berjumlah RM50 bilion dalam buku, ruang fiskal kekal amat terhad.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Khidmat hutang pada 16% hasil adalah cukai senyap terhadap masa depan. Setiap ringgit yang dibelanjakan untuk faedah adalah ringgit yang TIDAK dibelanjakan untuk penjagaan kesihatan (di mana 33,000 doktor menjalani kontrak sementara), pendidikan (di mana skor PISA merosot), infrastruktur (di mana LRT3 ditangguhkan dan jalan runtuh), atau jaring keselamatan sosial (di mana keluarga B40 bergelut dengan kos). Kesan kompaun sangat dahsyat: hutang tinggi memerlukan khidmat tinggi, yang mengurangkan ruang fiskal, yang memaksa lebih banyak pinjaman, yang meningkatkan kos khidmat masa depan. Ia gelung azab fiskal — dan PH tidak melakukan apa-apa untuk memecahkannya.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Kerajaan terus mencari komitmen perbelanjaan baru — subsidi, gaji perkhidmatan awam, projek pembangunan, 238 pelantikan politik ke GLC — yang menjadikan penyatuan fiskal sebagai sasaran yang sentiasa surut. Defisit struktur bermakna generasi masa depan rakyat Malaysia akan mewarisi timbunan hutang untuk keputusan perbelanjaan yang dibuat hari ini, dengan faedah kompaun memastikan masalah hanya membesar. Apabila Anwar kata tekanan hutang akan “reda menjelang akhir 2028,” beliau meminta rakyat Malaysia menerima enam tahun kesakitan fiskal untuk janji penambahbaikan akhirnya.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Beban hutang Malaysia bukan baru, tetapi ia telah diburukkan secara konsisten oleh perbelanjaan politik. 1MDB menambah RM50 bilion. Rangsangan pandemik di bawah Muhyiddin menambah berpuluh bilion lagi. Akta Kewangan Awam dan Tanggungjawab Fiskal sepatutnya mengenakan disiplin — tetapi sasaran hutang-KDNK 60% telah dilanggar tanpa akibat.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Setiap ringgit keenam hasil cukai pergi untuk membayar faedah — bukan membina hospital, bukan mengambil guru, bukan membaiki jalan. 16% hasil untuk khidmat hutang adalah kegagalan tadbir urus. Potong perbelanjaan boros: 238 pelantikan GLC politik, bajet propaganda RM85.5J, dan peruntukan budi bicara yang melayani politik bukannya rakyat. Kuatkuasakan pematuhan cukai korporat. Terbitkan peta jalan penyatuan fiskal yang boleh dipercayai. Atau terus tunda ke 2028 — dan biar generasi seterusnya bayar bilnya.

Sumber: Bloomberg | The Star

item.title: [BM] Konsesi Lebuhraya: Rakyat Bayar Tol Untuk Kayakan Kroni — Janji Mansuh Tol Dimungkiri LAGI
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Transport Ministry

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Janji teras manifesto PH untuk memansuhkan atau mengurangkan tol lebuhraya secara ketara telah dimungkiri secara sistematik. Daripada pemansuhan tol, kerajaan membelanjakan RM591 juta pada 2025 semata-mata untuk membekukan kenaikan tol — membayar syarikat konsesi dengan wang awam supaya tidak menaikkan harga yang sepatutnya tidak dikenakan. Tempoh bebas tol perayaan dibatalkan. Struktur asas konsesi lebuhraya — di mana syarikat swasta mengutip hasil terjamin daripada orang awam yang terperangkap sementara kerajaan menjamin keuntungan mereka — kekal sepenuhnya tidak berubah.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Sistem tol mewakili cukai regresif terhadap mobiliti: pekerja berpendapatan rendah yang berulang-alik jarak jauh ke kilang membayar bahagian pendapatan yang tidak seimbang untuk tol. Konsesi lebuhraya, banyak dianugerahkan di bawah pentadbiran terdahulu melalui proses yang dipersoalkan, menjana keuntungan besar untuk syarikat yang mempunyai hubungan politik. Kerajaan mendakwa tidak mampu membeli balik konsesi, namun menjumpai wang untuk pelantikan politik, propaganda, dan JAKIM.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Kamu berjanji memansuhkan tol. Dua kali. Dalam dua pilihan raya umum. Sebaliknya, kamu membayar syarikat swasta RM591 juta wang awam untuk mengekalkan status quo. Ini hasil yang paling teruk: janji dimungkiri, tol berterusan, DAN wang awam dipindahkan kepada pemegang saham konsesi. Perjanjian konsesi distrukturkan untuk menjamin keuntungan swasta dengan belanja awam — jumlah trafik terjamin, pulangan terjamin, pampasan kerajaan bagi kekurangan. Konsesi secara literal tidak boleh rugi.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Janji pemansuhan tol telah dibuat oleh setiap gabungan pembangkang sejak awal 2000-an. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) tidak memansuhkan tol walaupun 22 bulan berkuasa. PH 2.0 kini sudah berkuasa lebih lama dan masih tidak menyampaikan. Konsesi — banyak dikaitkan dengan ahli perniagaan yang mempunyai hubungan politik — tahu coraknya: ahli politik berjanji mansuh untuk memenangi undi, kemudian dapati ia “terlalu mahal” sebaik sahaja berkuasa.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Berapa kali kamu boleh berjanji memansuhkan tol dan tidak menyampaikan sebelum pengundi berhenti mempercayai kamu? Rundingan semula perjanjian konsesi. Terbitkan syarat — biar rakyat Malaysia lihat apa yang kerajaan mereka tandatangani untuk mereka. Jika perjanjian begitu berat sebelah sehingga membeli balik menelan belanja “berbilion,” itu sendiri adalah skandalnya.

Sumber: Paul Tan

item.title: Highway Concessions: Rakyat Pays Tolls to Make Cronies Rich — Toll Abolition Promise Broken AGAIN
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Transport Ministry

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The PH government’s core manifesto promise to abolish or significantly reduce highway tolls has been systematically broken. Instead of toll abolition, the government spent RM591 million in 2025 to merely freeze toll hikes — paying concessionaires with public money to not raise prices they shouldn’t be charging in the first place. Festive toll-free periods were cancelled. The fundamental structure of highway concessions — where private companies collect guaranteed revenue from a captive public while the government underwrites their profits — remains completely unchanged. Abolishing tolls was a core PH campaign promise in both GE14 (2018) and GE15 (2022). Three years into government, the promise has been quietly shelved with no accountability.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The toll system represents a regressive tax on mobility: lower-income workers who commute long distances to factory jobs in industrial areas pay a disproportionate share of their income on tolls. Highway concessions, many awarded under previous administrations through questionable processes, generate enormous profits for politically connected companies. The government claims it cannot afford to buy back concessions, yet finds money for political appointments, propaganda, and JAKIM. The rakyat pays twice: once at the toll booth, once through taxes that subsidise the freeze.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: You promised to abolish tolls. Twice. In two general elections. Instead, you’re paying private companies RM591 million of public money to maintain the status quo. This is the worst possible outcome: broken promise, continued tolls, AND public money transferred to concessionaire shareholders. The concession agreements are structured to guarantee private profits at public expense — guaranteed traffic volumes, guaranteed returns, government compensation for shortfalls. The concessionaires literally cannot lose. And the “reform” government has done nothing to change this structure. Not renegotiation. Not buyback. Not restructuring. Nothing.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The toll abolition promise has been made by every opposition coalition since the early 2000s. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) didn’t abolish tolls despite 22 months in government. PH 2.0 has now been in power longer and still hasn’t delivered. The concessionaires — many linked to politically connected businesspeople — know the pattern: politicians promise abolition to win votes, then discover it’s “too expensive” once in office. The tolls continue. The profits flow. The cycle repeats. And by GE16, PH will probably promise toll abolition again.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: How many times can you promise to abolish tolls and not deliver before voters stop believing you? You’ve now spent more public money FREEZING toll hikes than some countries spend building entire road networks. Renegotiate the concession agreements. Publish the terms — let Malaysians see what their government signed them up for. If the agreements are so lopsided that buying them back costs “billions,” that itself is the scandal. Who negotiated these deals? Who benefited? And why has your government done nothing to fix them in three years?

Sources: Paul Tan

item.title: National Debt Servicing Eats 16% of Revenue — Every Sixth Ringgit Goes to Paying Interest
item.description:
Said by: Ministry of Finance / Bloomberg

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s debt servicing costs consume approximately 16% of federal government revenue — meaning roughly every sixth ringgit collected in taxes goes straight to paying interest on accumulated debt rather than funding schools, hospitals, or infrastructure. With the debt-to-GDP ratio at 64.6% — above the government’s own 60% target — and 1MDB legacy debt still totalling RM50 billion on the books, fiscal space remains severely constrained. Anwar told Bloomberg that debt pressure would only “ease from end-2028.” The Star reported that 1MDB debt remains “Malaysia’s largest fiscal burden.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Debt servicing at 16% of revenue is a silent tax on the future. Every ringgit spent on interest is a ringgit NOT spent on healthcare (where 33,000 doctors are on temporary contracts), education (where PISA scores are declining), infrastructure (where LRT3 is delayed and roads crumble), or social safety nets (where B40 families struggle with costs). The compound effect is devastating: high debt requires high servicing, which reduces fiscal space, which forces more borrowing, which increases future servicing costs. It’s a fiscal doom loop — and PH has done nothing to break it.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government continues to find new spending commitments — subsidies, civil service wages, development projects, 238 political appointments to GLCs — that make fiscal consolidation an ever-receding target. The structural deficit means future generations of Malaysians will inherit a mountain of debt for spending decisions made today, with compound interest ensuring the problem only grows. When Anwar says debt pressure will “ease from end-2028,” he’s asking Malaysians to accept six years of fiscal pain for a promise of eventual improvement. That’s not a plan — it’s a hope, and hope compounds about as well as debt does.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s debt burden is not new, but it has been consistently worsened by political spending. 1MDB added RM50 billion. Pandemic stimulus under Muhyiddin added tens of billions more. Subsidy commitments under every government add annual billions. The Public Finance and Fiscal Responsibility Act was supposed to impose discipline — but the 60% debt-to-GDP target has been breached without consequence. The act has no enforcement mechanism, making it aspirational rather than binding. Every government passes fiscal responsibility laws and then ignores them.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Every sixth ringgit of tax revenue goes to paying interest — not building hospitals, not hiring teachers, not fixing roads. 16% of revenue servicing debt is a governance failure that spans administrations, but you’ve been in power long enough to own it now. Cut wasteful spending: 238 political GLC appointments, RM85.5M propaganda budgets, and discretionary allocations that serve politics rather than people. Enforce corporate tax compliance. Publish a credible fiscal consolidation roadmap with annual targets. Or keep kicking the can to 2028 — and let the next generation pay the bill.

Sources: Bloomberg | The Star

item.title: [BM] Perang Tanah Felda-Terengganu — 15,000 Hektar Dieksploitasi 40 Tahun Tanpa Bayar Negeri Sesen Pun
item.description:
Said by: Terengganu State Government / Felda

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-30

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pertikaian tanah berdekad antara Lembaga Kemajuan Tanah Persekutuan (Felda) dan kerajaan negeri Terengganu meletup pada akhir November 2025 apabila negeri mengeluarkan notis pencerobohan meliputi 10 ladang berjumlah kira-kira 15,000 hektar. Terengganu menuduh Felda membangunkan tanah negeri selama lebih 40 tahun tanpa membayar premium, cukai, atau bahagian keuntungan — pada dasarnya mengekstrak berbilion hasil minyak sawit daripada tanah negeri secara percuma. FGV Holdings juga diberikan notis. TPM Zahid akhirnya masuk campur untuk rundingan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Ini bukan pertikaian birokrasi kecil — ia kes agensi persekutuan mengekstrak berbilion daripada sebuah negeri selama empat dekad tanpa pampasan. Exco negeri Terengganu menuduh Felda menggunakan peneroka sebagai “perisai manusia,” meletakkan petani biasa di tengah-tengah apa yang pada asasnya adalah rampasan tanah korporat. Negeri seperti Pahang dan Sabah telah menerima bayaran mereka — Terengganu semata-mata diabaikan. Layanan terpilih menimbulkan persoalan: adakah kerajaan persekutuan melayan negeri yang ditadbir PN secara berbeza daripada negeri PH?

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Terengganu menuntut 30% daripada keuntungan kasar Felda pada 2024 tetapi Felda enggan mencadangkan angka balas. Kerajaan persekutuan — di bawah pengawasan PH — membenarkan GLC untuk menumpang percuma di tanah negeri selama empat dekad sementara peneroka menanggung kos reputasi. Hanya apabila Terengganu mengancam pengusiran barulah TPM Zahid masuk campur. Itu bukan tadbir urus — ia kawalan kerosakan yang dicetuskan oleh rasa malu. Jika ini negeri yang ditadbir PH, tindak balas persekutuan akan segera.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Pertikaian tanah persekutuan-negeri adalah tema berulang dalam politik Malaysia, dan ia hampir sentiasa berpecah mengikut garis politik. Pertikaian royalti minyak Kelantan dengan Petronas berlarutan berdekad di bawah BN. Sabah dan Sarawak telah berjuang untuk hak sumber sejak pembentukan Malaysia. Felda sendiri mempunyai sejarah panjang salah urus — RM4 bilion dalam pelaburan dipersoalkan di bawah FGV.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Kamu berkhotbah tentang federalisme dan layanan adil kepada semua negeri. Tetapi apabila negeri yang ditadbir PN dieksploitasi oleh agensi persekutuan, kamu memandang ke arah lain selama bertahun-tahun. Kelakuan Felda di bawah pengawasan kamu tidak boleh dibezakan daripada pengekstrakan zaman penjajah — ambil sumber, abaikan penduduk tempatan, dan berlindung di sebalik peneroka apabila ditangkap. Bayar Terengganu apa yang terhutang. Audit setiap operasi Felda di tanah negeri di seluruh negara.

Sumber: Reuters | FMT | The Vibes

item.title: Felda-Terengganu Land War — 15,000 Hectares Exploited for 40 Years Without Paying the State a Sen
item.description:
Said by: Terengganu State Government / Felda

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-30

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: A decades-long land dispute between the Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) and the Terengganu state government exploded in late November 2025 when the state issued trespass notices covering 10 plantations totalling approximately 15,000 hectares. Terengganu accused Felda of developing state land for over 40 years without paying premiums, taxes, or profit shares — essentially extracting billions in palm oil revenue from state land for free. FGV Holdings was also served notices. DPM Zahid eventually stepped in for negotiations.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This is not a minor bureaucratic dispute — it is a case of a federal agency extracting billions from a state for four decades without compensation. The Terengganu state Exco accused Felda of using settlers as “human shields,” placing ordinary farmers in the middle of what is fundamentally a corporate land grab. States like Pahang and Sabah had received their payments — Terengganu was simply being ignored. The selective treatment raises the question: does the federal government treat PN-governed states differently from PH-governed ones?

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Terengganu demanded 30% of Felda’s gross profits in 2024 but Felda refused to even propose a counter-figure. The federal government — under PH oversight — allowed a GLC to freeload on state land for four decades while settlers bore the reputational cost. Only when Terengganu threatened eviction did DPM Zahid step in. That’s not governance — that’s damage control triggered by embarrassment. If this were a PH-governed state, the federal response would have been immediate. The delay tells you everything about how this government treats opposition states.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Federal-state land disputes are a recurring theme in Malaysian politics, and they almost always break along political lines. Kelantan’s oil royalty dispute with Petronas dragged on for decades under BN. Sabah and Sarawak have fought for resource rights since Malaysia’s formation. The pattern is consistent: federal agencies extract, states complain, and resolution only comes when political pressure becomes unbearable. Felda itself has a long history of mismanagement — RM4 billion in questionable investments under FGV, settler land schemes that enriched intermediaries rather than farmers.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You preach federalism and fair treatment of all states. But when it’s a PN-governed state being exploited by a federal agency, you looked the other way for years. Felda’s behaviour under your watch is indistinguishable from colonial extraction — take the resources, ignore the locals, and hide behind the settlers when caught. Pay Terengganu what it is owed. Audit every Felda operation on state land nationwide. Publish the revenue extracted versus premiums paid for every state. Or admit that your federalism only applies to states that voted for you.

Sources: Reuters | FMT | The Vibes

item.title: [BM] Perolehan PTMP Pulau Pinang: Tiada Tender Terbuka, Hanya 'Permintaan Cadangan' Untuk Berbilion Kontrak
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow / DAP Penang / Civil Society Critics

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-21

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pelbagai laporan, termasuk dari FMT dan komuniti akademik, mendokumenkan bahawa Pelan Induk Pengangkutan Pulau Pinang (PTMP) DAP bergantung kepada mekanisme perolehan ‘permintaan cadangan’ (RFP) dan bukannya tender terbuka kompetitif untuk berbilion ringgit kontrak infrastruktur. Kumpulan masyarakat sivil mengkritik ini sebagai tidak telus dan mesra pemaju. PTMP termasuk terowong bawah laut (kini dalam perbicaraan rasuah), LRT Mutiara, dan pelbagai projek jalan raya — semuanya diperoleh tanpa proses tender terbuka standard yang dituntut PH daripada kerajaan persekutuan.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Tender terbuka kompetitif adalah piawaian emas untuk perolehan awam — ia memastikan harga terbaik, mencegah rasuah, dan membolehkan penelitian awam. DAP menuntut tender terbuka daripada kerajaan persekutuan untuk setiap kontrak melebihi RM50 juta. Tetapi di Pulau Pinang, di mana DAP mempunyai kawalan penuh, berbilion kontrak infrastruktur dianugerahkan melalui mekanisme RFP yang membenarkan negeri memilih pembida terlebih dahulu. Kemunafikan ini bersifat struktur, bukan kebetulan.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Pendirian DAP mengenai perolehan: tender terbuka untuk semua orang lain, RFP untuk kami. Parti yang mengkritik BN berdekad-dekad tentang ketelusan perolehan mengamalkan mekanisme kelam yang sama apabila mengawal tabung. Terowong bawah laut — projek utama PTMP — kini menjadi kes rasuah. Saksi SPRM menggambarkannya sebagai ‘penipuan tanah.’ Ini adalah hasil perolehan tidak telus. DAP mewujudkan keadaan untuk rasuah dengan mengelakkan langkah perlindungan yang dituntut daripada pihak lain.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Setiap skandal rasuah besar dalam infrastruktur Malaysia — dari 1MDB ke PKFZ ke Pandora Papers — melibatkan perolehan tidak telus. Jawapan DAP untuk setiap satunya adalah menuntut ketelusan dan tender terbuka. Namun di Pulau Pinang, parti yang sama menggunakan RFP untuk projeknya yang paling mahal.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Anda menuntut tender terbuka daripada Putrajaya. Anda guna RFP di Pulau Pinang. Bekas KM anda sedang dibicarakan atas projek yang diperoleh melalui kaedah tidak telus kegemaran anda. Pengajarannya jelas: amalkan tender terbuka kompetitif untuk semua projek negeri melebihi RM10 juta. Terbitkan semua dokumen perolehan. Dan berhenti menggunakan kaedah perolehan yang kes rasuah anda sendiri buktikan boleh dieksploitasi.

Sumber: FMT

item.title: Penang PTMP Procurement: No Open Tenders, Just 'Request for Proposals' for Billions in Contracts
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow / DAP Penang / Civil Society Critics

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-21

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Multiple reports, including from FMT and the academic community, documented that DAP’s Penang Transport Master Plan (PTMP) relied on ‘request-for-proposal’ (RFP) procurement mechanisms rather than open competitive tenders for billions of ringgit in infrastructure contracts. Civil society groups criticised this as opaque and developer-friendly. The PTMP includes the undersea tunnel (now under corruption trial), the Mutiara LRT, and various road projects — all procured without the standard open tender process that PH demands from the federal government.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Open competitive tendering is the gold standard for public procurement — it ensures the best price, prevents corruption, and allows public scrutiny. DAP demands open tenders from the federal government for every contract above RM50 million. But in Penang, where DAP has total control, billions in infrastructure contracts were awarded through RFP mechanisms that allow the state to pre-select bidders. The hypocrisy is structural, not incidental.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: DAP’s position on procurement is: open tenders for everyone else, RFPs for us. The party that lectured BN for decades about procurement transparency adopted the same opaque mechanisms when it controlled the purse strings. The undersea tunnel — the PTMP’s flagship project — is now a corruption case. MACC witnesses described it as a ’land scam.’ These are the outcomes of opaque procurement. DAP created the conditions for corruption by avoiding the safeguards it demands from others.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every major corruption scandal in Malaysian infrastructure — from 1MDB to PKFZ to the Pandora Papers — involved opaque procurement. DAP’s response to each was to demand transparency and open tenders. Yet in Penang, the same party used RFPs for its most expensive projects. The evidence from the Guan Eng trial suggests the procurement process was exploited for personal benefit.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You demand open tenders from Putrajaya. You use RFPs in Penang. Your former CM is on trial over a project procured through your preferred opaque method. The lesson is obvious: adopt open competitive tendering for all state projects above RM10 million. Publish all procurement documents. And stop using a procurement method that your own corruption case demonstrates can be exploited.

Sources: FMT

item.title: [BM] Ledakan Pusat Data Keringkan Air Dan Kuasa — Johor Disuruh Tunggu Hingga 2027 Untuk Bekalan Air
item.description:
Said by: Johor state government and Anwar Ibrahim

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-18

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Kegilaan pusat data Malaysia terhantuk tembok pada penghujung 2025 apabila Johor — pusat utama cita-cita lembah silikon negara — memberitahu pelabur untuk menangguhkan projek berpendingin air sekurang-kurangnya 18 bulan kerana negeri itu tidak mampu membekalkan air secukupnya. Menjelang Februari 2026, Anwar membekukan semua kelulusan pusat data bukan AI sepenuhnya. Antara 2023 dan 2025, kerajaan telah meluluskan berbilion projek pusat data daripada Microsoft, Google, AWS, Oracle, ByteDance, dan GDS Holdings — tanpa memastikan infrastruktur mampu menyokongnya. Kadar air tidak berhasil (NRW) negara ialah 37.1%, bermakna lebih satu pertiga air terawat hilang melalui paip bocor sebelum sampai kepada pengguna.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Kerajaan meraikan komitmen pelaburan pusat data berbilion sebagai bukti ekonomi Madani menarik modal global. Kini ia memberitahu pelabur yang sama untuk menunggu kerana tiada air dan kuasa elektrik tidak mencukupi. Anda tidak boleh meluluskan projek perindustrian besar-besaran yang masing-masing memakan air sebanyak sebuah bandar kecil tanpa memastikan infrastruktur mampu menyokongnya. Ini tadbir urus asas, dan kerajaan gagal kerana pengumuman pelaburan menghasilkan tajuk berita yang lebih baik daripada audit infrastruktur. Pembekuan itu mewujudkan ketidakpastian pelabur pada saat yang paling tidak sesuai — syarikat yang komited berbilion berdasarkan kelulusan kerajaan kini disuruh menunggu.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Kerajaan secara serentak meluluskan zon ekonomi khas Johor yang direka untuk menarik lebih banyak pelaburan perindustrian, mewujudkan konflik langsung antara cita-cita ekonomi dan realiti infrastruktur. Malaysia kehilangan 37.1% air terawat melalui paip bocor. Negeri seperti Perlis dan Kelantan membazir 50-60%. Membina industri intensif air di atas sistem air yang runtuh adalah kecuaian. Ironinya mencekik: kerajaan yang tidak mampu menyelenggara paip air untuk rakyatnya sendiri meluluskan pusat data yang menelan air untuk syarikat asing, kemudian terpaksa memberitahu kedua-dua kumpulan untuk menunggu. Tadbir urus melalui tajuk berita, kegagalan melalui infrastruktur.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia mempunyai pola meluluskan projek mega sebelum membina infrastruktur untuk menyokongnya. Zon pembangunan Iskandar Malaysia menjanjikan “bandar bertaraf dunia” tetapi masih bergelut dengan perkhidmatan asas. Forest City diluluskan sebagai pembangunan RM400 bilion tetapi sebahagian besar kosong. Setiap kali, kerajaan mengutamakan pengumuman pelaburan berbanding perancangan infrastruktur, kemudian kelam-kabut apabila realiti mengejar. Pembekuan pusat data adalah ulangan terbaru pola yang sama: luluskan dahulu, rancang tak pernah.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda meluluskan pelaburan pusat data berbilion tanpa memastikan bekalan air dan elektrik. Kini anda menyuruh pelabur menunggu 18 bulan. Baiki paip dahulu — 37.1% air tidak berhasil adalah memalukan negara. Terbitkan penilaian kapasiti infrastruktur nasional sebelum meluluskan sebarang projek perindustrian baharu. Berhenti mengumumkan kemenangan pelaburan yang anda tidak mampu sampaikan. Atau teruskan membina lembah silikon di atas asas paip bocor dan grid terlebih beban — dan saksikan pelabur berpindah ke Thailand dan Indonesia.

Sumber: SCMP | PV Magazine

item.title: Data Centre Boom Drains Water and Power — Johor Told to Wait Until 2027 for Water Supply
item.description:
Said by: Johor state government and Anwar Ibrahim

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s data centre gold rush hit a wall in late 2025 when Johor — ground zero for the country’s silicon valley ambitions — told investors to postpone water-cooled projects for at least 18 months because the state couldn’t supply enough water. By February 2026, Anwar froze all non-AI data centre approvals entirely. Between 2023 and 2025, the government had approved billions in data centre projects from Microsoft, Google, AWS, Oracle, ByteDance, and GDS Holdings — without ensuring the infrastructure could support them. Malaysia’s national non-revenue water rate is 37.1%, meaning over a third of treated water is lost to leaking pipes before reaching consumers.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The government celebrated billions in data centre investment commitments as proof that the Madani economy was attracting global capital. Now it’s telling those same investors to wait because there’s no water and not enough electricity. You cannot approve massive industrial projects that each consume as much water as a small city without ensuring the infrastructure can support them. This is basic governance, and the government failed at it because investment announcements make better headlines than infrastructure audits. The freeze creates investor uncertainty at exactly the wrong moment — companies that committed billions based on government approvals are now being told to wait.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government simultaneously approved a Johor special economic zone designed to attract even more industrial investment, creating a direct conflict between economic ambition and infrastructure reality. Malaysia loses 37.1% of treated water to leaking pipes. States like Perlis and Kelantan waste 50-60%. Building water-intensive industries on top of a crumbling water system is negligence. The irony is suffocating: the government that can’t maintain water pipes for its own citizens approved water-guzzling data centres for foreign corporations, then had to tell both groups to wait. Governance by headline, failure by infrastructure.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a pattern of approving mega-projects before building the infrastructure to support them. The Iskandar Malaysia development zone promised a “world-class city” but still struggles with basic services. Forest City was approved as a RM400 billion development but sits largely empty. Each time, the government prioritises investment announcements over infrastructure planning, then scrambles when reality catches up. The data centre freeze is the latest iteration of the same pattern: approve first, plan never.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You approved billions in data centre investment without ensuring water and electricity supply. Now you’re telling investors to wait 18 months. Fix the pipes first — 37.1% non-revenue water is a national embarrassment. Publish a national infrastructure capacity assessment before approving any new industrial projects. Stop announcing investment wins that you can’t deliver on. Or keep building a silicon valley on a foundation of leaking pipes and overloaded grids — and watch investors move to Thailand and Indonesia instead.

Sources: SCMP | PV Magazine

item.title: [BM] Projek Terowong Bawah Laut Pulau Pinang 'Masih Aktif' — 14 Tahun, Sifar Pembinaan, Harga RM6.3 Bilion
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-14

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 14 November 2025, Chow Kon Yeow memberitahu media bahawa Projek Terowong Bawah Laut Pulau Pinang ‘bukan projek terbengkalai malah masih aktif dan dalam fasa semakan teknikal serta penyiapan reka bentuk.’ Projek bernilai RM6.3 bilion ini dicadangkan pada 2011 di bawah Pelan Induk Pengangkutan Pulau Pinang (PTMP). Empat belas tahun kemudian, tiada satu meter pun terowong dibina. Sementara itu, perbicaraan rasuah bekas Ketua Menteri Lim Guan Eng berkaitan projek ini diteruskan, dengan saksi SPRM menggambarkannya sebagai ‘umpama penipuan tanah.’

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Projek RM6.3 bilion yang dicadangkan 14 tahun lalu dengan sifar pembinaan sama ada projek gagal atau projek hantu. Desakan Chow bahawa ia ‘masih aktif’ menguji kebolehpercayaan hingga ke tahap pecah. Projek ini sepatutnya menyelesaikan krisis trafik Pulau Pinang — yang semakin teruk sepanjang 14 tahun menunggu. Sementara itu, bekas Ketua Menteri yang meluluskan projek ini sedang dibicarakan atas rasuah berkaitannya. Pada tahap mana ‘masih aktif’ menjadi ‘masih berpura-pura’?

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: ‘Fasa semakan teknikal dan penyiapan reka bentuk’ — selepas 14 tahun. Sebagai perbandingan, China membina Jambatan Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau (lintasan laut terpanjang di dunia) dalam 9 tahun. Singapura membina laluan MRT Thomson-East Coast dalam masa yang lebih singkat. Pulau Pinang tidak mampu menyiapkan fasa reka bentuk untuk terowong yang sepatutnya dibina sedekad lalu. Projek ini wujud di atas kertas untuk mewajarkan kewujudan PTMP dan tebus guna tanah yang sepatutnya membiayainya. Terowong ini adalah infrastruktur Schrödinger — secara serentak aktif dan tidak wujud.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: PTMP dikritik dari awal kerana perolehan yang tidak telus — menggunakan mekanisme permintaan cadangan dan bukannya tender terbuka. Tebus guna Kepulauan Selatan Pulau Pinang direka sebagai mekanisme pembiayaan untuk PTMP. Jadi logiknya: musnahkan laut untuk membiayai terowong yang belum dibina selama 14 tahun. Setiap projek mewajarkan yang lain; tiada satu pun yang menyampaikan untuk orang ramai.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Empat belas tahun. RM6.3 bilion. Sifar meter dibina. Bekas Ketua Menteri dibicarakan atas rasuah berkaitan projek. Ketua Menteri semasa berkeras ia ‘masih aktif.’ Pada tahap mana anda mengakui projek ini gagal? Batalkannya, audit perbelanjaannya, dan alihkan dana kepada penyelesaian pengangkutan yang benar-benar boleh dibina — transit pantas bas, infrastruktur berbasikal, pengurusan trafik. Pengguna jalan raya Pulau Pinang perlukan penyelesaian, bukan terowong hantu.

Sumber: The Vibes

item.title: Penang Undersea Tunnel Project 'Still Active' — 14 Years, Zero Construction, RM6.3B Price Tag
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-14

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 14 November 2025, Chow Kon Yeow told media that the Penang Undersea Tunnel Project is ’not an abandoned project but is still active and in the technical review and design completion phase.’ The RM6.3 billion project was proposed in 2011 under the Penang Transport Master Plan (PTMP). Fourteen years later, not a single metre of tunnel has been constructed. Meanwhile, the corruption trial of former CM Lim Guan Eng over the project continues, with MACC witnesses describing it as ‘akin to a land scam.’

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A RM6.3 billion project proposed 14 years ago with zero construction is either a failed project or a phantom one. Chow’s insistence that it’s ‘still active’ strains credibility to breaking point. The project was supposed to solve Penang’s traffic crisis — which has only worsened during the 14-year wait. Meanwhile, the former CM who approved the project is on trial for corruption related to it. At what point does ‘still active’ become ‘still pretending’?

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: ‘Technical review and design completion phase’ — after 14 years. For comparison, China built the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge (the world’s longest sea crossing) in 9 years. Singapore built the Thomson-East Coast MRT line in less time. Penang can’t complete the design phase for a tunnel that was supposed to be built a decade ago. The project exists on paper to justify the PTMP’s existence and the land reclamation that was supposed to fund it. The tunnel is Schrödinger’s infrastructure — simultaneously active and non-existent.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The PTMP was criticised from the start for its opaque procurement — using request-for-proposal mechanisms instead of open tenders. The Penang South Islands reclamation was conceived as the funding mechanism for the PTMP. So the logic is: destroy the sea to fund a tunnel that hasn’t been built in 14 years. Each project justifies the other; neither delivers for the public.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Fourteen years. RM6.3 billion. Zero metres built. Former CM on trial for corruption related to the project. Current CM insists it’s ‘still active.’ At what point do you admit this project is a failure? Cancel it, audit the spending, and redirect the funds to transport solutions that can actually be built — bus rapid transit, cycling infrastructure, traffic management. Penang’s commuters need solutions, not phantom tunnels.

Sources: The Vibes

item.title: [BM] AS Isytihar 'Kemenangan Terhadap China' Selepas Peroleh Ikrar Mineral Tanpa Had Daripada Anwar
item.description:
Said by: MalaysiaNow report

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-03

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 3 November 2025, MalaysiaNow melaporkan bahawa Amerika Syarikat telah “mengisytiharkan kemenangan terhadap China” selepas memperoleh ikrar daripada Anwar Ibrahim untuk tidak menyekat eksport mineral kritikal dan unsur nadir bumi ke AS. Di bawah ART AS-Malaysia, Malaysia komited untuk “mengelak daripada mengharamkan, atau mengenakan kuota ke atas, eksport mineral kritikal atau unsur nadir bumi ke AS” — komitmen perdagangan yang mengikat, bukan isyarat muhibah. Malaysia juga bersetuju untuk bekerjasama dengan firma Amerika “bagi mewujudkan kepastian untuk perniagaan meningkatkan kapasiti pengeluaran,” pada dasarnya menjamin akses AS kepada sumber mineral Malaysia selama-lamanya.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Indonesia menyekat eksport nikel pada 2020 dan menggunakan daya tarikan itu untuk membina industri pemprosesan domestik yang kini bernilai berbilion dolar — menarik pelaburan pelebur, mewujudkan puluhan ribu pekerjaan, dan bergerak naik rantaian nilai. Malaysia hanya bersetuju untuk terus menghantar bahan mentah ke AS. Satu negara menggunakan sumbernya untuk berperindustrian. Yang satu lagi bersetuju untuk kekal sebagai pembekal bahan mentah. Kawalan eksport nadir bumi adalah alat geopolitik utama — mungkin yang paling berkuasa dimiliki negara kaya sumber. China mengawal 60% perlombongan nadir bumi global dan 90% pemprosesan, serta menggunakan kawalan eksport sebagai daya tarikan geopolitik. Malaysia menyerahkan alat strategik yang sama sebagai pertukaran pengurangan tarif daripada 25% kepada 19%.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Apabila AS “mengisytiharkan kemenangan terhadap China” menggunakan sumber anda, anda bukan rakan kongsi — anda adalah bidak. Penyampaian itu sendiri menjelaskan segalanya: ini tentang kepentingan strategik AS, dan Malaysia adalah alat. Kos jangka panjang tidak ternilai. Mineral kritikal adalah terhad. Sebaik sahaja komited kepada eksport tanpa sekatan, Malaysia tidak boleh kemudiannya mengenakan kawalan tanpa melanggar ART dan mencetuskan tindakan balas AS. Negara ini telah mengunci dirinya dalam kebergantungan bahan mentah. Penyokong Anwar berhujah perjanjian itu mengekalkan akses pasaran dan menarik pelaburan. Tetapi akses pasaran dengan kos kedaulatan sumber adalah perdagangan yang rugi. Tanya mana-mana negara yang mengeksport sumbernya tanpa menambah nilai — mereka semua masih negara membangun.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Sejarah eksport sumber Malaysia adalah kisah amaran. Negara ini pernah menjadi pengeluar timah terbesar dunia — ia mengeksport timah mentah selama beberapa dekad sementara negara lain membina industri pemprosesan. Menjelang Malaysia cuba menambah nilai, pasaran timah telah berubah. Pola yang sama berlaku dengan getah, minyak sawit, dan petroleum. Setiap kali, Malaysia mengeksport komoditi mentah sementara jiran membina kapasiti pemprosesan. Strategi nikel Indonesia — sekat eksport, paksa pemprosesan domestik, tangkap nilai — adalah tepat apa yang Malaysia sepatutnya lakukan dengan nadir bumi. Sebaliknya, Anwar menandatangani pilihan itu.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Indonesia menggunakan nasionalisme sumber untuk membina industri pemprosesan nikel berbilion dolar. Anda menggunakan sumber anda untuk mendapat pengurangan tarif 6 mata peratusan. Satu strategi membina kapasiti perindustrian. Yang satu lagi mengunci anda dalam kebergantungan bahan mentah. Rundingkan semula klausa mineral. Kenakan syarat pemprosesan domestik sebelum eksport. Bina kapasiti penapisan nadir bumi di Malaysia dan bukannya menghantar bahan mentah ke kilang Amerika. Atau teruskan membiarkan Washington “mengisytiharkan kemenangan” menggunakan sumber anda — dan jelaskan kepada generasi akan datang mengapa Malaysia masih menjadi pembekal bahan mentah.

Sumber: MalaysiaNow

item.title: US Declares 'Victory Against China' After Securing Unlimited Minerals Pledge From Anwar
item.description:
Said by: MalaysiaNow report

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-03

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 3 November 2025, MalaysiaNow reported that the United States had “declared victory against China” after securing a pledge from Anwar Ibrahim to not restrict exports of critical minerals and rare earth elements to the US. Under the US-Malaysia ART, Malaysia committed to “refrain from banning, or imposing quotas on, exports to the US of critical minerals or rare earth elements” — a binding trade commitment, not a goodwill gesture. Malaysia also agreed to partner with American firms “to create certainty for businesses to increase production capacity,” essentially guaranteeing US access to Malaysian mineral resources in perpetuity.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Indonesia restricted nickel exports in 2020 and used the leverage to build a domestic processing industry now worth billions of dollars — attracting smelter investments, creating tens of thousands of jobs, and moving up the value chain. Malaysia just agreed to keep sending raw materials to the US. One country used its resources to industrialise. The other agreed to remain a raw material supplier. Rare earth export controls are a key geopolitical tool — perhaps the most powerful one a resource-rich country possesses. China controls 60% of global rare earth mining and 90% of processing, and uses export controls as geopolitical leverage. Malaysia gave up the same strategic tool in exchange for a tariff reduction from 25% to 19%.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: When the US “declares victory against China” using your resources, you are not a partner — you are a pawn. The framing says it all: this was about US strategic interests, and Malaysia was the tool. The long-term cost is incalculable. Critical minerals are finite. Once committed to unrestricted export, Malaysia cannot later impose controls without breaching the ART and triggering US retaliation. The country has locked itself into raw material dependency. Anwar’s defenders argue the deal preserves market access and attracts investment. But market access at the cost of resource sovereignty is a losing trade. Ask any country that exported its resources without adding value — they are all still developing countries.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s resource export history is a cautionary tale. The country was once the world’s largest tin producer — it exported raw tin for decades while others built processing industries. By the time Malaysia tried to add value, the tin market had moved on. The same pattern played out with rubber, palm oil, and petroleum. Each time, Malaysia exported raw commodities while neighbours built processing capacity. Indonesia’s nickel strategy — restrict exports, force domestic processing, capture value — is exactly what Malaysia should have done with rare earths. Instead, Anwar signed away the option.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Indonesia used resource nationalism to build a billion-dollar nickel processing industry. You used your resources to get a 6-percentage-point tariff reduction. One strategy builds industrial capacity. The other locks you into raw material dependency. Renegotiate the minerals clause. Impose a domestic processing requirement before export. Build rare earth refining capacity in Malaysia instead of shipping raw materials to American factories. Or keep letting Washington “declare victory” using your resources — and explain to the next generation why Malaysia is still a raw material supplier.

Sources: MalaysiaNow

item.title: 'Stop Lecturing Us on Sovereign Rights' — US Envoy Told Off Amid Anger Over Trade Deal
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian public and politicians

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Days after the US-Malaysia trade deal was signed, public anger boiled over. The US envoy was publicly told to “stop lecturing us on sovereign rights” amid growing fury over what critics called Anwar’s “surrender” to Washington. MalaysiaNow reported the confrontation. The deal required Malaysia to align with US export controls, sanctions, and trade enforcement — provisions widely seen as infringing on sovereignty. Adding insult to injury, details of the agreement were published on the White House website before being shared with the Malaysian Parliament, fueling perceptions that Malaysia was an afterthought in its own trade negotiations.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When your own people are telling foreign diplomats to stop lecturing them, the government has a legitimacy problem. The deal triggered a rare moment of bipartisan fury — both opposition and ruling coalition members questioned the terms. A deal signed in haste, published by the other side first, and defended after the fact is not diplomacy — it is capitulation with a press release. Malaysia’s neutral foreign policy — maintained across BN, PH 1.0, and PN — was compromised in one signing ceremony.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government’s defence was that the deal preserved market access and prevented worse tariffs. But “it could have been worse” is not a negotiating triumph — it’s an admission of weakness. Publishing the deal on the White House website before Parliament saw it was not a procedural oversight — it was a power display by Washington showing who controls the narrative. Anwar’s team either agreed to this sequencing (which is humiliating) or didn’t anticipate it (which is incompetent). Either way, the Malaysian public learned about their own trade deal from a foreign government’s website.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a long history of trade agreements that prioritise access over sovereignty. The TPPA under Najib triggered massive protests over investor-state dispute settlement (ISDS) clauses. PH 1.0 withdrew from the TPPA, calling it an infringement on sovereignty. PH 2.0 has now signed a bilateral deal with sovereignty provisions that are arguably worse — and did so without the parliamentary scrutiny that even the TPPA received. The cycle is consistent: opposition parties criticise trade deals as sovereignty surrenders, then sign similar or worse deals when they come to power.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You withdrew from the TPPA because it infringed on sovereignty. Then you signed a bilateral deal that commits Malaysia to US export controls, sanctions alignment, and unrestricted mineral exports — and let Washington publish the terms before your own Parliament saw them. If the TPPA was a sovereignty problem, this deal is worse. Table the full agreement in Parliament for debate. Publish a clause-by-clause comparison with the TPPA you rejected. Explain why this is different — if you can. Or admit that sovereignty concerns were only useful when you were in opposition.

Sources: MalaysiaNow

item.title: [BM] 'Berhenti Berkuliah Kami Soal Hak Kedaulatan' — Utusan AS Ditempelak Di Tengah Kemarahan Soal Perjanjian Perdagangan
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian public and politicians

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Beberapa hari selepas perjanjian perdagangan AS-Malaysia ditandatangani, kemarahan awam memuncak.
