Utusan AS diberitahu secara terbuka untuk “berhenti berkuliah kami soal hak kedaulatan” di tengah kemarahan yang semakin membara terhadap apa yang pengkritik panggil “penyerahan” Anwar kepada Washington. MalaysiaNow melaporkan konfrontasi itu. Perjanjian itu memerlukan Malaysia menyelaraskan dengan kawalan eksport, sekatan, dan penguatkuasaan perdagangan AS — peruntukan yang dilihat secara meluas sebagai mencabuli kedaulatan. Menambah penghinaan, butiran perjanjian diterbitkan di laman web Rumah Putih sebelum dikongsi dengan Parlimen Malaysia, mencetuskan persepsi bahawa Malaysia hanya pertimbangan kemudian dalam rundingnya sendiri.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Apabila rakyat sendiri memberitahu diplomat asing berhenti berkuliah mereka, kerajaan mempunyai masalah legitimasi. Perjanjian ini mencetuskan momen bipartisan yang jarang berlaku — ahli pembangkang dan gabungan pemerintah sama-sama mempersoalkan syaratnya. Perjanjian yang ditandatangani dengan tergesa-gesa, diterbitkan oleh pihak lain terlebih dahulu, dan dipertahankan selepas fakta bukan diplomasi — ia penyerahan dengan kenyataan akhbar. Dasar luar berkecuali Malaysia — yang dikekalkan merentasi BN, PH 1.0, dan PN — dikompromi dalam satu majlis menandatangani.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Pembelaan kerajaan adalah perjanjian itu memelihara akses pasaran dan mencegah tarif yang lebih teruk. Tetapi “ia boleh jadi lebih teruk” bukan kemenangan rundingan — ia pengakuan kelemahan. Penerbitan perjanjian di laman web Rumah Putih sebelum Parlimen melihatnya bukan kesilapan prosedur — ia pameran kuasa oleh Washington menunjukkan siapa yang mengawal naratif.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang perjanjian perdagangan yang mengutamakan akses berbanding kedaulatan. TPPA di bawah Najib mencetuskan protes besar-besaran atas klausa ISDS. PH 1.0 menarik diri daripada TPPA, memanggilnya pencabulan kedaulatan. PH 2.0 kini menandatangani perjanjian dua hala dengan peruntukan kedaulatan yang boleh dikatakan lebih buruk — dan berbuat demikian tanpa penelitian parlimen yang diberikan kepada TPPA.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Anda menarik diri daripada TPPA kerana ia mencabuli kedaulatan. Kemudian anda menandatangani perjanjian dua hala yang mengkomitkan Malaysia kepada kawalan eksport AS, penyelarasan sekatan, dan eksport mineral tanpa sekatan — dan membiarkan Washington menerbitkan syarat sebelum Parlimen anda melihatnya. Jika TPPA masalah kedaulatan, perjanjian ini lebih teruk. Bentangkan perjanjian penuh di Parlimen untuk perbahasan. Atau akui bahawa kebimbangan kedaulatan hanya berguna apabila anda dalam pembangkang.

Sumber: MalaysiaNow

item.title: [BM] Chow Akui 'Pembangunan Pesat' Pulau Pinang Tidak Dirasai Sesetengah Komuniti — Selepas 16 Tahun Pemerintahan DAP
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 2 November 2025, KM Pulau Pinang Chow Kon Yeow mengakui bahawa ‘pembangunan pesat’ negeri itu ’tidak dirasai oleh sesetengah komuniti.’ Beliau menggesa pemimpin DAP ‘kekal dekat dengan rakyat dan jaga akar umbi’ dan berkata parti mesti menumpukan penyelesaian masalah harian seperti banjir, kesesakan lalu lintas, bekalan air, dan pembaikan perumahan. Beliau mengakui bahawa menambah baik prestasi di peringkat bawahan akan mengukuhkan keyakinan awam terhadap DAP.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Ini pengakuan luar biasa daripada pemimpin negeri paling agresif pembangunan di Malaysia. DAP telah mentadbir Pulau Pinang sejak 2008 — 16 tahun pemerintahan tanpa putus. Jika selepas 16 tahun, KM mengakui komuniti tidak merasai manfaat pembangunan, masalahnya bukan pada pemesejan — ia pada model pembangunan. KDNK Pulau Pinang telah berkembang, FDI telah mengalir masuk, dan harga hartanah melonjak. Tetapi nelayan, peniaga, golongan pekerja, dan komuniti luar bandar tertinggal.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Pengakuan Chow dibuat di acara dalaman DAP — ia ditujukan kepada pemimpin parti, bukan orang ramai. Mesejnya pada dasarnya: ‘kita ada masalah persepsi, baiki di akar umbi.’ Tetapi masalahnya bukan persepsi — ia substans. Model pembangunan Pulau Pinang mengutamakan FDI, penambakan, dan projek mega berbanding perumahan mampu milik, pengangkutan awam, dan infrastruktur komuniti. Trafik antara yang terburuk di Malaysia. Perumahan tidak mampu dimiliki. Banjir semakin teruk. Pembangunan yang Chow raikan kelihatan kepada pelabur; ia tidak kelihatan kepada penduduk.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Laporan Aliran 2025 mendokumenkan ‘kegagalan sistematik dalam perancangan’ di Pulau Pinang — trafik, banjir, dan ruang hijau yang lenyap. Penduduk menuntut ketelusan sementara negeri meluluskan pembangunan demi pembangunan tanpa infrastruktur yang mencukupi.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Enam belas tahun. Itulah tempoh DAP mentadbir Pulau Pinang. Jika KM sendiri mengakui rakyat tidak merasai pembangunan, model pembangunan itu rosak. Berhenti membina untuk pelabur dan mula membina untuk penduduk. Perumahan mampu milik, pengangkutan awam berfungsi, mitigasi banjir, dan ruang hijau harus menjadi keutamaan — bukan lagi projek penambakan dan zon perindustrian yang rakyat Pulau Pinang biasa tidak boleh akses.

Sumber: FMT | Aliran

item.title: Chow Admits Penang's 'Rapid Development' Not Felt by Some Communities — After 16 Years of DAP Rule
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 2 November 2025, Penang CM Chow Kon Yeow admitted that the state’s ‘rapid development’ was ’not felt by some communities.’ He urged DAP leaders to ‘stay close to the people and take care of the grassroots’ and said the party must focus on solving everyday problems like flooding, traffic congestion, water supply, and housing repairs. He acknowledged that improving ground-level performance would reinforce public confidence in DAP.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This is an extraordinary admission from the leader of Malaysia’s most development-aggressive state. DAP has governed Penang since 2008 — 16 years of unbroken rule. If after 16 years, the CM is admitting that communities aren’t feeling the benefits of development, the problem isn’t the messaging — it’s the development model. Penang’s GDP has grown, FDI has flowed in, and property prices have soared. But the fishermen, the hawkers, the working class, and the rural communities have been left behind.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Chow’s admission came at a DAP internal event — it was directed at party leaders, not the public. The message was essentially: ‘we have an optics problem, fix it at the grassroots.’ But the problem isn’t optics — it’s substance. Penang’s development model prioritises FDI, reclamation, and mega-projects over affordable housing, public transport, and community infrastructure. Traffic is among the worst in Malaysia. Housing is unaffordable. Floods are worsening. The development that Chow celebrates is visible to investors; it’s invisible to residents.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Aliran’s 2025 report documented ‘systematic failures in planning’ in Penang — traffic, floods, and vanishing green spaces. Residents demanded transparency as the state approved development after development without adequate infrastructure. FMT reported DAP Youth blaming PN for traffic woes — a laughable deflection when DAP has controlled planning approvals for 16 years.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Sixteen years. That’s how long DAP has governed Penang. If your own CM admits the people aren’t feeling the development, the development model is broken. Stop building for investors and start building for residents. Affordable housing, functional public transport, flood mitigation, and green spaces should be the priority — not more reclamation projects and industrial zones that ordinary Penangites can’t access.

Sources: FMT | Aliran

item.title: [BM] Kebankrapan Peribadi Naik 5.7% — Trend Kebankrapan Isytihar Sendiri 'Membimbangkan'
item.description:
Said by: Department of Insolvency Malaysia

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Dalam sembilan bulan pertama 2025, Malaysia mencatatkan 4,875 kes kebankrapan peribadi — kenaikan 5.7% daripada tempoh sama 2024. Ketua Pengarah Jabatan Insolvensi memberi amaran tentang “kebimbangan yang semakin meningkat”: kenaikan kebankrapan isytihar sendiri, di mana rakyat Malaysia begitu tertekan dari segi kewangan sehingga secara sukarela mengisytiharkan diri bankrap dan bukannya menunggu pemiutang memaksa proses tersebut. Data menunjukkan tekanan kewangan tidak tertumpu kepada pemilik perniagaan sahaja — ia merebak kepada pekerja bergaji, profesional muda, dan peserta ekonomi gig.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Apabila orang secara sukarela mengisytiharkan kebankrapan, bermakna mereka telah berputus asa dari harapan untuk pulih. Kebankrapan isytihar sendiri adalah tindakan menyerah dari segi kewangan — ia menandakan individu tidak melihat jalan untuk membayar balik hutang melalui pendapatan sahaja. Kenaikan 5.7% dalam jumlah kes, digabungkan dengan trend isytihar sendiri yang meningkat, menunjukkan krisis kos sara hidup Malaysia menolak isi rumah biasa melepasi titik pecah. Inflasi rasmi 1.3%. Tetapi kebankrapan naik 5.7%. Kedua-dua angka itu tidak boleh kedua-duanya memberitahu kebenaran tentang ekonomi.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Kerajaan meraikan pertumbuhan KDNK, rekod FDI, dan inflasi tajuk rendah sementara semakin ramai rakyat Malaysia benar-benar mengisytiharkan diri bankrap. Ketidakpadanan antara penunjuk makro dan realiti mikro adalah ciri penentu ekonomi Madani: angka kelihatan baik di Putrajaya, tetapi rakyat lemas dalam hutang. Ambang kebankrapan dinaikkan dari RM50,000 ke RM100,000 pada 2020 — bermakna angka semasa sebenarnya mengecilkan masalah, kerana orang dengan hutang bawah RM100,000 tidak lagi boleh diisytiharkan bankrap oleh pemiutang.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Kebankrapan peribadi telah meningkat di Malaysia selama bertahun-tahun. Di bawah BN, kerajaan menaikkan ambang kebankrapan untuk menutup trend. Di bawah PH 1.0, moratorium prosiding kebankrapan diperkenalkan semasa COVID. Di bawah PH 2.0, ambang kekal pada RM100,000 dan kes kebankrapan mendaki semula. Setiap kerajaan menguruskan optik dan bukannya menangani punca: gaji benar yang tidak bergerak, kos sara hidup meningkat, akses mudah kepada kredit pengguna, dan celik kewangan yang tidak mencukupi.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Kebankrapan naik 5.7% sementara anda dakwa inflasi 1.3%. Kebankrapan isytihar sendiri meningkat — orang begitu terdesak sehingga menyerah secara sukarela. Pertumbuhan KDNK anda tidak bermakna jika ia tidak diterjemahkan kepada keselamatan kewangan isi rumah. Perluaskan perkhidmatan kaunseling kewangan melalui AKPK. Kawal selia amalan pinjaman pemangsa. Atau terus petik angka makro sementara lebih ramai rakyat Malaysia berjalan masuk ke Jabatan Insolvensi untuk mengisytiharkan diri bankrap.

Sumber: Bernama | Malay Mail | The Star

item.title: [BM] Lowy Institute: 'Kejayaan Antarabangsa Anwar Masam Di Tanah Air' — Tindak Balas Perjanjian Perdagangan AS
item.description:
Said by: Lowy Institute Analysis

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada November 2025, Lowy Institute menerbitkan analisis perjanjian perdagangan AS-Malaysia (ART), menyimpulkan bahawa “kejayaan antarabangsa Anwar masam di tanah air.” Analisis itu mengenal pasti klausa ‘pil racun’ yang berfungsi sebagai “ujian kesetiaan” yang memerlukan Malaysia menyelaraskan dengan kepentingan strategik AS — termasuk kawalan eksport, penguatkuasaan sekatan, dan eksport mineral kritikal tanpa sekatan. Lowy Institute menyatakan tarif 19% ke atas barangan Malaysia dikekalkan, bukan dikurangkan ke sifar seperti yang dibayangkan oleh pembingkaian awam Anwar.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Apabila badan pemikir antarabangsa bebas dan disegani menggambarkan perjanjian perdagangan anda sebagai mengandungi “pil racun” dan “ujian kesetiaan,” naratif kerajaan bahawa ini kemenangan diplomatik runtuh. Analisis Lowy mengesahkan apa yang pengkritik domestik telah katakan: ART mengutamakan kepentingan AS berbanding kedaulatan Malaysia. Tarif 19% — masih antara yang tertinggi dikenakan ke atas mana-mana rakan dagangan AS — dibentangkan secara domestik sebagai kemenangan rundingan, sedangkan realitinya ia mewakili kegagalan mendapatkan akses tarif sifar yang dicapai pesaing seperti Vietnam.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Anwar membingkai perjanjian perdagangan sebagai bukti kedudukan antarabangsa Malaysia yang tinggi. Lowy Institute membingkaikannya sebagai bukti Malaysia ditewaskan dalam taktik. Jurang antara naratif kerajaan dan analisis bebas adalah besar. Klausa “pil racun” — yang memerlukan penyelarasan dengan kawalan eksport dan sekatan AS — secara efektif menundukkan dasar luar Malaysia kepada agenda strategik Washington. Bagi PM yang memperjuangkan berkecuali dan solidariti Selatan Global, menandatangani perjanjian yang digambarkan badan pemikir antarabangsa sebagai “ujian kesetiaan” adalah percanggahan yang tidak boleh diputarbelitkan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Sejarah perjanjian perdagangan Malaysia penuh dengan perjanjian yang kelihatan baik di sidang akhbar dan teruk dalam amalan. TPPA di bawah Najib ditangguhkan selepas kemarahan awam atas klausa kedaulatan. Setiap perjanjian mengikut corak sama: kerajaan raikan penandatanganan, penganalisis kenal pasti masalah, kemarahan awam membina. ART mengikut corak ini tepat — kecuali kali ini, pengkritik termasuk institusi antarabangsa yang disegani.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Lowy Institute memanggil perjanjian perdagangan anda “ujian kesetiaan.” Penganalisis bebas mengenal pasti klausa “pil racun.” Tarif kekal pada 19%, bukan sifar. Naratif anda tidak sepadan dengan analisis. Terbitkan teks penuh ART dengan penjelasan klausa demi klausa tentang apa yang Malaysia komitkan. Atau terus meraikan perjanjian yang penganalisis antarabangsa gambarkan sebagai kekalahan strategik.

Sumber: Lowy Institute | MalaysiaNow

item.title: [BM] Pengangguran Belia Terperangkap Pada 12.28% — Satu Daripada Lapan Anak Muda Malaysia Tak Dapat Kerja
item.description:
Said by: DOSM / ILO / World Bank Data

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Sehingga penghujung 2025, kadar pengangguran belia Malaysia (umur 15-24) kekal terperangkap pada 12.28% — kira-kira 297,500 anak muda Malaysia menganggur. Ini tiga hingga empat kali ganda lebih tinggi daripada kadar pengangguran nasional keseluruhan 3.2%. Data DOSM, ILO, dan Bank Dunia semuanya mengesahkan sifat struktur masalah ini: Malaysia mengeluarkan graduan yang ekonomi tidak perlukan, manakala industri yang mengambil pekerja tidak dapat mencari tenaga kerja dengan kemahiran yang betul. “Ekonomi Madani” yang diraikan Anwar nampaknya tidak termasuk anak muda.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Satu daripada lapan anak muda Malaysia tidak dapat mencari kerja. Ini bukan kemelesetan kitaran — ia adalah kegagalan struktur saluran pendidikan-ke-pekerjaan. Universiti menghasilkan graduan dalam bidang yang permintaannya terhad manakala industri menghadapi kekurangan akut dalam bidang teknikal, digital, dan kemahiran tangan. Hasilnya: satu generasi anak muda Malaysia berpendidikan yang terlalu layak untuk pekerjaan yang ada dan kurang layak untuk pekerjaan yang wujud. Pengangguran belia pada 12.28% adalah bom masa sosial — ia memacu penghijrahan otak, melambatkan pembentukan isi rumah, meningkatkan beban kesihatan mental, dan menghakis kepercayaan terhadap institusi demokrasi.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Kerajaan membanggakan pertumbuhan KDNK melebihi 5% dan FDI rekod sebagai bukti ekonomi berkembang maju. Tetapi ekonomi yang berkembang sambil meninggalkan satu daripada lapan anak muda menganggur bukan berkembang maju — ia mengekstrak nilai tanpa mengagihkan peluang. PH menjanjikan reformasi TVET, program kebolehpasaran graduan, dan pendidikan sejajar industri. Tiga tahun kemudian, angka-angka tidak berubah. Program wujud di atas kertas. Graduan kekal menganggur. Jurang antara retorik kerajaan (“Malaysia sedang berkembang pesat”) dan realiti belia (“Saya tak dapat kerja”) adalah percanggahan ekonomi yang mentakrifkan era Anwar.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Pengangguran belia secara struktur telah meningkat di Malaysia selama lebih sedekad. Di bawah BN, kadar itu berputar sekitar 10-11%. Di bawah PH 1.0, ia turun sebentar sebelum COVID. Di bawah Muhyiddin dan Ismail Sabri, ia melonjak ke 14%+ semasa pandemik. Di bawah PH 2.0, ia menetap pada 12.28% — lebih baik daripada puncak pandemik tetapi lebih teruk daripada paras sebelum pandemik. Tiada kerajaan menyelesaikannya kerana tiada kerajaan sanggup mereformasi sistem pendidikan yang menghasilkan ketidakpadanan itu. Universiti adalah institusi politik di Malaysia — menyusun semulanya bermakna mencabar kepentingan yang berakar.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Satu daripada lapan anak muda Malaysia tak dapat kerja. KDNK anda berkembang melebihi 5%. FDI anda pada rekod tertinggi. Dan belia anda menganggur. Ketidakselarasan ini bersifat struktur — dan ia tanggungjawab anda sekarang. Reformasi pembiayaan TVET supaya sepadan dengan permintaan industri. Terbitkan data kebolehpasaran graduan mengikut universiti dan program supaya pelajar dapat membuat pilihan bermaklumat. Bekerjasama dengan industri dalam program perantisan dengan laluan pekerjaan terjamin. Atau teruskan meraikan pertumbuhan KDNK sementara satu generasi anak muda Malaysia meninggalkan negara untuk mencari kerja di tempat lain.

Sumber: Tinjauan Tenaga Buruh DOSM | Pemantau Ekonomi Malaysia Bank Dunia | ILO

item.title: [BM] Rakyat Kata Inflasi 1.3% Adalah Pembohongan — 'Tak Tahu Dari Mana Madanon Dapat Nombor Ni'
item.description:
Said by: Public Sentiment / Social Media

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Kadar inflasi rasmi Malaysia untuk Oktober 2025 dilaporkan pada 1.3% oleh Jabatan Perangkaan (DOSM). Rakyat Malaysia bertindak balas dengan ejekan terbuka di media sosial, dengan komen seperti “tak tahu dari mana Madanon dapat nombor ni.” Jurang antara inflasi tajuk dan pengalaman sebenar amat ketara: inflasi makanan luar rumah berjalan pada 4.3-4.7%, jauh melebihi kadar tajuk. Kos sewa, penjagaan kesihatan, dan perbelanjaan pendidikan — kategori yang secara tidak seimbang menjejaskan isi rumah berpendapatan sederhana dan rendah — semuanya meningkat lebih cepat daripada angka komposit rasmi.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Apabila rakyat secara terbuka mengejek statistik rasmi kerajaan mereka, ia menandakan keruntuhan kredibiliti institusi. Angka tajuk 1.3% secara teknikal tepat — ia mengukur bakul IHP seperti yang ditakrifkan DOSM. Tetapi bakul IHP tidak mencerminkan cara rakyat Malaysia biasa sebenarnya membelanjakan wang mereka. Hidangan penjaja yang berharga RM5 dua tahun lalu kini berharga RM7-8. Teh tarik mamak telah naik dari RM1.80 kepada RM2.50. Ini harga yang dirasai rakyat setiap hari — dan ia langsung tidak menyerupai 1.3%.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Kerajaan menggunakan angka 1.3% untuk mendakwa kestabilan ekonomi sambil mengabaikan komposisi angka itu. IHP tajuk ditarik turun oleh harga terkawal (subsidi bahan api, kawalan harga) yang menyembunyikan inflasi asas. Apabila anda mensubsidi RON95 dan mengawal harga tepung dan gula, inflasi tajuk kelihatan rendah — tetapi sebaik sahaja subsidi ditarik, inflasi yang ditahan menghentam sekaligus. Kerajaan bukan mengawal inflasi — ia menyembunyikannya di sebalik subsidi yang tidak mampu dikekalkan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Setiap kerajaan Malaysia telah menggunakan angka inflasi tajuk untuk melukis gambaran lebih cerah daripada realiti. PH menjanjikan ketelusan dan kejujuran dalam tadbir urus — tetapi pengendalian data inflasinya tidak dapat dibezakan daripada pendahulunya.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Rakyat ketawa melihat nombor inflasi anda. Itu sepatutnya membimbangkan anda lebih daripada nombor itu sendiri. Terbitkan “indeks kos sara hidup” yang menjejak bakul barangan dan perkhidmatan sebenar yang digunakan isi rumah B40 dan M40 — bukan komposit IHP yang merangkumi barangan import mewah dan harga terkawal. Tunjukkan rakyat Malaysia anda faham apa yang mereka sebenarnya bayar. Atau terus petik 1.3% sementara hidangan mamak berharga RM8 — dan tertanya mengapa tiada siapa percaya apa sahaja yang anda kata tentang ekonomi.

Sumber: DOSM | FMT | Malaysiakini

item.title: Lowy Institute: 'Anwar's International Success Sours at Home' — US Trade Deal Backlash
item.description:
Said by: Lowy Institute Analysis

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In November 2025, the Lowy Institute published an analysis of the US-Malaysia trade deal (ART), concluding that “Anwar’s international success sours at home.” The analysis identified ‘poison pill’ clauses that function as a “loyalty test” requiring Malaysia to align with US strategic interests — including export controls, sanctions enforcement, and unrestricted critical mineral exports. The Lowy Institute noted that the 19% tariff on Malaysian goods was maintained, not reduced to zero as Anwar’s public framing implied. The deal was characterised as a US strategic win dressed up as a bilateral partnership.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When an independent, respected international think tank describes your trade deal as containing “poison pills” and “loyalty tests,” the government’s spin that this was a diplomatic triumph collapses. The Lowy analysis confirmed what domestic critics had been saying: the ART prioritises US interests over Malaysian sovereignty. The 19% tariff — still among the highest imposed on any US trading partner — was presented domestically as a negotiating win, when in reality it represents a failure to secure the zero-tariff access that competitors like Vietnam achieved.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar framed the trade deal as proof of Malaysia’s elevated international standing. The Lowy Institute framed it as proof that Malaysia got outmanoeuvred. The gap between the government’s narrative and independent analysis is enormous. The “poison pill” clauses — requiring alignment with US export controls and sanctions — effectively subordinate Malaysia’s foreign policy to Washington’s strategic agenda. For a PM who champions non-alignment and Global South solidarity, signing a deal that an international think tank describes as a “loyalty test” is a contradiction that cannot be spun away.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s trade deal history is littered with agreements that looked good in press conferences and bad in practice. The TPPA under Najib was shelved after public fury over sovereignty clauses. The RCEP was signed but implementation has been slow. Each agreement follows the same pattern: government celebrates the signing, analysts identify the problems, public anger builds, and the government pivots to damage control. The ART follows this pattern exactly — except this time, the critics include a respected international institution, not just domestic opposition.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: The Lowy Institute called your trade deal a “loyalty test.” Independent analysts identified “poison pill” clauses. The tariff stayed at 19%, not zero. Your own narrative doesn’t match the analysis. Publish the full text of the ART with a clause-by-clause explanation of what Malaysia committed to. Commission an independent economic impact assessment. Explain why Malaysia accepted “loyalty test” provisions that other countries rejected. Or keep celebrating a deal that international analysts describe as a strategic defeat.

Sources: Lowy Institute | MalaysiaNow

item.title: Personal Bankruptcies Rise 5.7% — Self-Declared Bankruptcy Trend 'Growing Concern'
item.description:
Said by: Department of Insolvency Malaysia

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In the first nine months of 2025, Malaysia recorded 4,875 personal bankruptcy cases — a 5.7% increase from the same period in 2024. The Insolvency Department Director-General flagged a “growing concern”: the rise in self-declared bankruptcies, where Malaysians are so financially distressed they voluntarily declare themselves bankrupt rather than waiting for creditors to force the process. The data indicates that financial distress is not concentrated among business owners — it is spreading to salaried workers, young professionals, and gig economy participants.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When people voluntarily declare bankruptcy, it means they have given up hope of recovery. Self-declared bankruptcy is an act of financial surrender — it signals that the individual sees no path to repaying debts through income alone. A 5.7% rise in total cases, combined with a rising self-declared trend, suggests that Malaysia’s cost-of-living crisis is pushing ordinary households past breaking point. Official inflation is 1.3%. But bankruptcies are rising 5.7%. Those two numbers cannot both be telling the truth about the economy.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government celebrates GDP growth, FDI records, and low headline inflation while an increasing number of Malaysians are literally declaring themselves bankrupt. The disconnect between macro indicators and micro reality is the defining feature of the Madani economy: the numbers look good in Putrajaya, but the rakyat is drowning in debt. The bankruptcy threshold was raised from RM50,000 to RM100,000 in 2020 — meaning current figures actually undercount the problem, since people with debts under RM100,000 can no longer be declared bankrupt by creditors. The real financial distress is worse than the data shows.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Personal bankruptcy has been rising in Malaysia for years. Under BN, the government raised the bankruptcy threshold to mask the trend. Under PH 1.0, a moratorium on bankruptcy proceedings was introduced during COVID. Under PH 2.0, the threshold remains at RM100,000 and bankruptcy cases are climbing again. Each government has managed the optics rather than addressing the root causes: stagnant real wages, rising living costs, easy access to consumer credit, and inadequate financial literacy. The cycle continues because addressing it would require admitting that the economy is not working for ordinary people.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Bankruptcies are up 5.7% while you claim inflation is 1.3%. Self-declared bankruptcies are rising — people are so desperate they’re surrendering voluntarily. Your GDP growth is meaningless if it doesn’t translate into household financial security. Expand financial counselling services through AKPK. Regulate predatory lending practices, especially in the buy-now-pay-later sector. Publish household debt-to-income data by income quintile so the rakyat can see the real picture. Or keep quoting macro numbers while more Malaysians walk into the Insolvency Department to declare themselves bankrupt.

Sources: Bernama | Malay Mail | The Star

item.title: Rakyat Says 1.3% Inflation is a Lie — 'Don't Know Where Madanon Gets These Numbers'
item.description:
Said by: Public Sentiment / Social Media

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s official inflation rate for October 2025 was reported at 1.3% by the Department of Statistics (DOSM). Malaysians responded with open mockery on social media, with comments like “don’t know where Madanon gets these numbers.” The disconnect between headline inflation and lived experience is stark: food-away-from-home inflation ran at 4.3-4.7%, far exceeding the headline rate. Rental costs, healthcare, and education expenses — categories that disproportionately affect middle and lower-income households — all rose faster than the official composite figure suggests.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When citizens openly mock their government’s official statistics, it signals a collapse in institutional credibility. The 1.3% headline figure is technically accurate — it measures the CPI basket as defined by DOSM. But the CPI basket doesn’t reflect how ordinary Malaysians actually spend their money. A hawker meal that cost RM5 two years ago now costs RM7-8. Mamak teh tarik has gone from RM1.80 to RM2.50. These are the prices people feel daily — and they bear no resemblance to 1.3%. The gap between official statistics and lived reality erodes public trust in all government data.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government uses the 1.3% figure to claim economic stability while ignoring the composition of that figure. Headline CPI is dragged down by administered prices (fuel subsidies, price controls) that mask underlying inflation. When you subsidise RON95 and control flour and sugar prices, headline inflation looks low — but the moment subsidies are withdrawn, the suppressed inflation hits all at once. The government is not controlling inflation — it is hiding it behind subsidies it can’t afford to maintain. Meanwhile, the items that aren’t price-controlled — food, services, healthcare, education — are rising at 3-5x the headline rate.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every Malaysian government has used headline inflation figures to paint a rosier picture than reality. Under Najib, the GST introduction in 2015 caused a spike that was partially masked by rebates and handouts. Under Muhyiddin, pandemic-era price controls suppressed official inflation while actual costs soared. The pattern is consistent: governments manipulate the visible number while ordinary households bear invisible cost increases. PH promised transparency and honesty in governance — but its handling of inflation data is indistinguishable from its predecessors.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: The rakyat is laughing at your inflation numbers. That should worry you more than the numbers themselves. Publish a “cost of living index” that tracks the actual basket of goods and services used by B40 and M40 households — not the CPI composite that includes imported luxury goods and administered prices. Show Malaysians you understand what they’re actually paying. Or keep quoting 1.3% while mamak meals cost RM8 — and wonder why nobody believes anything you say about the economy.

Sources: DOSM | FMT | Malaysiakini

item.title: Youth Unemployment Stuck at 12.28% — One in Eight Young Malaysians Can't Find Work
item.description:
Said by: DOSM / ILO / World Bank Data

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As of late 2025, Malaysia’s youth unemployment rate (ages 15-24) remains stuck at 12.28% — roughly 297,500 unemployed young Malaysians. This is three to four times higher than the overall national unemployment rate of 3.2%. DOSM, the ILO, and World Bank data all confirm the structural nature of the problem: Malaysia produces graduates the economy doesn’t need, while industries that are hiring can’t find workers with the right skills. The “Madani economy” that Anwar celebrates apparently doesn’t include young people.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: One in eight young Malaysians can’t find work. This isn’t a cyclical downturn — it’s a structural failure of the education-to-employment pipeline. Universities churn out graduates in fields with limited demand while industries face acute shortages in technical, digital, and skilled trades. The result: a generation of educated young Malaysians who are overqualified for available jobs and underqualified for the jobs that exist. Youth unemployment at 12.28% is a social time bomb — it drives brain drain, delays household formation, increases mental health burdens, and erodes faith in democratic institutions.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government touts GDP growth above 5% and record FDI as proof the economy is thriving. But an economy that grows while leaving one in eight young people unemployed is not thriving — it is extracting value without distributing opportunity. PH promised TVET reform, graduate employability programmes, and industry-aligned education. Three years later, the numbers haven’t moved. The programmes exist on paper. The graduates remain unemployed. The gap between government rhetoric (“Malaysia is booming”) and youth reality (“I can’t find a job”) is the defining economic contradiction of the Anwar era.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Youth unemployment has been structurally elevated in Malaysia for over a decade. Under BN, the rate hovered around 10-11%. Under PH 1.0, it briefly dipped before COVID. Under Muhyiddin and Ismail Sabri, it spiked to 14%+ during the pandemic. Under PH 2.0, it has settled at 12.28% — better than the pandemic peak but worse than pre-pandemic levels. No government has solved it because no government has been willing to reform the education system that produces the mismatch. Universities are political institutions in Malaysia — restructuring them means challenging entrenched interests.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: One in eight young Malaysians can’t find work. Your GDP is growing above 5%. Your FDI is at record highs. And your youth are unemployed. The disconnect is structural — and it’s your responsibility now. Reform TVET funding so it matches industry demand. Publish graduate employability data by university and programme so students can make informed choices. Partner with industries on apprenticeship programmes with guaranteed employment pathways. Or keep celebrating GDP growth while a generation of young Malaysians leaves the country to find work elsewhere.

Sources: DOSM Labour Force Survey | World Bank Malaysia Economic Monitor | ILO

item.title: [BM] Zafrul: 'Kita Tiada Pilihan Selain Berunding' — Pembelaan Paling Jujur Dan Mendakwa Soal Perjanjian Perdagangan AS
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-29

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Berucap di Parlimen pada 29 Oktober 2025, Tengku Zafrul menyampaikan apa yang dimaksudkan sebagai pembelaan ART tetapi yang didengari pengkritik sebagai pengakuan kelemahan: ‘Kita tiada pilihan selain berunding. Suka atau tidak, kita perlu mempertahankan akses kita ke pasaran AS, walaupun di bawah tekanan.’ Beliau memanggil perjanjian itu ‘senario terbaik’ dan menuduh pengkritik ‘kejahilan mengenai rundingan sengit di sebalik tabir.’

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: ‘Kita tiada pilihan’ adalah kenyataan paling mendedahkan yang pernah dibuat mana-mana menteri PH. Ia mengesahkan apa yang disyaki pengkritik: Malaysia berunding dari kedudukan lemah, menerima syarat yang tidak disukai, dan menandatangani kerana alternatif — tarif AS — lebih teruk. Itu mungkin benar. Tetapi ‘kita tiada pilihan’ bukan pembelaan; ia epitaf untuk leveraj perundingan Malaysia. Apabila menteri anda memberitahu Parlimen negara tiada pilihan, anda memberitahu setiap rakan dagangan masa depan bahawa Malaysia akan menerima apa sahaja tawaran yang diberikan.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Negara lain mempunyai pilihan. Vietnam, Indonesia, dan Thailand semua menghadapi tarif AS tetapi belum menandatangani perjanjian dengan peruntukan yang sama. Indonesia berunding syarat sendiri. Vietnam memanfaatkan asas pembuatannya. Malaysia, di bawah Zafrul, menandatangani dahulu dan mempertahankan kemudian. Pembingkaian ’tiada pilihan’ amat merosakkan kerana ia menghapuskan agensi — seolah-olah Malaysia penerima pasif kuasa geopolitik dan bukannya negara berdaulat dengan pilihan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Perunding perdagangan Malaysia secara sejarah antara yang paling mahir di ASEAN. Mahathir — dengan segala kelemahannya — enggan ditolak dalam hal perdagangan. Malah rundingan TPPA Najib mengekstrak pengecualian yang signifikan. Pengakuan Zafrul bahawa Malaysia ’tiada pilihan’ mewakili titik rendah bersejarah dalam keyakinan perundingan perdagangan.

🎯 SERUAN PH: ‘Kita tiada pilihan’ bukan slogan reformasi. Ia kenyataan menyerah. Jika anda benar-benar tiada pilihan, itu kegagalan persediaan diplomatik, bukan pembelaan hasilnya. Rakyat berhak tahu: apakah pilihan yang anda pertimbangkan dan tolak? Apakah alternatif yang dinilai? Atau anda hanya menandatangani apa yang Washington letakkan di hadapan anda?

Sumber: Malay Mail | CNA

item.title: Zafrul: 'We Had No Choice But to Negotiate' — The Most Honest and Damning Defence of the US Trade Deal
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-29

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Speaking in Parliament on 29 October 2025, Tengku Zafrul delivered what he intended as a defence of the ART but what critics heard as an admission of weakness: ‘We had no choice but to negotiate. Whether we like it or not, we need to defend our access to the US markets, even when under pressure.’ He called the deal the ‘best case scenario’ and accused detractors of ‘ignorance regarding the intense, behind-the-scenes negotiations.’

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: ‘We had no choice’ is the most revealing statement any PH minister has made. It confirms what critics suspected: Malaysia negotiated from a position of weakness, accepted terms it didn’t like, and signed because the alternative — US tariffs — was worse. That may be true. But ‘we had no choice’ is not a defence; it’s an epitaph for Malaysia’s negotiating leverage. When your minister tells Parliament the country had no options, you are telling every future trading partner that Malaysia will take whatever deal is offered.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Other countries had choices. Vietnam, Indonesia, and Thailand all face US tariffs but haven’t signed agreements with the same provisions. Indonesia negotiated its own terms. Vietnam leveraged its manufacturing base. Malaysia, under Zafrul, signed first and defended later. The ’no choice’ framing is particularly damaging because it removes agency — as if Malaysia is a passive recipient of geopolitical forces rather than a sovereign nation with options.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s trade negotiators have historically been among ASEAN’s most skilled. Mahathir — for all his flaws — refused to be pushed around on trade. Even Najib’s TPPA negotiations extracted significant carve-outs. Zafrul’s admission that Malaysia ‘had no choice’ represents a historic low in trade negotiation confidence.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: ‘We had no choice’ is not a reform slogan. It’s a surrender statement. If you truly had no choice, that’s a failure of diplomatic preparation, not a defence of the outcome. The rakyat deserves to know: what choices did you consider and reject? What alternatives were evaluated? Or did you just sign what Washington put in front of you?

Sources: Malay Mail | CNA

item.title: [BM] Air Selangor: 27% Hilang Akibat Kebocoran — Amirudin Promosi Meter Pintar Sementara Paip Pecah
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-27

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 27 Oktober 2025, Amirudin memberitahu Parlimen bahawa projek perintis meter air pintar di Selangor telah terbukti berkesan — memetik Bukit Jelutong di mana air tidak berhasil (NRW) dicatatkan pada 27%. Beliau menggesa kerajaan persekutuan mengamalkan meter pintar di seluruh negara. Secara berasingan, kerajaan Selangor mengumumkan akan menanggung kenaikan kos air untuk lebih satu juta pengguna dan mempercepatkan penggantian paip pada 300km setahun, mensasarkan margin rizab air 20% menjelang 2030.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Selangor telah ditadbir PH sejak 2008. Selepas 17 tahun, 27% air terawat masih hilang akibat kebocoran dan ketidakcekapan dalam kawasan perintis. Di seluruh negeri, kadar NRW lebih tinggi. Air Selangor melayan 9.62 juta pengguna di Selangor, KL, dan Putrajaya — utiliti air terbesar di negara ini. Kegagalan berdekad-dekad membaiki paip bocor sementara gangguan air kerap menjejaskan berjuta-juta pengguna adalah salah satu kegagalan tadbir urus PH yang paling ketara.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Amirudin berkuliah kepada kerajaan persekutuan tentang meter pintar sementara negeri sendiri tidak mampu menghalang paip daripada pecah. Selangor telah mengalami krisis air berulang — penutupan loji rawatan akibat pencemaran, gangguan bekalan, dan catuan air — di bawah pengawasan beliau. Pelan induk air Amirudin hanya diumumkan pada 2025, selepas 17 tahun pemerintahan PH.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Gangguan air 2023 dan 2024 di Lembah Klang menjejaskan berjuta-juta dan disebabkan pencemaran sungai menutup loji rawatan — masalah yang pelaburan sewajarnya dalam sumber alternatif akan mencegah. Pelan induk air Amirudin hanya diumumkan pada 2025, selepas 17 tahun pemerintahan PH.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Anda telah mentadbir infrastruktur air Selangor selama 17 tahun. NRW masih pada 27%. Paip masih pecah. Loji rawatan masih ditutup akibat pencemaran. Dan penyelesaian anda adalah menyuruh kerajaan persekutuan beli meter pintar? Baiki paip sendiri dulu. Rakyat tidak perlukan pelan induk yang diumumkan pada tahun ke-17 — mereka memerlukannya pada tahun pertama.

Sumber: NST | Yahoo News

item.title: Selangor Water: 27% Lost to Leaks — Amirudin Touts Smart Meters While Pipes Burst
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-27

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 27 October 2025, Amirudin told Parliament that pilot smart water meter projects in Selangor had proven effective — citing Bukit Jelutong where non-revenue water (NRW) was recorded at 27%. He urged the federal government to adopt smart meters nationwide. Separately, the Selangor government announced it would absorb increased water costs for over one million users and accelerate pipe replacement at 300km per year, targeting a 20% water reserve margin by 2030.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Selangor has been PH-governed since 2008. After 17 years, 27% of treated water is still lost to leaks and inefficiencies in a pilot area. Across the state, NRW rates are higher. Air Selangor serves 9.62 million consumers in Selangor, KL, and Putrajaya — the largest water utility in the country. The decades-long failure to fix leaking pipes while water disruptions regularly affect millions of consumers is one of PH’s most tangible governance failures.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Amirudin is lecturing the federal government about smart meters while his own state can’t keep pipes from bursting. Selangor has had repeated water crises — pollution shutdowns, supply disruptions, and rationing — under his watch. The RM320 million Sungai Selangor Alternative Raw Water scheme (SJAM) is being framed as a solution, but the fundamental problem is infrastructure decay that PH has failed to address in 17 years of state government.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The 2023 and 2024 water disruptions in the Klang Valley affected millions and were caused by river pollution shutting down treatment plants — a problem that proper investment in alternative sources would have prevented. Amirudin’s water master plan was only announced in 2025, after 17 years of PH governance.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You’ve governed Selangor’s water infrastructure for 17 years. NRW is still at 27%. Pipes still burst. Treatment plants still shut down from pollution. And your solution is to tell the federal government to buy smart meters? Fix your own pipes first. The rakyat doesn’t need a masterplan announced in year 17 — they needed it in year 1.

Sources: NST | Yahoo News

item.title: [BM] Butiran Perjanjian Perdagangan AS Diterbitkan Di Laman Web Rumah Putih Sebelum Parlimen Malaysia Diberitahu
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / White House

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Apabila Perjanjian Perdagangan Timbal Balik AS-Malaysia ditandatangani pada 26 Oktober 2025, USTR menerbitkan helaian fakta terperinci di laman web Rumah Putih yang menggariskan komitmen Malaysia — termasuk akses pasaran keutamaan untuk barangan AS, komitmen mengenai mineral nadir bumi, dan jadual tarif. Ahli Parlimen Malaysia dan orang awam mengetahui butiran perjanjian perdagangan negara mereka sendiri daripada laman web kerajaan Amerika. Terma perjanjian itu diterbitkan di Washington sebelum dibentangkan di Putrajaya.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Komitmen perdagangan sesebuah negara berdaulat sepatutnya disampaikan kepada parlimen dan rakyatnya sendiri sebelum diumumkan oleh pihak lain. Hakikat bahawa rakyat Malaysia terpaksa membaca laman web Rumah Putih untuk memahami apa yang kerajaan mereka telah persetujui adalah satu penghinaan terhadap kedaulatan parlimen. Ia juga bermakna AS mengawal naratif — terma itu dibingkaikan sebagai kemenangan Amerika sebelum Malaysia sempat memutar cerita sendiri.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: PH menjanjikan tadbir urus telus dan keutamaan parlimen. Kemudian Zafrul menandatangani perjanjian perdagangan dan membiarkan Washington menerbitkan termanya dahulu. Fahmi Fadzil ialah Menteri Komunikasi — di mana pendedahan proaktif Malaysia? Urutan itu memberitahu anda segala-galanya tentang dinamik kuasa: AS menentukan terma, menentukan garis masa, dan menentukan naratif. Peranan Malaysia hanyalah menandatangani dan mempertahankan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Rundingan TPPA Malaysia di bawah Najib juga dikritik kerana kekurangan ketelusan — tetapi sekurang-kurangnya kerajaan cuba mengawal naratifnya sendiri. Di bawah PH, naratif itu secara literal diserahkan kepada Rumah Putih. Eurasia Review menyatakan perjanjian itu ‘disusun tergesa-gesa’ dan ’tidak bertujuan untuk menarik banyak perhatian.’ Ketergesa-gesaan itulah perkara utama — perjanjian yang tergesa-gesa memihak kepada pihak yang lebih kuat.

🎯 TEGURAN KEPADA PH: Anda membiarkan Rumah Putih memberitahu rakyat Malaysia apa yang kerajaan mereka sendiri telah persetujui. Itu bukan perkongsian setara. Itu adalah hubungan di mana satu pihak menulis kenyataan akhbar dan pihak lain hanya mengangguk. Jika anda tidak mampu pun mengawal komunikasi mengenai perjanjian perdagangan anda sendiri, bagaimana anda harap rakyat Malaysia percaya anda mengawal rundingannya?

Sumber: Helaian Fakta USTR | Eurasia Review

item.title: [BM] Malaysia Komited Untuk Tidak Pernah Mengharamkan Eksport Nadir Bumi Ke AS — Di Bawah Perjanjian Perdagangan Zafrul
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / USTR

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Di bawah Perjanjian Perdagangan Timbal Balik AS-Malaysia yang ditandatangani oleh Tengku Zafrul pada 26 Oktober 2025, Malaysia komited untuk tidak mengenakan ‘sekatan kuantitatif’ (larangan atau kuota) ke atas eksport mineral kritikal atau nadir bumi ke Amerika Syarikat. Helaian fakta USTR secara jelas menonjolkan perkara ini sebagai kemenangan AS. Sementara itu, Zafrul menolak dakwaan Mahathir bahawa Malaysia bersetuju membekalkan nadir bumi mentah pada pulangan rendah, dengan mengatakan dasar sedia ada mewajibkan pemprosesan domestik sebelum eksport.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Mineral nadir bumi adalah sumber paling strategik abad ke-21 — penting untuk semikonduktor, sistem pertahanan, kenderaan elektrik, dan tenaga boleh baharu. China mengawal kira-kira 60% perlombongan nadir bumi global dan 90% pemprosesan. AS sangat memerlukan bekalan alternatif, dan Malaysia — dengan loji Lynas di Kuantan — adalah nod utama. Dengan komited secara bertulis untuk tidak pernah menyekat eksport ke AS, Malaysia menyerahkan salah satu titik leveraj geopolitik paling berkuasa untuk tidak lebih daripada mengekalkan tarif status quo.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Indonesia, Australia, dan negara kaya mineral lain menggunakan kawalan eksport sebagai leveraj rundingan untuk menarik pemprosesan hiliran dan pemindahan teknologi. Malaysia menyerahkan leveraj itu begitu sahaja. Komitmen ini bermakna walaupun kerajaan masa depan mahu menggunakan nadir bumi sebagai alat tawar-menawar — seperti yang dilakukan China dengan berkesan — Malaysia terikat secara kontrak untuk tidak berbuat demikian. Zafrul menukarkan aset strategik untuk kadar tarif yang tidak pun bertambah baik.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Sekatan eksport nadir bumi China telah menjadi senjata perdagangan paling berkesan selama berdekad-dekad. Larangan eksport nikel Indonesia memaksa syarikat membina kilang lebur secara domestik, mewujudkan pekerjaan dan nilai tambah. Malaysia mempunyai peluang yang sama — dan menandatanganinya begitu sahaja dalam perjanjian perdagangan.

🎯 TEGURAN KEPADA PH: Anda baru memberi Amerika Syarikat jaminan bertulis bahawa Malaysia tidak akan pernah menggunakan sumber nadir buminya sebagai leveraj. Bukan untuk pemindahan teknologi. Bukan untuk pekerjaan hiliran. Bukan untuk terma perdagangan yang lebih baik. Untuk tiada apa-apa. Menteri anda kata dasar pemprosesan-dahulu Malaysia masih utuh — tetapi perjanjian itu kata anda tidak boleh menyekat eksport. Apabila Washington datang mengetuk pintu untuk mineral mentah, ‘dasar’ anda akan tunduk kepada obligasi perjanjian.

Sumber: Helaian Fakta USTR | FMT

item.title: [BM] Perjanjian Perdagangan AS-Malaysia Dipanggil 'Perjanjian Terburuk Sejak Merdeka' Dan 'Tindakan Menyerah Kalah'
item.description:
Said by: Tommy Thomas, Former Attorney General (and critics)

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

Perjanjian Yang Menjual Kedaulatan Malaysia

Pada 26 Oktober 2025, Anwar Ibrahim dan Donald Trump menandatangani Perjanjian Perdagangan Timbal Balik AS-Malaysia (ART) di Sidang Kemuncak ASEAN. Bekas Peguam Negara Tommy Thomas memanggilnya perjanjian terburuk yang Malaysia masuki sejak merdeka pada 1957. Beliau tidak membesar-besarkan.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Perjanjian ini mengekalkan tarif AS 19% ke atas barangan Malaysia — turun dari 25%, tetapi masih menghukum mengikut sebarang standard. Tarif efektif Malaysia ke atas barangan AS sebelum perjanjian ini adalah kira-kira 6%. AS mendapat bahagian lebih baik tiga kali ganda.

Fakta: Malaysia komited untuk: menguatkuasakan peraturan pengelakan tarif AS terhadap negara ketiga, menyelaraskan dengan kawalan eksport dan rejim sekatan AS, tidak mengharamkan atau menyekat eksport mineral kritikal atau nadir bumi ke AS, menahan diri daripada mengenakan cukai perkhidmatan digital ke atas syarikat teknologi AS, dan membeli barangan dan perkhidmatan Amerika tambahan.

Fakta: Klausa penamatan membenarkan AS mengenakan semula tarif asal 25% jika Malaysia memasuki perjanjian perdagangan yang “menjejaskan kepentingan penting AS.” Ini secara efektif memberi Washington kuasa veto ke atas dasar perdagangan bebas Malaysia — termasuk dengan China, rakan dagangan terbesar Malaysia.

Fakta: Teks penuh perjanjian itu pertama kali diterbitkan di laman web Rumah Putih sebelum dikongsi dengan Parlimen Malaysia. Penggubal undang-undang Malaysia mengetahui terma perjanjian perdagangan mereka sendiri daripada kenyataan akhbar kerajaan asing.

Fakta: Bekas PM Mahathir berkata peruntukannya berjumlah “menyerahkan” kemerdekaan negara. Walaupun bagi seorang yang menyelia bahagian perjanjian meragukan sendiri, ini terlalu jauh.

Fakta: Analisis East Asia Forum, bertajuk “The Art of the Deal Lost in US-Malaysia Pact,” berhujah Malaysia menyerahkan kawalan eksport mineral kritikal, hak percukaian perkhidmatan digital, dan dasar perdagangan bebas sebagai pertukaran untuk pemotongan tarif yang masih meletakkan Malaysia membayar tiga kali ganda daripada AS.

Fakta: The Guardian melaporkan Anwar “mempertahankan” perjanjian itu dengan berhujah ia adalah “yang terbaik Malaysia boleh dapat dalam keadaan ini” — hujah yang mengakui perjanjian itu buruk sambil mendakwa ia boleh jadi lebih teruk.

Fakta: SCMP melaporkan Anwar “menghentam” pengkritik kerana menuduhnya menyerahkan kedaulatan — tetapi tidak pernah menangani isi hujah mereka, hanya nada mereka.

Kenapa Ia Penting

  • Ini bukan perjanjian timbal balik mengikut sebarang definisi. Malaysia membuat konsesi luas dan mengikat merentas mineral, percukaian digital, penjajaran sekatan, dan perolehan. AS mengurangkan tarif punitif sebanyak 6 mata peratusan dan mengekalkan hak untuk mengenakan semula bila-bila masa. Dalam mana-mana buku teks rundingan, ini dipanggil penyerahan.
  • Klausa kedaulatan adalah peruntukan paling berbahaya. Jika Malaysia mahu memperdalam hubungan dagangan dengan China — rakan dagangan terbesarnya — AS boleh menamatkan ART dan mengenakan semula tarif 25%. Malaysia secara efektif telah memberi Washington veto ke atas dasar ekonomi luarnya.
  • Menerbitkan butiran di laman web Rumah Putih sebelum memaklumkan Parlimen bukan sekadar memalukan — ia adalah penghinaan terhadap perlembagaan. Perjanjian perdagangan yang mengekang kedaulatan negara sepatutnya memerlukan perbahasan dan kelulusan parlimen sebelum ditandatangani. Anwar memintas badan perundangan mengenai perjanjian ekonomi paling berkesan dalam beberapa dekad.
  • Setiap jiran ASEAN sedang memerhati. Indonesia menolak cadangan AS yang serupa. Vietnam berunding lebih keras. Thailand menangguhkan. Malaysia menandatangani dahulu dan bertanya soalan kemudian. Ini melemahkan posisi rundingan Malaysia dalam setiap perbincangan perdagangan dua hala dan pelbagai hala masa depan.
  • Pembelaan ‘yang terbaik yang kita boleh dapat’ adalah pengakuan kelemahan, bukan justifikasi. Jika ini benar-benar hasil terbaik, bermakna leveraj diplomatik Malaysia lebih lemah daripada yang didakwa kerajaan — yang menimbulkan persoalan tentang keseluruhan jenama dasar luar Madani. *Sumber: The Guardian · East Asia Forum · SCMP · *Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Tengku Zafrul Rundingkan Perjanjian Perdagangan AS Yang Pengkritik Panggil 'Perjanjian Terburuk Sejak Merdeka' — Kemudian Dinaikkan Pangkat
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, former MITI Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 26 Oktober 2025, Menteri MITI ketika itu Tengku Zafrul menandatangani Perjanjian Perdagangan Timbal Balik (ART) dengan Amerika Syarikat di Sidang Kemuncak ASEAN ke-47 di Kuala Lumpur. Perjanjian itu mengkomitkan Malaysia untuk menyediakan akses pasaran keutamaan bagi produk AS merentas bahan kimia, jentera, kenderaan, tenusu, ayam, daging babi, beras, dan etanol bahan api. Malaysia juga komited untuk tidak mengenakan larangan atau kuota ke atas eksport mineral kritikal atau nadir bumi ke AS. Sebagai balasan, AS mengekalkan tarif timbal balik 19% ke atas barangan Malaysia — dengan hanya 60.4% baris tarif pada kadar sifar. Pengkritik dari seluruh spektrum politik, termasuk Mahathir dan Rafizi Ramli, memanggilnya penyerahan berat sebelah.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Helaian fakta USTR diterbitkan di laman web Rumah Putih sebelum dikongsi dengan Parlimen Malaysia — bermakna Washington memberitahu dunia apa yang Malaysia persetujui sebelum penggubal undang-undang Malaysia sendiri tahu. Perjanjian itu memerlukan Malaysia menyelaraskan dengan sekatan AS, bertindak terhadap ‘amalan tidak adil’ oleh firma negara ketiga (baca: China), dan membuka pasaran dalam sektor di mana pengeluar Malaysia terdedah. Eurasia Review menyatakan perjanjian itu ‘disusun tergesa-gesa untuk menyenangkan presiden AS semasa lawatannya’ dan ’tidak bertujuan untuk menarik banyak perhatian.’ Ia menarik banyak perhatian.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Zafrul mempertahankan perjanjian itu sebagai ‘senario terbaik’ — seolah-olah berunding dari posisi lemah adalah satu pencapaian. Beliau memberitahu Parlimen: ‘Kita tiada pilihan selain berunding.’ Itu bukan pembelaan; itu pengakuan. Dasar luar neutral Malaysia — yang dikekalkan selama berdekad-dekad — terjejas dalam satu majlis tandatangan. Ganjaran Zafrul untuk ini? Kenaikan pangkat kepada Penasihat Politik Kanan PM dan pelantikan sebagai Pengerusi MIDA. Dalam Malaysia PH, anda dinaikkan pangkat kerana menandatangani penyerahan kedaulatan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia pernah menandatangani perjanjian perdagangan sebelum ini tanpa tahap kontroversi ini. CPTPP dirundingkan selama bertahun-tahun dengan penelitian parlimen yang meluas. ART digegas dalam beberapa minggu, ditandatangani di sidang kemuncak, dan dipertahankan selepas fakta. Corak keputusan ekonomi PH — dari rangkaian dwi 5G ke ECRL ke ini — adalah konsisten: buat pilihan yang selesa secara politik, kemudian habiskan berbulan-bulan mempertahankannya.

🎯 TEGURAN KEPADA PH: Menteri anda menandatangani perjanjian yang mengkomitkan Malaysia kepada pematuhan sekatan AS dan jaminan eksport mineral, dan pembelaan anda adalah ‘kita tiada pilihan.’ Anda sentiasa ada pilihan — anda memilih untuk tunduk. Kemudian anda naikkan pangkat orang yang melakukannya. Jika ini benar-benar ‘senario terbaik,’ posisi rundingan Malaysia di bawah PH lebih teruk daripada yang sesiapa bayangkan.

Sumber: CNA | USTR | Eurasia Review

item.title: Malaysia Committed to Never Banning Rare Earth Exports to the US — Under Zafrul's Trade Deal
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / USTR

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Under the US-Malaysia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade signed by Tengku Zafrul on 26 October 2025, Malaysia committed to not imposing ‘quantitative restrictions’ (bans or quotas) on exports of critical or rare-earth minerals to the United States. The USTR fact sheet explicitly highlights this as a US victory. Meanwhile, Zafrul dismissed Mahathir’s claim that Malaysia agreed to supply unprocessed rare earth at low returns, saying existing policy mandates domestic processing before export.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Rare earth minerals are the 21st century’s most strategic resource — essential for semiconductors, defence systems, EVs, and renewable energy. China controls roughly 60% of global rare earth mining and 90% of processing. The US desperately needs alternative supplies, and Malaysia — with Lynas’s Kuantan plant — is a key node. By committing in writing to never restrict exports to the US, Malaysia surrendered one of its most powerful geopolitical leverage points for nothing more than maintaining the status quo tariff.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Indonesia, Australia, and other mineral-rich nations are using export controls as negotiating leverage to attract downstream processing and technology transfer. Malaysia gave that leverage away. The commitment means that even if a future government wants to use rare earth as a bargaining chip — as China has done effectively — Malaysia is contractually bound not to. Zafrul traded a strategic asset for a tariff rate that didn’t even improve.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: China’s rare earth export restrictions have been its most effective trade weapon for decades. Indonesia’s nickel export ban forced companies to build smelters domestically, creating jobs and value-add. Malaysia had the same opportunity — and signed it away in a trade deal.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You just gave the United States a written guarantee that Malaysia will never use its rare earth resources as leverage. Not for technology transfer. Not for downstream jobs. Not for better trade terms. For nothing. Your minister says Malaysia’s processing-first policy is intact — but the agreement says you can’t restrict exports. When Washington comes knocking for raw minerals, your ‘policy’ will fold against a treaty obligation.

Sources: USTR Fact Sheet | FMT

item.title: Tengku Zafrul Negotiated the US Trade Deal Critics Call 'Worst Agreement Since Independence' — Then Got Promoted
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, former MITI Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 26 October 2025, then-MITI Minister Tengku Zafrul signed the Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) with the United States at the 47th ASEAN Summit in Kuala Lumpur. The deal committed Malaysia to providing preferential market access for US products across chemicals, machinery, vehicles, dairy, poultry, pork, rice, and fuel ethanol. Malaysia also committed to not imposing bans or quotas on the export of critical or rare-earth minerals to the US. In return, the US maintained a 19% reciprocal tariff on Malaysian goods — with only 60.4% of tariff lines zero-rated. Critics from across the political spectrum, including Mahathir and Rafizi Ramli, called it a one-sided surrender.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The USTR fact sheet was published on the White House website before it was shared with Malaysia’s Parliament — meaning Washington told the world what Malaysia agreed to before Malaysia’s own legislators knew. The deal requires Malaysia to mirror US sanctions, act against ‘unfair practices’ by third-country firms (read: China), and open markets in sectors where Malaysian producers are vulnerable. Eurasia Review noted the deal was ‘hastily put together to please the US president during his visit’ and was ’not intended to catch much attention.’ It caught plenty.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Zafrul defended the deal as the ‘best case scenario’ — as if negotiating from weakness is an achievement. He told Parliament: ‘We had no choice but to negotiate.’ That is not a defence; it’s a confession. Malaysia’s neutral foreign policy — maintained for decades — was compromised in one signing ceremony. Zafrul’s reward for this? A promotion to Senior Political Advisor to the PM and appointment as MIDA chairman. In PH’s Malaysia, you get promoted for signing away sovereignty.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has signed trade agreements before without this level of controversy. The CPTPP was negotiated over years with extensive parliamentary scrutiny. The ART was rushed through in weeks, signed at a summit, and defended after the fact. The pattern of PH economic decisions — from 5G dual network to ECRL to this — is consistent: make the politically convenient choice, then spend months defending it.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your minister signed a deal that commits Malaysia to US sanctions compliance and mineral export guarantees, and your defence is ‘we had no choice.’ You always have a choice — you chose to capitulate. Then you promoted the man who did it. If this was really the ‘best case scenario,’ Malaysia’s negotiating position under PH is worse than anyone imagined.

Sources: CNA | USTR | Eurasia Review

item.title: US Trade Deal Details Published on White House Website Before Malaysian Parliament Was Told
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / White House

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: When the US-Malaysia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade was signed on 26 October 2025, the USTR published a detailed fact sheet on the White House website outlining Malaysia’s commitments — including preferential market access for US goods, commitments on rare earth minerals, and tariff schedules. Malaysian MPs and the public learned the details of their own country’s trade agreement from an American government website. The deal’s terms were published in Washington before they were presented in Putrajaya.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A sovereign nation’s trade commitments should be communicated to its own parliament and citizens before they are announced by the other party. The fact that Malaysians had to read the White House website to understand what their government had agreed to is a humiliation of parliamentary sovereignty. It also meant the US controlled the narrative — the terms were framed as an American victory before Malaysia could spin its own story.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH promised transparent governance and parliamentary supremacy. Then Zafrul signed a trade agreement and let Washington publish the terms first. Fahmi Fadzil is the Communications Minister — where was the proactive Malaysian disclosure? The sequence tells you everything about the power dynamics: the US dictated the terms, dictated the timeline, and dictated the narrative. Malaysia’s role was to sign and defend.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s TPPA negotiations under Najib were also criticised for opacity — but at least the government attempted to control its own narrative. Under PH, the narrative was literally ceded to the White House. Eurasia Review noted the deal was ‘hastily put together’ and ’not intended to catch much attention.’ The haste is the point — rushed deals favour the stronger party.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You let the White House tell Malaysians what their own government agreed to. That is not a partnership of equals. That is a relationship where one party writes the press release and the other party nods. If you can’t even control the communications around your own trade deals, how do you expect Malaysians to believe you controlled the negotiations?

Sources: USTR Fact Sheet | Eurasia Review

item.title: US-Malaysia Trade Deal Called 'Worst Agreement Since Independence' and 'Act of Surrender'
item.description:
Said by: Tommy Thomas, Former Attorney General (and critics)

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

The Deal That Sold Malaysia’s Sovereignty

On 26 October 2025, Anwar Ibrahim and Donald Trump signed the US-Malaysia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) at the ASEAN Summit. Former Attorney-General Tommy Thomas called it the worst agreement Malaysia has entered since independence in 1957. He was not exaggerating.

The Facts

Fact: The deal maintains a 19% US tariff on Malaysian goods — down from 25%, but still punitive by any standard. Malaysia’s effective tariff on US goods before this deal was roughly 6%. The US got the better end by a factor of three.

Fact: Malaysia committed to: enforce US tariff evasion rules against third countries, align with US export controls and sanctions regimes, not ban or restrict exports of critical minerals or rare earths to the US, refrain from imposing digital services taxes on US tech companies, and purchase additional American goods and services.

Fact: A termination clause allows the US to reimpose the original 25% tariff if Malaysia enters a trade deal that “jeopardizes essential US interests.” This effectively gives Washington veto power over Malaysia’s independent trade policy — including with China, Malaysia’s largest trading partner.

Fact: The deal’s full text was first published on the White House website before being shared with Malaysian Parliament. Malaysian lawmakers learned the terms of their own trade agreement from a foreign government’s press release.

Fact: Former PM Mahathir said the provisions amounted to “handing over” the country’s independence. Even for a man who oversaw his own share of questionable deals, this one was a bridge too far.

Fact: East Asia Forum’s analysis, titled “The Art of the Deal Lost in US-Malaysia Pact,” argued Malaysia surrendered critical mineral export controls, digital services taxation rights, and independent trade policy in exchange for a tariff cut that still left Malaysia paying three times what the US pays.

Fact: The Guardian reported that Anwar “defended” the deal by arguing it was “the best Malaysia could get under the circumstances” — an argument that concedes the deal is bad while claiming it could have been worse.

Fact: SCMP reported Anwar “slammed” critics for accusing him of surrendering sovereignty — but never addressed the substance of their arguments, only their tone.

Why It Matters

  • This is not a reciprocal deal by any definition of the word. Malaysia makes extensive, binding concessions across minerals, digital taxation, sanctions alignment, and procurement. The US reduces a punitive tariff by 6 percentage points and retains the right to reimpose it at will. In any negotiation textbook, this is called capitulation.
  • The sovereignty clause is the most dangerous provision. If Malaysia wants to deepen trade ties with China — its largest trading partner — the US can terminate the ART and reimpose 25% tariffs. Malaysia has effectively given Washington a veto over its foreign economic policy.
  • Publishing the details on the White House website before informing Parliament is not just embarrassing — it is a constitutional affront. Trade agreements that constrain national sovereignty should require parliamentary debate and approval before signing. Anwar bypassed the legislature on the most consequential economic agreement in decades.
  • Every ASEAN neighbour is watching. Indonesia walked away from a similar US proposal. Vietnam negotiated harder. Thailand delayed. Malaysia signed first and asked questions later. This weakens Malaysia’s negotiating position in every future bilateral and multilateral trade discussion.
  • The “best we could get” defence is an admission of weakness, not a justification. If this was truly the best outcome, it means Malaysia’s diplomatic leverage is weaker than the government has claimed — which raises questions about the entire Madani foreign policy brand. *Sources: The Guardian · East Asia Forum · SCMP · *Straits Times

item.title: [BM] 77% Rakyat Malaysia Bergelut Dengan Kos Sara Hidup Yang Meningkat — Tetapi Anwar Kata Ekonomi 'Kukuh'
item.description:
Said by: Consumer Frontiers International survey

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-15

77% Berbanding KDNK

Pada Oktober 2025, satu tinjauan mendapati bahawa 77% rakyat Malaysia berkata mereka bergelut dengan kos sara hidup yang meningkat. Pada bulan yang sama, kerajaan memetik pertumbuhan KDNK dan angka pelaburan sebagai bukti ekonomi kukuh.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Consumer Frontiers International mendapati 77% rakyat Malaysia bergelut dengan kos yang meningkat meskipun gaji bertambah.

Fakta: DOSM melaporkan purata pendapatan isi rumah boleh guna bulanan meningkat 3.2% kepada RM7,584 pada 2024.

Fakta: Tetapi ahli ekonomi Lee Heng Guie memberi amaran: “Kita juga mesti mengakui bahawa kos sara hidup terus meningkat.” Keuntungan sebenar sedang dihakis oleh inflasi.

Fakta: The Star meletakkan tajuk: “Kos sara hidup yang meningkat meredam keuntungan pendapatan.”

Kenapa Ia Penting

  • Pertumbuhan KDNK dan pelaburan diluluskan adalah angka makro. Ia tidak mencerminkan apa yang dialami 77% penduduk.
  • Apabila tiga perempat rakyat anda berkata mereka bergelut, memetik angka pelaburan adalah pekak nada.
  • Jurang antara statistik kerajaan dan pengalaman hidup adalah jurang yang mewujudkan gerakan bantahan. *Sumber: Business Today · *The Star

item.title: 77% of Malaysians Struggle With Rising Living Costs — But Anwar Says Economy Is 'Strong'
item.description:
Said by: Consumer Frontiers International survey

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-15

The 77% vs The GDP

In October 2025, a survey found that 77% of Malaysians said they struggled with rising living costs. In the same month, the government was citing GDP growth and investment figures as evidence that the economy was strong.

The Facts

Fact: Consumer Frontiers International found 77% of Malaysians struggled with rising costs despite rising wages.

Fact: DOSM reported average monthly disposable household income increased 3.2% to RM7,584 in 2024.

Fact: But economist Lee Heng Guie warned: “We must also recognise that the cost of living continues to rise.” Real gains were being eroded by inflation.

Fact: The Star headlined it: “Rising cost of living dampens income gains.”

Why It Matters

  • GDP growth and approved investments are macro numbers. They do not reflect what 77% of the population is experiencing.
  • When three-quarters of your citizens say they’re struggling, citing investment figures is tone-deaf.
  • The gap between government statistics and lived experience is the gap that creates protest movements. *Sources: Business Today · *The Star

item.title: [BM] Pekerja Ekonomi Gig Masih Tanpa Perlindungan — Penghantar Grab Dan Foodpanda Pertaruhkan Nyawa Untuk Gaji Kemiskinan

item.title: [BM] MITI Dituduh Curi Konsep Pavilion Ekspo Dunia Osaka — Agensi Kreatif Hantar Surat Tuntutan
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / Current Media Group

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-08-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Ogos 2025, MITI di bawah Tengku Zafrul dihantar surat tuntutan oleh Current Media Group (CMG), sebuah konsultansi kreatif yang mendakwa kementerian mencuri konsepnya untuk pavilion Ekspo Dunia 2025 Osaka Malaysia tanpa memberikan pengiktirafan atau bayaran. Dakwaan itu menyatakan pitching asal CMG digunakan sebagai asas reka bentuk pavilion tanpa kontrak atau kredit. Zafrul menjawab perkara itu dalam jawapan parlimen kepada AP Kepong Lim Lip Eng.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Jika sebuah kementerian kerajaan menggunakan harta intelek syarikat swasta tanpa bayaran atau kredit, itu adalah kecurian — tanpa mengira sama ada ia dipanggil ‘inspirasi’ atau ‘pembangunan selari.’ Pavilion Ekspo Dunia adalah pameran nasional berprofil tinggi dengan bajet yang besar. Dakwaan bahawa MITI mengambil konsep tanpa membayarnya menimbulkan persoalan serius tentang integriti perolehan dan penghormatan harta intelek dalam kementerian.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Kerajaan ‘reformis’ yang menceramah dunia tentang tadbir urus baik dan urusan adil didakwa mencuri konsep kreatif daripada syarikat Malaysia. PH naik berkuasa menjanjikan perolehan telus dan penghormatan terhadap proses wajar. Jika MITI tidak mampu menugaskan reka bentuk pavilion tanpa menerima surat tuntutan daripada pencipta asal, apa yang dikatakan tentang bagaimana kontrak lain yang lebih besar diuruskan di sebalik tabir?

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Kecurian harta intelek kerajaan daripada kontraktor swasta adalah aduan berulang di Malaysia. Agensi kreatif, arkitek, dan perunding telah lama melaporkan bahawa pitching kerajaan dilayan sebagai kerja percuma — idea diambil, kontrak pergi ke tempat lain, dan pencipta asal ditinggalkan. Di bawah BN, ini adalah standard. Di bawah PH, ia sepatutnya berubah.

🎯 TEGURAN PH: Anda mewakili Malaysia di pentas dunia di Osaka dan anda tidak mampu membayar orang yang mereka bentuknya? Jika dakwaan itu benar, pampaskan CMG dan siasat bagaimana ini berlaku. Jika palsu, keluarkan dokumen perolehan. Ketelusan berfungsi dua hala — anda tidak boleh menceramah dunia tentang tadbir urus sementara kementerian anda sendiri dihantar surat tuntutan atas kecurian harta intelek.

Sumber: Marketing Interactive

item.title: MITI Accused of Stealing World Expo Osaka Pavilion Concept — Creative Agency Serves Letter of Demand
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / Current Media Group

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-08-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In August 2025, MITI under Tengku Zafrul was served a letter of demand by Current Media Group (CMG), a creative consultancy that claimed the ministry stole its concept for Malaysia’s World Expo 2025 Osaka pavilion without providing recognition or payment. The allegation was that CMG’s original pitch was used as the basis for the pavilion design without a contract or credit. Zafrul addressed the matter in a parliamentary reply to Kepong MP Lim Lip Eng.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: If a government ministry used a private company’s intellectual property without payment or credit, that is theft — regardless of whether it’s called ‘inspiration’ or ‘parallel development.’ World Expo pavilions are high-profile national showcases with significant budgets. The allegation that MITI took a concept without paying for it raises serious questions about procurement integrity and intellectual property respect within the ministry.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The ‘reformist’ government that lectures the world about good governance and fair dealing allegedly stole a creative concept from a Malaysian company. PH came to power promising transparent procurement and respect for due process. If MITI can’t even commission a pavilion design without a letter of demand from the original creator, what does that say about how other, larger contracts are handled behind closed doors?

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Government IP theft from private contractors is a recurring complaint in Malaysia. Creative agencies, architects, and consultants have long reported that government pitches are treated as free work — ideas are taken, contracts go elsewhere, and the original creators are ghosted. Under BN, this was standard. Under PH, it was supposed to change.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You represent Malaysia on the world stage at Osaka and you can’t even pay the people who designed it? If the allegation is true, compensate CMG and investigate how this happened. If it’s false, release the procurement documents. Transparency works both ways — you don’t get to lecture the world about governance while your own ministry is served letters of demand for IP theft.

Sources: Marketing Interactive

item.title: [BM] Puluhan Ribu Berarak Tuntut Anwar Letak Jawatan Atas Kos Sara Hidup — Bantahan Besar Pertama Sejak PRU15
item.description:
Said by: Opposition parties and protesters

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-26

Jalanan Bersuara

Pada 26 Julai 2025, puluhan ribu rakyat Malaysia berarak ke Dataran Merdeka di Kuala Lumpur, menuntut Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim meletak jawatan. Himpunan itu — bantahan besar pertama sejak PRU15 — didorong oleh kos sara hidup yang meningkat dan janji reformasi yang dimungkiri.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Al Jazeera melaporkan ribuan berkumpul di Dataran Merdeka, menuntut Anwar meletak jawatan.

Fakta: Tajuk Bloomberg terus terang: “Janji Mungkir Anwar Cetus Bantahan Menuntut Penyingkirannya.”

Fakta: Himpunan dianjurkan parti pembangkang tetapi menarik penyertaan awam luas didorong kemarahan kos sara hidup.

Fakta: Menjelang Oktober 2025, tinjauan mendapati 77% rakyat Malaysia berkata mereka terbeban dengan kos sara hidup yang meningkat.

Fakta: Hanya beberapa hari sebelum bantahan, Anwar mengumumkan bayaran tunai RM100 kepada semua warga dewasa dan pemotongan harga RON95 — dilihat meluas sebagai langkah mendahului.

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Apabila puluhan ribu berarak menentang kerajaan yang menjanjikan “keluarga Malaysia” dan reformasi, ia bukan bantahan pinggiran. Ia adalah keputusan.
  • Bantuan RM100 adalah rawatan politik — pelekat luka pada kesakitan ekonomi struktur.
  • 77% terbeban kos sara hidup. Itu bukan naratif pembangkang. Itu realiti negara. *Sumber: Al Jazeera · Bloomberg · *Business Today

item.title: Tens of Thousands March Demanding Anwar Resign Over Cost of Living — First Major Protest Since GE15
item.description:
Said by: Opposition parties and protesters

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-26

The Streets Speak

On 26 July 2025, tens of thousands of Malaysians marched to Merdeka Square in Kuala Lumpur, demanding Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim step down. The rally — the first major protest since GE15 — was driven by rising costs and broken reform promises.

The Facts

Fact: Al Jazeera reported thousands gathered at Independence Square, calling for Anwar to resign.

Fact: Bloomberg’s headline was blunt: “Anwar’s Broken Promises Spark Protests Demanding His Ouster.”

Fact: The rally was organized by opposition parties but attracted broad public participation driven by cost of living anger.

Fact: By October 2025, a survey found 77% of Malaysians said they struggled with rising living costs.

Fact: Just days before the protest, Anwar announced a RM100 cash payment to all adult citizens and a further RON95 price cut — widely seen as a pre-emptive measure.

Why It Matters

  • When tens of thousands march against a government that promised “keluarga Malaysia” and reform, it is not a fringe protest. It is a verdict.
  • The RM100 handout was political triage — a band-aid on structural economic pain.
  • 77% struggling with cost of living. That is not an opposition narrative. That is a national reality. *Sources: Al Jazeera · Bloomberg · *Business Today

item.title: [BM] Dasar Bumiputera 'Tidak Boleh Dirunding' Dalam Rundingan Perdagangan AS — Anwar Pertahan Ekonomi Berasaskan Kaum
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-21

Anwar declared Bumiputera policy ’non-negotiable’ in US tariff talks, saying ’no country should interfere with domestic decisions.’ Critics argue race-based policies are the very market distortion the US objects to.

item.title: Bumiputera Policy 'Non-Negotiable' in US Trade Talks — Anwar Defends Race-Based Economics
item.description:

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-21
