item.title: [BM] Tarif Elektrik Industri Dinaikkan Pada Hari Sama SST Diperluaskan — 'Periuk Berita Buruk'
item.description:
Said by: Ministry of Finance

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-01

Periuk Berita Buruk

Pada 1 Julai 2025, Malaysia dilanda tamparan berganda: SST yang diperluaskan berkuat kuasa, meliputi sewaan, penjagaan kesihatan, pendidikan dan banyak lagi, seiring kenaikan tarif elektrik untuk sektor perindustrian. Straits Times memanggilnya “periuk berita buruk ekonomi.”

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Perluasan SST berkuat kuasa 1 Julai 2025, mengenakan cukai ke atas enam kategori perkhidmatan baharu termasuk sewaan, pembinaan, perkhidmatan kewangan, penjagaan kesihatan swasta, dan pendidikan.

Fakta: Pada hari yang sama, tarif elektrik meningkat untuk sektor perindustrian.

Fakta: Kerajaan juga merancang mengurangkan subsidi gas memasak untuk kedai makan komersial dan melaksanakan reformasi subsidi RON95 menjelang akhir tahun.

Fakta: Straits Times menyatakan “perluasan SST hanyalah langkah kontroversi terbaru” oleh kerajaan.

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Mana-mana satu langkah ini boleh ditangani. Kesemua pada garis masa yang sama adalah serangan kos sara hidup.
  • Kesan terkumpul kepada perniagaan kecil adalah dahsyat: cukai lebih tinggi, elektrik lebih tinggi, minyak lebih tinggi, dan subsidi dikurangkan.
  • Kerajaan kata setiap langkah adalah “perlu.” Tetapi keperluan tanpa penjujukan tetap tadbir urus yang lemah. *Sumber: *Straits Times

item.title: Electricity Tariffs Hiked for Industry on Same Day as SST Expansion — 'Hotpot of Bad News'
item.description:
Said by: Ministry of Finance

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-01

The Hotpot of Bad News

On 1 July 2025, Malaysia was hit by a double whammy: the expanded SST took effect, covering rental, healthcare, education, and more, alongside increased electricity tariffs for the industrial sector. The Straits Times called it a “hotpot of bad economic news.”

The Facts

Fact: SST expansion took effect 1 July 2025, taxing six new service categories including rental, construction, financial services, private healthcare, and education.

Fact: On the same day, electricity tariffs increased for the industrial sector.

Fact: The government was also planning to reduce cooking gas subsidies for commercial eateries and implement the RON95 subsidy reform by year end.

Fact: The Straits Times noted the “widening of the SST is just the latest controversial move” by the government.

Why It Matters

  • Any one of these measures is manageable. All of them on the same timeline is a cost of living assault.
  • The cumulative effect on small businesses is devastating: higher tax, higher electricity, higher fuel, and reduced subsidies.
  • The government says each measure is “necessary.” But necessity without sequencing is still bad governance. *Source: *Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Rafizi Ramli Dipaksa Keluar Dari Kementerian Ekonomi — Anwar Kata 'Tiada Perlu Rombak Apa-apa'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-16

Menteri Ekonomi Pergi Dan PM Mengangkat Bahu

Pada Jun 2025, Rafizi Ramli — boleh dikatakan menteri paling berasaskan data dalam kabinet Anwar — meletak jawatan sebagai Menteri Ekonomi selepas kalah dalam perlumbaan timbalan presiden PKR kepada Nurul Izzah. Respons Anwar amat santai.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Rafizi meletak jawatan daripada kabinet pada 16 Jun 2025, berikutan kekalahannya dalam pilihan raya parti PKR.

Fakta: Anwar memberitahu wartawan: “Saya tidak nampak keperluan untuk merombak apa-apa setakat ini. Saya ucapkan selamat maju jaya kepada Rafizi.”

Fakta: Portfolio Kementerian Ekonomi kekal kosong selama enam bulan sehingga rombakan Disember 2025, apabila Akmal Nasrullah dilantik.

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Kementerian Ekonomi bukan portfolio kecil. Membiarkannya kosong selama setengah tahun semasa menguruskan reformasi subsidi, tekanan kos sara hidup, dan penggabungan fiskal adalah kecuaian.
  • Sikap santai Anwar menunjukkan ekonomi sebenar tidak pernah sepenting ekonomi politik — iaitu pengurusan gabungan dan penempatan pilihan raya.
  • Rafizi sejak itu muncul sebagai pengkritik dalaman, memberi amaran bahawa Malaysia “tidak mampu lagi” menangguhkan reformasi. SCMP bertanya pada Februari 2026 sama ada Rafizi kini adalah “hati nurani gerakan reformasi” — gelaran yang pernah dipegang Anwar. *Sumber: Bloomberg · SCMP · *Reuters

item.title: Rafizi Ramli Forced Out of Economy Ministry — Anwar Says 'No Need to Reshuffle Anything'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-16

The Economy Minister Leaves and the PM Shrugs

In June 2025, Rafizi Ramli — arguably the most data-driven minister in Anwar’s cabinet — resigned as Economy Minister after losing the PKR deputy president race to Nurul Izzah. Anwar’s response was breathtakingly casual.

The Facts

Fact: Rafizi resigned from cabinet on 16 June 2025, following his defeat in the PKR party elections.

Fact: Anwar told reporters: “I do not see the need to reshuffle anything so far. I wish all the best to Rafizi.”

Fact: The Economy Ministry portfolio remained vacant for six months until the December 2025 reshuffle, when Akmal Nasrullah was appointed.

Why It Matters

  • The Economy Ministry is not a minor portfolio. Leaving it vacant for half a year while managing subsidy reform, cost of living pressures, and fiscal consolidation is negligence.
  • Anwar’s casual dismissal suggests the real economy was never as important as the political economy — meaning coalition management and election positioning.
  • Rafizi has since emerged as an internal critic, warning that Malaysia “can no longer afford” to delay reforms. SCMP asked in February 2026 whether Rafizi is now “the conscience of the reformasi movement” — a title Anwar once held. *Sources: Bloomberg · SCMP · *Reuters

item.title: [BM] Beban Pensijilan Halal Wajib Ke Atas PKS Bukan Islam — Bayar Atau Tutup Kedai
item.description:
Said by: SME Owners / JAKIM

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Keperluan pensijilan halal JAKIM yang semakin meluas mengenakan kos pematuhan yang ketara ke atas perniagaan makanan, termasuk yang dimiliki oleh bukan Islam. Perniagaan mesti membayar untuk pensijilan, mematuhi keperluan menyeluruh meliputi ramuan, penyediaan, penyimpanan dan kakitangan, serta menjalani audit berkala. Belanjawan JAKIM sebanyak RM2.6 bilion menjadikannya salah satu agensi paling banyak dibiayai dalam kerajaan — lebih banyak daripada banyak agensi yang berkhidmat terus kepada semua rakyat Malaysia.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Bagi perniagaan makanan kecil bukan Islam — kedai kopi Cina, restoran India, kedai masakan campuran — keperluan pensijilan halal mewujudkan beban kewangan dan operasi yang boleh menjadi terlalu membebankan. Pilihan menjadi: belanja beribu-ribu untuk pematuhan halal (dalam perniagaan yang mungkin tidak melayani majoriti pelanggan Islam) atau hilang akses kepada kontrak kerajaan, acara, dan ruang komersial tertentu yang memerlukan pensijilan halal.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

JAKIM menerima RM2.6 bilion setahun — lebih daripada belanjawan sukan, lebih daripada perlindungan alam sekitar, dan tidak seimbang dengan sumbangannya kepada KDNK atau kebajikan sosial. Rejim pensijilan yang semakin meluas berfungsi sebagai cukai agama ke atas perniagaan, dengan kos akhirnya diserahkan kepada pengguna. Bagi seorang PM yang memperjuangkan imej ‘sederhana’ di peringkat antarabangsa, pertumbuhan kuasa dan belanjawan JAKIM di bawah pengawasannya menceritakan kisah berbeza di dalam negara.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Kontroversi KK Mart pada 2024 — di mana rangkaian kedai serbaneka diboikot dan menghadapi pertuduhan jenayah kerana secara tidak sengaja menjual stoking dengan reka bentuk berkaitan Allah — menunjukkan bagaimana penguatkuasaan agama boleh memusnahkan perniagaan atas insiden kecil. Di bawah PH, agensi agama tidak diikat; mereka dikuatkan.

🎯 Seruan PH

Kerajaan ‘Madani’ anda membelanjakan RM2.6 bilion untuk JAKIM sementara penjagaan kesihatan runtuh dan sekolah menghasilkan pelajar yang tidak boleh mengira. Perniagaan bukan Islam mensubsidi birokrasi agama melalui kos pematuhan wajib. Jika ‘Malaysia Madani’ bermakna semua rakyat Malaysia, mengapa peruntukan belanjawan menunjukkan ia bermakna hanya infrastruktur agama satu komuniti?

Sumber

  • CNA — Anwar Hadapi Krisis Rasuah
  • Eurasia Review — Dasar Ekonomi Berasaskan Kaum

item.title: [BM] Kelemahan Ringgit Berterusan Walaupun Pertumbuhan KDNK — Mata Wang Tak Percaya Kisah Ekonomi Anwar
item.description:
Said by: BNM / Currency Markets

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Walaupun naratif pertumbuhan KDNK yang optimistik daripada kerajaan, ringgit Malaysia kekal lemah secara berterusan berbanding dolar AS dan mata wang serantau, berlegar sekitar paras terendah dalam sejarah. Pasaran mata wang — yang menghimpunkan pertimbangan kolektif ribuan pelabur dan penganalisis — pada dasarnya mengundi tidak percaya terhadap asas ekonomi Malaysia.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Kelemahan ringgit membawa kesan langsung kepada rakyat Malaysia biasa: barangan import menjadi lebih mahal (menyumbang kepada inflasi makanan yang dirasai rakyat tetapi tidak dicerminkan sepenuhnya oleh statistik rasmi), kos pendidikan luar negara meningkat, dan penghijrahan otak semakin pesat kerana profesional Malaysia mendapati kemahiran mereka bernilai berlipat ganda di negara mata wang lebih kukuh.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Kerajaan menunjuk kepada faktor global termasuk dasar monetari AS dan ketidaktentuan geopolitik, dan ini memang faktor sebenar. Tetapi perbandingan serantau mendedahkan ringgit telah mengatasi prestasi beberapa rakan ASEAN, menunjukkan faktor khusus Malaysia sedang bermain: ketidaktentuan politik, skandal SPRM, kebimbangan tentang kelestarian fiskal dengan nisbah hutang-kepada-KDNK 64.6%, dan persoalan tentang kebolehpercayaan reformasi ekonomi. Pasaran mata wang adalah barometer kejujuran yang brutal terhadap keyakinan negara. Kelemahan ringgit menceritakan kisah yang tidak diceritakan oleh angka KDNK kerajaan.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda boleh memilih angka KDNK untuk sidang akhbar. Tetapi anda tidak boleh memutar pasaran forex. Ringgit tidak peduli tentang poin cakap anda — ia bertindak balas kepada asas. Dan sekarang, asas tersebut mengatakan pasaran tidak percaya kisah ekonomi anda. Betulkan substansi, dan mata wang akan mengikut. Terus berputar, dan ia tidak akan.

Sumber

  • Bloomberg — Anwar Mengenai Tekanan Hutang

item.title: [BM] Krisis Persaraan KWSP — Purata Baki RM47,600 Pada Umur 55 Bermakna KEMISKINAN Di Hari Tua
item.description:
Said by: EPF Data / Retirement Adequacy Studies

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Data KWSP secara konsisten menunjukkan majoriti rakyat Malaysia mempunyai simpanan persaraan yang sangat tidak mencukupi. Baki median pada umur 55 (umur pengeluaran wajib) adalah kira-kira RM47,600 — cukup untuk bertahan kira-kira 5 tahun pada RM800/bulan, jauh di bawah mana-mana garis kemiskinan yang munasabah. Dasar kerajaan membenarkan pengeluaran KWSP berulang (Akaun 3 menyaksikan RM14.79 bilion dikeluarkan oleh 4.63 juta pencarum) telah mempercepatkan kehabisan dana persaraan.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Malaysia tidak mempunyai jaring keselamatan sosial menyeluruh untuk pesara selain KWSP (untuk sektor swasta) dan pencen (untuk penjawat awam). Apabila KWSP habis — yang berlaku untuk kebanyakan pesara dalam 5-10 tahun — warga emas Malaysia menghadapi kesengsaraan. Tiada pencen usia tua sejagat, kemudahan jagaan warga emas awam yang tidak mencukupi, dan bantuan sosial yang terhad. Satu generasi rakyat Malaysia sedang menuju kemiskinan persaraan.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

Daripada mengukuhkan simpanan persaraan, kerajaan telah berulang kali merompak KWSP untuk kemenangan politik jangka pendek. Akaun 3 — skim pengeluaran terbaru — dibentangkan sebagai ‘fleksibiliti’ tetapi hakikatnya kerajaan membenarkan rakyat membelanjakan masa depan mereka untuk bertahan pada masa kini. Apabila anda tidak mampu membetulkan gaji, kos sara hidup, atau pekerjaan, jawapan yang mudah secara politik ialah ‘biarkan mereka makan persaraan mereka.’ Ia bukan penyelesaian — ia menendang malapetaka 20 tahun ke hadapan.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Pengeluaran KWSP semasa COVID-19 (di bawah PN/BN) menghabiskan RM145 bilion daripada dana. PH mengkritik pengeluaran itu sebagai tidak bertanggungjawab. Kemudian PH memperkenalkan Akaun 3 dengan syarat pengeluaran yang lebih mudah. Setiap kerajaan — BN, PN, PH — telah melayan KWSP sebagai tabung simpanan untuk kemudahan politik. Pesara tahun 2040 akan membayar harganya.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda mengkritik Muhyiddin kerana membenarkan pengeluaran KWSP COVID. Kemudian anda mencipta Akaun 3, menjadikannya LEBIH MUDAH untuk mengeluarkan simpanan persaraan. RM14.79 bilion lesap dalam beberapa bulan. Anda telah memperdagangkan jaminan kewangan persaraan 4.63 juta rakyat Malaysia untuk kelulusan politik jangka pendek. Apabila orang-orang ini berumur 70 dan melarat, mereka akan ingat siapa yang membenarkan mereka membelanjakan hari tua mereka hari ini.

Sumber

  • KWSP — Belanjawan 2026
  • CNA — Pelan Pembangunan Malaysia

item.title: [BM] Pengumuman FDI Lawan Pekerjaan Sebenar — Angka RM Nampak Hebat, Tapi Mana Pekerjaan Untuk Rakyat Malaysia?
item.description:
Said by: MIDA / Economic Analysts

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Kerajaan kerap mengumumkan angka Pelaburan Langsung Asing (FDI) yang besar — berbilion ringgit dalam komitmen daripada gergasi teknologi, pengeluar dan pengendali pusat data. Pengumuman ini menjana tajuk berita yang mengagumkan. Walau bagaimanapun, jurang antara FDI yang diumumkan dan pelaburan sebenar yang direalisasikan, dan antara pelaburan dan pekerjaan sebenar rakyat Malaysia, kekal ketara.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Pengumuman FDI adalah kemenangan PR yang mudah — anda berdiri di sebelah CEO asing, tandatangan MOU, dan umumkan berbilion. Tetapi FDI diluluskan ≠ FDI direalisasikan (banyak projek ditangguh atau dibatalkan). FDI direalisasikan ≠ pekerjaan rakyat Malaysia (banyak pelaburan berintensif modal dengan pekerjaan minimum, atau menggaji pakar asing). Dan pekerjaan yang diwujudkan sering bukan jawatan berkualiti tinggi, bergaji baik yang menangani cabaran pengangguran dan genangan gaji Malaysia.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

Pusat data — permata mahkota pengumuman FDI — menggaji sangat sedikit orang berbanding saiz pelaburan mereka. Pusat data bernilai berbilion ringgit mungkin menggaji 50-200 kakitangan tetap, kebanyakan pakar teknikal. Bandingkan dengan kilang yang menggaji ribuan. Kerajaan mempromosikan angka FDI yang nampak mengagumkan di atas kertas sementara pengangguran belia kekal pada 12.28% dan pengangguran graduan berterusan. Angka tajuk berita tidak diterjemahkan kepada kehidupan rakyat Malaysia biasa.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

BN juga kerap mengumumkan angka FDI yang besar sementara kualiti pekerjaan tergenang. Program Transformasi Ekonomi Najib menghasilkan statistik pelaburan yang mengagumkan tetapi gagal menaikkan gaji median secara bermakna atau mengurangkan ketidaksamaan. PH berjanji strategi FDI mereka akan berbeza — lebih berkualiti, lebih banyak pekerjaan, lebih banyak pemindahan teknologi. Strateginya identik: umumkan angka besar, harap tiada siapa semak data pekerjaan.

🎯 Seruan PH

Berhenti mengumumkan komitmen FDI seolah-olah ia pencapaian. Umumkan PEKERJAAN. Umumkan GAJI. Umumkan berapa rakyat Malaysia mendapat pekerjaan, pada gaji berapa, dalam peranan apa. Satu bilion ringgit FDI yang mewujudkan 100 pekerjaan bukan kisah kejayaan ekonomi — ia pemberian insentif cukai yang menguntungkan pemegang saham asing sementara rakyat Malaysia menghantar makanan mereka dengan motosikal untuk RM6 sehantar.

Sumber

  • ISEAS — Transformasi Bumiputera 2035
  • Bloomberg — Tekanan Hutang Malaysia

item.title: [BM] Perangkap Kebergantungan Buruh Asing — Ekonomi Tak Boleh Berfungsi Tanpa Pekerja Yang Malaysia Layan Macam Barang Buangan
item.description:
Said by: Labour Economists / ILO

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Malaysia kekal sangat bergantung kepada pekerja asing — dianggarkan 2-3 juta berdokumen dan mungkin berjuta lagi tanpa dokumen — dalam sektor kritikal termasuk pembinaan, pembuatan, pertanian, dan perkhidmatan. Dasar pekerja asing kerajaan berayun antara liberalisasi dan sekatan berdasarkan angin politik dan bukannya perancangan ekonomi, mewujudkan ketidaktentuan kronik bagi majikan dan pekerja.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Kebergantungan buruh asing mendedahkan kegagalan Malaysia untuk bergerak naik rantaian nilai — ekonomi masih bergantung secara struktur kepada buruh murah yang boleh dieksploitasi dan bukannya automasi, teknologi, dan pekerja domestik berkemahiran. Kebergantungan ini menekan gaji tempatan, membolehkan eksploitasi, dan mewujudkan ketegangan sosial.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Setiap kerajaan berjanji ‘mengurangkan kebergantungan kepada buruh asing’ dan ‘bergerak ke ekonomi bernilai tinggi.’ Setiap kerajaan kemudian membenarkan berjuta pekerja asing kerana industri yang membiayai parti politik tidak boleh berfungsi tanpa mereka. Ladang kelapa sawit, syarikat pembinaan, dan kilang MEMERLUKAN buruh asing murah. Ahli politik MEMERLUKAN pembiayaan kempen daripada industri ini. Hasilnya ialah kitaran janji reformasi dan kebergantungan sebenar yang berkekalan.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Dasar pekerja asing BN sama kacau dan membolehkan eksploitasi. Laporan Pemerdagangan Orang Jabatan Negara AS telah menandakan isu buruh paksa Malaysia selama bertahun-tahun — di bawah BN, PN, dan PH. Sistem levi pekerja asing pelbagai peringkat, yang berulang kali ditangguhkan, sepatutnya menjadi penyelesaian PH. Ia bukan.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Ekonomi anda secara literal tidak boleh membina bangunan, menuai buah kelapa sawit, atau menjalankan restoran tanpa pekerja asing — pekerja yang tiada hak, tiada perlindungan, dan tiada suara. Kerangka ‘Madani’ anda nampaknya tidak merangkumi berjuta manusia yang sebenarnya menggerakkan ekonomi anda. Betulkan eksploitasi, betulkan gaji, betulkan kebergantungan — atau berhenti berpura-pura Malaysia adalah ekonomi yang bertamadun.

Sumber

  • ISEAS — Reformasi Subsidi Petrol
  • Eurasia Review — Dasar Ekonomi Berasaskan Kaum

item.title: [BM] PKS Susah Dapat Pinjaman — Bank Pilih Risiko Rendah Sementara Kerajaan Dakwa 'Mesra Perniagaan'
item.description:
Said by: SME Association / Bank Negara Data

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

SMEs employ 7M+ workers and contribute 40% of GDP but face significant barriers accessing financing. Banks remain risk-averse, loan rejection rates high. SMEs face a perfect storm: rising costs from SST, labour shortages, and slow-paying government agencies.

item.title: EPF Retirement Crisis — Average Balance of RM47,600 at Age 55 Means POVERTY in Old Age
item.description:
Said by: EPF Data / Retirement Adequacy Studies

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

EPF data consistently shows that the majority of Malaysians have grossly inadequate retirement savings. The median balance at age 55 (mandatory withdrawal age) is approximately RM47,600 — enough to last roughly 5 years at RM800/month, well below any reasonable poverty line. The government’s policy of allowing repeated EPF withdrawals (Account 3 saw RM14.79 billion withdrawn by 4.63 million contributors) has accelerated retirement fund depletion.

🔥 Why It Matters

Malaysia has no comprehensive social safety net for retirees outside of EPF (for private sector) and pensions (for civil servants). When EPF runs dry — which it does for most retirees within 5-10 years — elderly Malaysians face destitution. There is no universal old-age pension, inadequate public aged care facilities, and limited social assistance. A generation of Malaysians is heading toward retirement poverty.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Instead of strengthening retirement savings, the government has repeatedly raided EPF for short-term political wins. Account 3 — the latest withdrawal scheme — was presented as ‘flexibility’ but in reality it’s the government allowing citizens to spend their future to survive the present. When you can’t fix wages, cost of living, or employment, the politically easy answer is ’let them eat their retirement.’ It’s not a solution — it’s kicking the catastrophe 20 years down the road.

📜 Previous Cases

EPF withdrawals during COVID-19 (under PN/BN) depleted RM145 billion from the fund. PH criticised those withdrawals as irresponsible. Then PH introduced Account 3 with even easier withdrawal terms. Every government — BN, PN, PH — has treated EPF as a piggy bank for political convenience. The retirees of 2040 will pay the price.

🎯 PH Callout

You criticised Muhyiddin for allowing COVID EPF withdrawals. Then you created Account 3, making it even EASIER to withdraw retirement savings. RM14.79 billion gone in months. You’ve traded the financial security of 4.63 million Malaysians’ retirement for short-term political approval. When these people are 70 and destitute, they’ll remember who let them spend their old age today.

Sources

  • EPF — Belanjawan 2026
  • CNA — Malaysia Development Plan

item.title: FDI Announcements vs Actual Jobs — RM Numbers Look Great, But Where Are the Jobs for Malaysians?
item.description:
Said by: MIDA / Economic Analysts

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

The government regularly announces massive Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) figures — billions of ringgit in commitments from tech giants, manufacturers, and data centre operators. These announcements generate impressive headlines. However, the gap between announced FDI and actual realised investment, and between investment and actual Malaysian employment, remains significant.

🔥 Why It Matters

FDI announcements are easy PR wins — you stand next to a foreign CEO, sign an MOU, and announce billions. But approved FDI ≠ realised FDI (many projects are delayed or cancelled). Realised FDI ≠ Malaysian jobs (many investments are capital-intensive with minimal employment, or employ foreign specialists). And the jobs that are created are often not the high-quality, well-paying positions that address Malaysia’s unemployment and wage stagnation challenges.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Data centres — the crown jewel of FDI announcements — employ remarkably few people relative to their investment size. A billion-ringgit data centre might employ 50-200 permanent staff, mostly technical specialists. Compare that to a factory employing thousands. The government promotes FDI numbers that look impressive on paper while youth unemployment stays at 12.28% and graduate unemployment remains persistent. The headline numbers don’t translate to the lives of ordinary Malaysians.

📜 Previous Cases

BN also regularly announced massive FDI figures while employment quality stagnated. Najib’s Economic Transformation Programme produced impressive investment statistics but failed to meaningfully raise median wages or reduce inequality. PH promised that their FDI strategy would be different — more quality, more jobs, more technology transfer. The strategy is identical: announce big numbers, hope nobody checks the employment data.

🎯 PH Callout

Stop announcing FDI commitments as if they’re achievements. Announce JOBS. Announce WAGES. Announce how many Malaysians got employed, at what salary, in what roles. A billion ringgit in FDI that creates 100 jobs is not an economic success story — it’s a tax incentive giveaway that benefits foreign shareholders while Malaysians deliver their food on motorcycles for RM6 per trip.

Sources

  • ISEAS — Bumiputera Transformation 2035
  • Bloomberg — Malaysia Debt Pressure

item.title: Foreign Labour Dependency Trap — Economy Can't Function Without Workers Malaysia Treats Like Disposable Parts
item.description:
Said by: Labour Economists / ILO

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

Malaysia remains heavily dependent on foreign workers — estimated at 2-3 million documented and possibly millions more undocumented — in critical sectors including construction, manufacturing, agriculture, and services. The government’s foreign worker policy oscillates between liberalisation and restriction based on political winds rather than economic planning, creating chronic uncertainty for both employers and workers.

🔥 Why It Matters

Foreign worker dependency reveals Malaysia’s failure to move up the value chain — the economy is still structurally reliant on cheap, exploitable labour rather than automation, technology, and skilled domestic workers. This dependency suppresses local wages, enables exploitation, and creates social tensions.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Every government promises to ‘reduce dependency on foreign labour’ and ‘move to a high-value economy.’ Every government then allows millions of foreign workers because the industries that fund political parties can’t function without them. Palm oil plantations, construction companies, and factories NEED cheap foreign labour. Politicians NEED campaign funding from these industries. The result is a permanent cycle of promised reform and actual dependency.

📜 Previous Cases

BN’s foreign worker policies were equally chaotic and exploitation-enabling. The US State Department’s Trafficking in Persons report has flagged Malaysia’s forced labour issues for years — under BN, PN, and PH. The multi-tier foreign worker levy system, repeatedly delayed, was supposed to be PH’s solution. It wasn’t.

🎯 PH Callout

Your economy literally can’t build a building, harvest a palm oil fruit, or run a restaurant without foreign workers — workers who have no rights, no protections, and no voice. Your ‘Madani’ framework apparently doesn’t extend to the millions of human beings who actually make your economy function. Fix the exploitation, fix the wages, fix the dependency — or stop pretending Malaysia is a civilised economy.

Sources

  • ISEAS — Petrol Subsidy Reform
  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: Mandatory Halal Certification Burden on Non-Muslim SMEs — Pay Up or Close Down
item.description:
Said by: SME Owners / JAKIM

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

JAKIM’s expanding halal certification requirements impose significant compliance costs on food businesses, including those owned by non-Muslims. Businesses must pay for certification, comply with extensive requirements covering ingredients, preparation, storage, and staffing, and undergo regular audits. JAKIM’s budget of RM2.6 billion makes it one of the best-funded agencies in government — better funded than many agencies that directly serve all Malaysians.

🔥 Why It Matters

For small non-Muslim food businesses — Chinese coffee shops, Indian restaurants, mixed-cuisine eateries — halal certification requirements create a financial and operational burden that can be prohibitive. The choice becomes: spend thousands on halal compliance (in a business that may not even serve a majority Muslim clientele) or lose access to government contracts, events, and certain commercial spaces that require halal certification.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

JAKIM receives RM2.6 billion annually — more than the sports budget, more than environmental protection, and disproportionate to its actual contribution to GDP or social welfare. The expanding certification regime functions as a religious tax on business, with the costs ultimately passed to consumers. For a PM who champions a ‘moderate’ image internationally, the growth of JAKIM’s power and budget under his watch tells a different story domestically.

📜 Previous Cases

The KK Mart controversy in 2024 — where a convenience store chain was boycotted and faced criminal charges for inadvertently selling socks with an Allah-related design — showed how religious enforcement can destroy businesses over minor incidents. Under PH, religious agencies have not been reined in; they’ve been emboldened.

🎯 PH Callout

Your ‘Madani’ government spends RM2.6 billion on JAKIM while healthcare crumbles and schools produce students who can’t do math. Non-Muslim businesses subsidise a religious bureaucracy through mandatory compliance costs. If ‘Malaysia Madani’ means all Malaysians, why does budget allocation suggest it means only one community’s religious infrastructure?

Sources

  • CNA — Anwar Faces Graft Crisis
  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: Ringgit Weakness Persists Despite GDP Growth — Currency Doesn't Believe Anwar's Economic Story
item.description:
Said by: BNM / Currency Markets

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

Despite the government’s optimistic GDP growth narrative, the Malaysian ringgit has remained persistently weak against the US dollar and regional currencies, hovering around historically low levels. The currency markets — which aggregate the collective judgment of thousands of investors and analysts — are essentially voting no-confidence on Malaysia’s economic fundamentals.

🔥 Why It Matters

The weak ringgit has direct consequences for ordinary Malaysians: imported goods become more expensive (contributing to the food inflation that rakyat experience but official statistics undercount), overseas education costs more, and the brain drain accelerates as Malaysian professionals find their skills worth multiples more in stronger-currency countries.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government has pointed to global factors including US monetary policy and geopolitical uncertainty, and these are real factors. But regional comparisons reveal that the ringgit has underperformed several ASEAN peers, suggesting Malaysia-specific factors are at play: political uncertainty, the MACC scandal, concern about fiscal sustainability with 64.6% debt-to-GDP, and questions about the credibility of economic reforms. Currency markets are brutally honest barometers of national confidence. The ringgit’s weakness tells a story the government’s GDP figures don’t.

🎯 PH Callout

You can cherry-pick GDP numbers for press conferences. But you can’t spin the forex market. The ringgit doesn’t care about your talking points — it responds to fundamentals. And right now, the fundamentals say the market doesn’t believe your economic story. Fix the substance, and the currency will follow. Keep spinning, and it won’t.

Sources

  • Bloomberg — Anwar on Debt Pressure

item.title: [BM] Sindiket Rasuah Imigresen Subur — Eksploitasi Pekerja Asing Industri Berbilion Ringgit
item.description:
Said by: Enforcement Agencies / Media Investigations

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-03-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Sistem imigresen Malaysia kekal dibelenggu sindiket rasuah yang mengeksploitasi berjuta pekerja asing, mengekstrak berbilion ringgit melalui levi haram, penipuan dokumen, dan pemerdagangan buruh. Walaupun janji kerajaan berulang kali untuk membersihkan sistem, siasatan terus mendedahkan rangkaian yang berakar umbi melibatkan pegawai imigresen, ejen buruh, dan majikan.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Pekerja asing — dianggarkan 2-3 juta berdokumen dan berjuta lagi tanpa dokumen — membayar yuran pengambilan yang melampau, sering menjadi hamba terikat yang terperangkap dalam kitaran ikatan hutang. Sistem levi pekerja asing pelbagai peringkat telah berulang kali ditangguhkan, mewujudkan ketidaktentuan yang menguntungkan perantara rasuah. Laporan Pemerdagangan Orang Jabatan Negara AS telah secara konsisten menandakan isu buruh paksa Malaysia, terutamanya dalam pertanian perladangan, pembinaan, dan pembuatan.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Kitaran respons kerajaan boleh diramalkan: umumkan tindakan keras, lakukan beberapa serbuan berprofil tinggi, keluarkan kenyataan tegas, kemudian biarkan sistem kembali seperti biasa. Rasuah imigresen adalah masalah struktur yang memerlukan reformasi sistematik proses pengambilan, kutipan levi, dan perlindungan pekerja — bukan teater penguatkuasaan berkala.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda mewarisi sistem yang rosak dan berjanji untuk membetulkannya. Sebaliknya, sindiket masih beroperasi, pekerja masih menderita, dan berbilion masih mengalir kepada perantara rasuah. Jika anda tidak boleh membersihkan jabatan imigresen sendiri, berhenti berpura-pura anda kerajaan reformasi.

Sumber

  • ISEAS — Reformasi Subsidi Petrol Malaysia

item.title: Immigration Corruption Syndicates Thrive — Foreign Worker Exploitation a Multi-Billion Ringgit Industry
item.description:
Said by: Enforcement Agencies / Media Investigations

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-03-01

📌 What Happened

Malaysia’s immigration system remains plagued by corruption syndicates that exploit millions of foreign workers, extracting billions of ringgit through illegal levies, document fraud, and labour trafficking. Despite repeated government promises to clean up the system, investigations continue to reveal deeply entrenched networks involving immigration officials, labour agents, and employers.

🔥 Why It Matters

Foreign workers — numbering an estimated 2-3 million documented and millions more undocumented — pay exorbitant recruitment fees, often becoming indentured servants trapped in cycles of debt bondage. The multi-tier foreign worker levy system has been delayed repeatedly, creating uncertainty that benefits corrupt intermediaries. US State Department Trafficking in Persons reports have consistently flagged Malaysia’s forced labour issues, particularly in plantation agriculture, construction, and manufacturing.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government’s response cycle is predictable: announce a crackdown, conduct a few high-profile raids, issue stern statements, then allow the system to return to business as usual. Immigration corruption is a structural problem requiring systemic reform of recruitment processes, levy collection, and worker protection — not periodic enforcement theatre.

🎯 PH Callout

You inherited a broken system and promised to fix it. Instead, the syndicates still operate, the workers still suffer, and the billions still flow to corrupt intermediaries. If you can’t clean up your own immigration department, stop pretending you’re a reform government.

Sources

  • ISEAS — Malaysia’s Petrol Subsidy Reform

item.title: [BM] Proton Masih Bergantung Pada Perlindungan Kerajaan — 40 Tahun 'Kereta Nasional' Dan Masih Tak Boleh Bersaing
item.description:
Said by: Automotive Industry Analysts

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Selepas 40 tahun dan berbilion dalam subsidi awam, pengecualian cukai, dan dasar perdagangan perlindungan, Proton masih tidak boleh bersaing di peringkat antarabangsa tanpa perlindungan kerajaan. Duti import yang tinggi — duti eksais sehingga 100% ke atas kereta asing — melambungkan harga kenderaan untuk 33 juta rakyat Malaysia. Kereta yang sama berharga separuh di Thailand atau Indonesia. Kerajaan Anwar tidak menunjukkan kecenderungan untuk mereformasi dasar perdagangan automotif atau menghapuskan perlindungan yang merugikan pengguna Malaysia berbilion setiap tahun.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Pemilikan kereta bukan kemewahan di Malaysia — ia keperluan di negara dengan pengangkutan awam yang tidak mencukupi di luar KL. Duti import tinggi bermakna keluarga Malaysia membelanjakan bahagian pendapatan yang tidak seimbang untuk kenderaan. Honda City yang berharga RM65,000 di Thailand berharga lebih RM100,000 di Malaysia. Toyota Corolla di Indonesia berharga kira-kira RM80,000; di Malaysia, lebih RM130,000. Dasar ‘kereta nasional’ memaksa 33 juta rakyat mensubsidi ketidakupayaan satu syarikat untuk bersaing — melalui harga lebih tinggi pada setiap kenderaan yang mereka beli.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT: Proton sepatutnya menjadi industri bayi sementara — dilindungi beberapa tahun sehingga ia boleh berdiri sendiri. Empat puluh tahun kemudian, ia masih tak boleh berdiri sendiri. Syarikat ini dijual kepada Geely (China) pada 2017 kerana tidak boleh hidup tanpa rakan kongsi asing. Namun duti import kekal, melindungi syarikat yang bukan lagi sepenuhnya milik Malaysia. Perlindungan itu bukan lagi untuk juara nasional — ia melayani usaha sama dengan pembuat kereta China sementara pengguna Malaysia membayar harga melambung untuk setiap kereta di jalan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Projek kereta nasional telah menjadi lembu suci politik sejak Mahathir melancarkan Proton pada 1983. Setiap kerajaan mengekalkan perlindungan kerana lobi automotif — pengedar, pembekal, pekerja — mewakili undi. BN melindungi Proton selama 35 tahun. PH 1.0 tidak membuat perubahan. PH 2.0 tidak membuat perubahan. Dasar ini bertahan bukan kerana ia berkesan tetapi kerana membongkarnya akan menimbulkan kemarahan pihak berkepentingan. Sementara itu, Thailand dan Indonesia — tanpa perlindungan kereta nasional — telah menjadi hab pembuatan automotif utama. Malaysia tidak.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Keluarga Malaysia membayar dua kali ganda untuk kereta kerana dasar berusia 40 tahun yang melindungi syarikat yang kini separuh dimiliki China. Pengangkutan awam tidak mencukupi. Pemilikan kereta wajib. Dan kamu mengekalkan duti import yang melambungkan harga untuk semua orang. Hapuskan perlindungan automotif secara berperingkat dalam 5-10 tahun. Laburkan penjimatan dalam pengangkutan awam. Biarkan pengguna Malaysia membeli kereta pada harga serantau. Atau terus memaksa 33 juta rakyat mensubsidi dasar perindustrian yang gagal — dan tertanya-tanya kenapa kos sara hidup menjadi masalah politik terbesar kamu.

Sumber: Paultan.org | Nikkei Asia | The Edge

item.title: Proton Still Dependent on Government Protections — 40 Years of 'National Car' and Still Can't Compete
item.description:
Said by: Automotive Industry Analysts

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: After 40 years and billions in public subsidies, tax exemptions, and protectionist trade policies, Proton still cannot compete internationally without government protection. High import duties — excise duties of up to 100% on foreign cars — inflate vehicle prices for 33 million Malaysians. The same car costs half as much in Thailand or Indonesia. Anwar’s government shows no inclination to reform automotive trade policies or phase out protections that cost Malaysian consumers billions annually.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Car ownership is not a luxury in Malaysia — it is a necessity in a country with inadequate public transport outside KL. High import duties mean Malaysian families spend a disproportionate share of their income on vehicles. A Honda City that costs RM65,000 in Thailand costs over RM100,000 in Malaysia. A Toyota Corolla in Indonesia costs about RM80,000; in Malaysia, over RM130,000. The “national car” policy forces 33 million people to subsidise one company’s inability to compete — through higher prices on every vehicle they buy.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Proton was supposed to be a temporary infant industry — protected for a few years until it could stand on its own. Forty years later, it still can’t stand on its own. The company was sold to Geely (China) in 2017 because it couldn’t survive without a foreign partner. Yet import duties remain, protecting a company that is no longer even fully Malaysian-owned. The protection no longer serves a national champion — it serves a joint venture with a Chinese automaker while Malaysian consumers pay inflated prices for every car on the road.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The national car project has been a political sacred cow since Mahathir launched Proton in 1983. Every government has maintained protections because the automotive lobby — dealers, suppliers, workers — represents votes. BN protected Proton for 35 years. PH 1.0 made no changes. PH 2.0 has made no changes. The policy survives not because it works but because dismantling it would anger connected interests. Meanwhile, Thailand and Indonesia — without national car protections — have become major automotive manufacturing hubs. Malaysia has not.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Malaysian families pay double for cars because of a 40-year-old policy that protects a company now half-owned by China. Public transport is inadequate. Car ownership is mandatory. And you maintain import duties that inflate prices for everyone. Phase out automotive protections over 5-10 years. Invest the savings in public transport. Let Malaysian consumers buy cars at regional prices. Or keep forcing 33 million people to subsidise a failed industrial policy — and wonder why cost of living is your biggest political problem.

Sources: Paultan.org | Nikkei Asia | The Edge

item.title: [BM] Nga Kor Ming Belanja RM4 Juta Untuk 4 'Tandas Pintar Kelas BMW' Sementara Rakyat Berjuang Dengan Kos Sara Hidup
item.description:
Said by: Nga Kor Ming, Housing and Local Government Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-11-14

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada November 2024, Menteri Perumahan dan Kerajaan Tempatan Nga Kor Ming mengumumkan bahawa RM4 juta telah diperuntukkan untuk membina empat tandas pintar “kelas BMW” di Kuala Lumpur dan Putrajaya — RM1 juta setiap tandas. Beliau membuat pengumuman itu di majlis Anugerah Tandas Terbaik Tahun (TOTYA) 2024, menggambarkannya sebagai sebahagian daripada peruntukan RM180 juta yang lebih luas selama tiga tahun untuk naik taraf tandas awam. Menjelang Jun 2025, bekas naib presiden MCA Ti Lian Ker menuntut kemas kini, menyatakan tiada laporan kemajuan sejak pengumuman.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: RM1 juta setiap tandas. Pendapatan purata isi rumah Malaysia adalah sekitar RM7,000-8,000 sebulan. Kerajaan membelanjakan setara lebih 10 tahun pendapatan sebuah isi rumah untuk satu tandas, sambil memberitahu rakyat supaya mengetatkan tali pinggang. Peruntukan RM180 juta naik taraf tandas kebangsaan adalah penggunaan dana yang sah. Tetapi menjadikan empat tandas pameran “kelas BMW” pada harga RM1 juta setiap satu — di KL dan Putrajaya, tempat yang sudah mempunyai infrastruktur terbaik — adalah jenis perbelanjaan pekak nada yang membuatkan rakyat hilang kepercayaan terhadap kerajaan reformasi.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Nga Kor Ming adalah menteri yang sama yang mengancam wartawan kerana bertanya tentang penamaan acara, dan yang program MyKiosknya gagal semakan SPRM kerana tiada penilaian keperluan. “Tandas kelas BMW” — penjenamaan sahaja memberitahu anda ini bukan tentang melayani rakyat, ia tentang membuatkan menteri kelihatan baik. Dan apabila ditanya untuk kemas kini beberapa bulan kemudian? Senyap. Tiada laporan kemajuan. Tiada akauntabiliti. Hanya pengumuman sia-sia diikuti lubang hitam pelaksanaan. Sementara itu, tandas awam biasa di seluruh Malaysia kekal kotor dan rosak. Tetapi sudah tentu, bina tandas mewah di kawasan yang sudah mempunyai infrastruktur terbaik.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang perolehan kerajaan yang melambung. Tempat duduk tandas RM600 dan pemutar skru RM1,200 era BN adalah cerita rasuah yang menjadi bualan harian. PH menjerit tentang pembaziran selama berdekad. Kini mereka membelanjakan RM1 juta setiap tandas dan memanggilnya inovasi. Nga sendiri mempunyai corak pengumuman besar-besaran dengan susulan yang lemah — program MyKiosk menjadi contoh terbaru SPRM memberi bendera tentang kegagalan tadbir urus projek kementeriannya.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda menjanjikan disiplin fiskal dan tadbir urus bertanggungjawab. Anda memberitahu rakyat Malaysia setiap ringgit akan dibelanjakan dengan bijak di bawah Kerajaan Madani. Kemudian anda belanja RM1 juta setiap tandas dan tak boleh beri laporan kemajuan bila ditanya. Rakyat tak mampu beli telur, tetapi menteri anda membina tandas BMW. Jika ini reformasi, rakyat mahu bayaran balik.

Sumber: FMT | NST | Bernama

item.title: Nga Kor Ming Blows RM4 Million on 4 'BMW-Class Smart Toilets' While Rakyat Struggles With Cost of Living
item.description:
Said by: Nga Kor Ming, Housing and Local Government Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-11-14

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In November 2024, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming announced that RM4 million had been allocated to build four “BMW-class” smart toilets in Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya — RM1 million per toilet. He made the announcement at the Toilet of the Year Award (TOTYA) 2024 ceremony, describing it as part of a broader RM180 million allocation over three years for public toilet upgrades. By June 2025, former MCA vice-president Ti Lian Ker demanded an update, noting zero progress reports since the announcement.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: RM1 million per toilet. The average Malaysian household income is around RM7,000-8,000 per month. The government is spending the equivalent of over 10 years of a household’s income on a single toilet, while telling the rakyat to tighten their belts. The RM180 million national toilet upgrade is a legitimate use of funds. But turning four toilets into “BMW-class” showpieces at RM1 million each — in KL and Putrajaya, the places that already have the best infrastructure — is the kind of tone-deaf spending that makes people lose faith in reform governments.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Nga Kor Ming is the same minister who threatened a journalist for asking about event naming, and whose MyKiosk programme failed MACC review for lacking a needs assessment. “BMW-class” toilets — the branding alone tells you this isn’t about serving the public, it’s about making the minister look good. And when asked for an update months later? Silence. No progress report. No accountability. Just a vanity announcement followed by a black hole of implementation. Meanwhile, regular public toilets across Malaysia remain filthy and broken. But sure, build luxury toilets in areas that already have the best infrastructure.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a long history of inflated government procurement. The RM600 toilet seats and RM1,200 screwdrivers of the BN era were bread-and-butter corruption stories. PH screamed about wasteful spending for decades. Now they’re spending RM1 million per toilet and calling it innovation. Nga himself has a pattern of grand announcements with poor follow-through — the MyKiosk programme being the most recent example of MACC flagging his ministry’s projects for governance failures.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You promised fiscal discipline and responsible governance. You told Malaysians every ringgit would be spent wisely under Kerajaan Madani. Then you spend RM1 million per toilet and can’t provide a progress report when asked. The rakyat can’t afford eggs, but your minister is building BMW toilets. If this is reform, the rakyat would like a refund.

Sources: FMT | NST | Bernama

item.title: [BM] Kekurangan Beras Timbulkan Kebimbangan Sekuriti Makanan — Anwar Mahu 'Lebih Pemantauan'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-11-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada penghujung 2024, kekurangan beras secara sporadik melanda Malaysia bersama banjir yang dahsyat. Rak pasar raya kehabisan jenis beras tempatan di beberapa negeri. Respons Anwar ialah menyeru “pemantauan dan penguatkuasaan yang kerap” — tindak balas lazim kerajaan apabila tiada pelan. Malaysia mengimport lebih 30% bekalan berasnya, dan industri padi domestik telah diabaikan selama berdekad. Harga beras global mencecah paras tertinggi bertahun-tahun akibat sekatan eksport India, kesan El Niño terhadap hasil tuaian Asia Tenggara, dan gangguan rantaian bekalan.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Beras adalah makanan ruji 33 juta rakyat Malaysia. Apabila rak beras kosong, sekuriti makanan bukan teori — ia segera. Kebergantungan import Malaysia melebihi 30% bermakna sebarang gangguan bekalan global secara langsung mengancam sekuriti makanan domestik. Industri padi telah kurang dibiayai, berpecah-pecah, dan diurus secara politik selama berdekad. Petani kecil menerima sokongan yang tidak mencukupi. Infrastruktur pengairan runtuh. Hasil per hektar ketinggalan di belakang Thailand, Vietnam, dan Indonesia. Kekurangan beras boleh dijangka — dan kerajaan tidak bersedia.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: “Pemantauan dan penguatkuasaan” adalah apa yang anda lakukan apabila tiada pelan. Ia setara birokrasi dengan memerhati masalah menjadi lebih teruk sambil kelihatan sibuk. PH menjanjikan reformasi sekuriti makanan. Tiga tahun kemudian, kebergantungan import beras Malaysia tidak berkurangan, pengeluaran domestik tidak meningkat secara bermakna, dan respons kerajaan terhadap kekurangan ialah penguatkuasaan — menghukum peniaga kerana menyorok dan bukannya menangani defisit bekalan struktur. Anda tidak boleh menguatkuasakan beras menjadi wujud. Anda perlu menanamnya, dan itu memerlukan pelaburan yang kerajaan tidak buat.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia menghadapi kekurangan beras secara berkala di bawah setiap kerajaan. Di bawah BN, responsnya sentiasa sama: lepaskan rizab strategik, salahkan penyorok, janjikan pelaburan dalam padi. Di bawah PH 1.0, sekuriti makanan sebentar dinaikkan sebagai keutamaan dasar sebelum Langkah Sheraton. Di bawah PH 2.0, kerajaan melancarkan inisiatif “sekuriti makanan” yang menghasilkan sidang akhbar tetapi tiada peningkatan yang boleh diukur dalam nisbah sara diri. Masalah struktur — penurunan keluasan padi, petani yang semakin tua, pengairan yang tidak mencukupi, dan kebergantungan pada import — kekal sama seperti 20 tahun lalu.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Rak beras kosong. Respons anda “pemantauan.” Malaysia mengimport lebih 30% berasnya dan anda tidak berbuat apa-apa untuk mengurangkan kebergantungan itu dalam tiga tahun. Laburkan dalam infrastruktur padi — pengairan, mekanisasi, peningkatan hasil. Sokong petani kecil dengan harga minimum terjamin. Bina rizab strategik yang mampu menyerap kejutan bekalan global. Atau teruskan “memantau” sementara rakyat Malaysia tak jumpa beras di pasar raya — dan harap mereka tak perasan bahawa “pemantauan” bukan sekuriti makanan.

Sumber: Bernama | FMT | Reuters

item.title: Rice Shortages Deepen Food Security Fears — Anwar Wants 'More Monitoring'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late 2024, sporadic rice shortages hit Malaysia alongside devastating floods. Supermarket shelves emptied of local rice varieties in multiple states. Anwar’s response was to call for “frequent monitoring and enforcement” — the government’s default when it has no plan. Malaysia imports over 30% of its rice supply, and the domestic padi industry has been neglected for decades. Global rice prices hit multi-year highs due to India’s export restrictions, El Niño impacts on Southeast Asian harvests, and supply chain disruptions.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Rice is the staple food for 33 million Malaysians. When rice shelves are empty, food security is not theoretical — it is immediate. Malaysia’s 30%+ import dependency means any global supply disruption directly threatens domestic food security. The padi industry has been underfunded, fragmented, and politically managed for decades. Smallholder farmers receive insufficient support. Irrigation infrastructure is crumbling. Yields per hectare lag behind Thailand, Vietnam, and Indonesia. The rice shortage was predictable — and the government was not prepared.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: “Monitoring and enforcement” is what you do when you have no plan. It is the bureaucratic equivalent of watching the problem get worse while looking busy. PH promised food security reform. Three years later, Malaysia’s rice import dependency hasn’t decreased, domestic production hasn’t meaningfully increased, and the government’s response to shortages is enforcement — punishing traders for hoarding rather than addressing the structural supply deficit. You can’t enforce rice into existence. You need to grow it, and that requires investment the government hasn’t made.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has faced periodic rice shortages under every government. Under BN, the response was always the same: release strategic reserves, blame hoarders, promise investment in padi. Under PH 1.0, food security was briefly elevated as a policy priority before the Sheraton Move. Under PH 2.0, the government launched a “food security” initiative that generated press conferences but no measurable improvement in self-sufficiency ratios. The structural problem — declining padi acreage, aging farmers, insufficient irrigation, and reliance on imports — remains identical to what it was 20 years ago.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Rice shelves are empty. Your response is “monitoring.” Malaysia imports 30%+ of its rice and you’ve done nothing to reduce that dependency in three years. Invest in padi infrastructure — irrigation, mechanisation, yield improvement. Support smallholder farmers with guaranteed minimum prices. Build strategic reserves that can absorb global supply shocks. Or keep “monitoring” while Malaysians can’t find rice at the supermarket — and hope they don’t notice that “monitoring” is not food security.

Sources: Bernama | FMT | Reuters

item.title: [BM] Sistem Levi Pekerja Asing Pelbagai Peringkat — Diumumkan 2023, Masih Belum Dilaksanakan Sepenuhnya
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-10-18

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada September 2023, Anwar mengumumkan sistem levi pelbagai peringkat untuk pekerja asing yang direka bagi mengurangkan pergantungan Malaysia terhadap tenaga kerja import. Sistem ini diulang dalam Belanjawan 2025 dengan butiran lanjut. Persekutuan Majikan-Majikan Malaysia (MEF) mengadu sistem itu masih tidak adil dan pelaksanaannya tidak mencukupi. Janji Anwar untuk memotong pergantungan pekerja asing kepada 15% tenaga kerja menjelang 2025 tidak dipenuhi. Sehingga akhir 2025, Malaysia masih menggaji kira-kira 2.2 juta pekerja asing berdokumen, dengan anggaran 1-2 juta tidak berdokumen — pergantungan yang tidak berubah secara bermakna di bawah PH.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Pergantungan pekerja asing menekan gaji tempatan, terutamanya bagi rakyat Malaysia B40 yang bersaing secara langsung dengan tenaga kerja import dalam pembinaan, pertanian, pembuatan dan perkhidmatan. Levi pelbagai peringkat sepatutnya menjadikannya semakin mahal bagi majikan untuk mengambil pekerja asing, memberi insentif kepada automasi, peningkatan produktiviti dan pengambilan tempatan. Dua tahun selepas pengumuman, sistem itu masih belum dilaksanakan sepenuhnya. Sementara itu, industri terus mengimport pekerja pada tahap yang melemahkan lantai gaji untuk warganegara Malaysia.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Mengumumkan dasar dan melaksanakannya adalah dua perkara berbeza — dan PH mahir dalam yang pertama sambil gagal dalam yang kedua. Levi pelbagai peringkat diumumkan pada 2023, diulang pada 2024, dan dimasukkan dalam Belanjawan 2025. Pada setiap peringkat, ia dibentangkan sebagai inisiatif baru. Sasaran 15% secara senyap digugurkan apabila jelas ia tidak akan dicapai. Aduan MEF mengesyorkan bahawa bahagian yang dilaksanakan pun direka dengan buruk. Lobi pekerja asing — mewakili syarikat perladangan, firma pembinaan dan pengilang dengan hubungan politik — telah berjaya menangguhkan reformasi bermakna sementara pengumuman diteruskan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Setiap kerajaan Malaysia telah berjanji mengurangkan pergantungan pekerja asing. Di bawah Mahathir 1.0, janji itu dibuat pada 1990-an. Di bawah Najib, pelbagai kenaikan levi diumumkan. Di bawah PH 1.0, pembekuan permit pekerja asing baru dilaksanakan secara ringkas sebelum ditarik balik. Di bawah PH 2.0, levi pelbagai peringkat diumumkan dengan penuh gembar-gembur tetapi pelaksanaan sangat perlahan. Coraknya konsisten: umumkan reformasi, hadapi tentangan industri, tangguhkan pelaksanaan, umumkan semula dalam belanjawan seterusnya. Sementara itu, 3-4 juta pekerja asing kekal di negara ini.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda mengumumkan levi pelbagai peringkat pada 2023. Sekarang 2026 dan ia masih belum dilaksanakan sepenuhnya. Sasaran 15% anda tidak tercapai. MEF kata sistem itu tidak adil. Industri terus mengimport pekerja pada kadar yang sama. Sama ada laksanakan levi dengan ketegasan — penguatkuasaan sebenar, kenaikan kos bermakna untuk majikan yang terlalu bergantung kepada tenaga kerja asing — atau akui bahawa pengumuman itu untuk tajuk berita, bukan untuk dasar. Pekerja Malaysia layak mendapat perlindungan gaji tempatan. Mereka tidak mendapatnya kerana kerajaan anda lebih takut mengganggu lobi perladangan daripada melindungi rakyat.

Sumber: Bernama | FMT | MEF

item.title: Foreign Worker Levy Multi-Tier System — Announced 2023, Still Not Fully Implemented
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-10-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In September 2023, Anwar announced a multi-tier levy system for foreign workers designed to reduce Malaysia’s dependency on imported labour. The system was repeated in Budget 2025 with further details. The Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF) complained the system was still unfair and insufficiently implemented. Anwar’s promise to cut foreign worker dependency to 15% of the workforce by 2025 was not met. As of late 2025, Malaysia still employed approximately 2.2 million documented foreign workers, with an estimated 1-2 million undocumented — a dependency that has not meaningfully changed under PH.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Foreign worker dependency suppresses local wages, particularly for B40 Malaysians who compete directly with imported labour in construction, agriculture, manufacturing, and services. The multi-tier levy was supposed to make it progressively more expensive for employers to hire foreign workers, incentivising automation, productivity improvements, and local hiring. Two years after announcement, the system is still not fully implemented. Meanwhile, industries continue importing workers at levels that undercut the wage floor for Malaysian citizens.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Announcing a policy and implementing it are two different things — and PH excels at the former while failing at the latter. The multi-tier levy was announced in 2023, repeated in 2024, and included in Budget 2025. At each stage, it was presented as a new initiative. The 15% target was quietly dropped when it became clear it wouldn’t be met. MEF’s complaints suggest that even the implemented portions are poorly designed. The foreign worker lobby — representing plantation companies, construction firms, and manufacturers with political connections — has successfully delayed meaningful reform while the announcements continue.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every Malaysian government has promised to reduce foreign worker dependency. Under Mahathir 1.0, the promise was made in the 1990s. Under Najib, various levy increases were announced. Under PH 1.0, a freeze on new foreign worker permits was briefly implemented before being reversed. Under PH 2.0, the multi-tier levy was announced with fanfare but implementation has been glacial. The pattern is consistent: announce reform, face industry pushback, delay implementation, announce it again in the next budget. Meanwhile, 3-4 million foreign workers remain in the country.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You announced the multi-tier levy in 2023. It’s 2026 and it’s still not fully implemented. Your 15% target was missed. MEF says the system is unfair. Industries keep importing workers at the same rate. Either implement the levy with teeth — real enforcement, meaningful cost increases for employers who over-rely on foreign labour — or admit that the announcement was for headlines, not for policy. Malaysian workers deserve local wage protection. They’re not getting it because your government is more afraid of upsetting the plantation lobby than protecting the rakyat.

Sources: Bernama | FMT | MEF

item.title: [BM] Zon Kewangan Khas Forest City — Cukai 0% Untuk Golongan Kaya Raya Sementara Rakyat Bayar SST 8%
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Securities Commission

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-10-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Oktober 2024, kerajaan PH melancarkan Zon Kewangan Khas (ZKK) Forest City di Johor, menawarkan cukai korporat 0% sehingga 15 tahun untuk perniagaan yang layak, cukai 0% untuk pejabat keluarga bernilai tinggi, dan insentif untuk syarikat fintech, pengurusan dana dan aset digital. ZKK direka untuk menyelamatkan Forest City — projek hartanah bernilai RM400 bilion yang disokong China yang kebanyakannya kosong — dengan mengubahnya menjadi hab kewangan. Sementara itu, rakyat Malaysia biasa membayar SST 8% ke atas perkhidmatan harian termasuk penjagaan kesihatan, pendidikan dan sewa.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: ZKK mewujudkan sistem cukai dua peringkat: 0% untuk golongan kaya raya yang meletak kekayaan mereka di Forest City, dan SST 8% untuk rakyat Malaysia biasa yang membeli barangan runcit, berjumpa doktor, atau menghantar anak-anak ke sekolah. Optiknya merosakkan — kerajaan pada masa yang sama mengembangkan SST untuk meliputi keperluan asas sambil menawarkan status bebas cukai kepada pejabat keluarga bilionair. Ini bukan tarikan pelaburan — ini adalah tempat perlindungan kekayaan yang dijenamakan sebagai pembangunan ekonomi.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Forest City adalah projek hartanah yang gagal. Berbilion dolar pelaburan China menghasilkan bandar hantu yang Mahathir sendiri ejek. Daripada membiarkan pasaran menilai kegagalan itu, kerajaan Anwar menggunakan dasar awam — pengecualian cukai, insentif pengawalseliaan, perbelanjaan infrastruktur — untuk menyelamatkan pembangunan swasta. Penerima manfaat adalah pemaju hartanah dan pelabur kaya raya. Kos ditanggung oleh pembayar cukai Malaysia yang kehilangan hasil yang perniagaan dikecualikan cukai akan sumbangkan. Menyosialisasikan kerugian dan menswastakan keuntungan adalah definisi kapitalisme kroni — dan PH berjanji untuk menamatkannya.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang zon ekonomi khas yang menjanjikan transformasi dan menyampaikan pemberian cukai. Wilayah Pembangunan Iskandar menawarkan insentif cukai selama lebih sedekad — ketidaksamaan Johor bertambah buruk. Pusat kewangan luar pesisir Labuan menarik syarikat cangkerang lebih daripada pelaburan tulen. Cyberjaya sepatutnya menjadi Silicon Valley Malaysia — ia menjadi taman pejabat. Setiap zon mengikut corak yang sama: insentif cukai yang murah hati, mekanisme pemulangan minimum, dan manfaat yang terakru kepada modal bukan buruh. ZKK Forest City menambah bab lain kepada corak ini.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda menawarkan cukai 0% kepada pejabat keluarga bilionair sambil mengenakan SST 8% ke atas penjagaan kesihatan dan pendidikan rakyat. Anda menyelamatkan projek hartanah China yang gagal dengan insentif dasar awam. Anda menyosialisasikan kerugian dan menswastakan keuntungan — tepat seperti yang anda tuduh BN lakukan. Terbitkan analisis kos-manfaat ZKK. Tunjukkan berapa banyak pekerjaan Malaysia yang akan diwujudkan berbanding berapa banyak hasil cukai Malaysia hilang. Jelaskan mengapa pejabat keluarga kaya raya layak mendapat cukai 0% sedangkan peniaga kecil membayar SST. Atau akui bahawa ZKK adalah penyelamatan untuk pemaju, bukan strategi ekonomi untuk Malaysia.

Sumber: Nikkei Asia | Reuters | Straits Times

item.title: Forest City Special Financial Zone — 0% Tax for the Ultra-Rich While Rakyat Pays 8% SST
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Securities Commission

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-10-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In October 2024, the PH government launched the Forest City Special Financial Zone (SFZ) in Johor, offering 0% corporate tax for up to 15 years for qualifying businesses, 0% tax for ultra-high-net-worth family offices, and incentives for fintech, fund management, and digital asset companies. The SFZ was designed to rescue Forest City — a RM400 billion Chinese-backed property development that sat largely empty — by transforming it into a financial hub. Meanwhile, ordinary Malaysians pay 8% SST on everyday services including healthcare, education, and rent.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The SFZ creates a two-tier tax system: 0% for the ultra-rich who park their wealth in Forest City, and 8% SST for ordinary Malaysians buying groceries, seeing a doctor, or sending their children to school. The optics are devastating — the government is simultaneously expanding SST to cover basic necessities while offering tax-free status to billionaires’ family offices. This is not investment attraction — it is a wealth shelter branded as economic development.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Forest City was a failed property project. Billions of dollars in Chinese investment produced a ghost city that Mahathir himself mocked. Instead of letting the market price in the failure, the Anwar government is using public policy — tax exemptions, regulatory incentives, infrastructure spending — to rescue a private development. The beneficiaries are property developers and ultra-wealthy investors. The cost is borne by Malaysian taxpayers who lose the revenue that tax-exempt businesses would otherwise contribute. Socialising losses and privatising gains is the definition of crony capitalism — and PH promised to end it.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a long history of special economic zones that promised transformation and delivered tax giveaways. The Iskandar Development Region offered tax incentives for over a decade — Johor’s inequality worsened. Labuan’s offshore financial centre attracted shell companies more than genuine investment. Cyberjaya was supposed to be Malaysia’s Silicon Valley — it became an office park. Each zone follows the same pattern: generous tax incentives, minimal clawback mechanisms, and benefits that accrue to capital rather than labour. Forest City SFZ adds another chapter to this pattern.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You’re offering 0% tax to billionaires’ family offices while charging 8% SST on the rakyat’s healthcare and education. You’re rescuing a failed Chinese property project with public policy incentives. You’re socialising the losses and privatising the gains — exactly what you accused BN of doing. Publish a cost-benefit analysis of the SFZ. Show how many Malaysian jobs it will create versus how much tax revenue Malaysia loses. Explain why ultra-wealthy family offices deserve 0% tax when hawkers pay SST. Or admit that the SFZ is a bailout for developers, not an economic strategy for Malaysia.

Sources: Nikkei Asia | Reuters | Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Harga Diesel Melompat 56% Semalaman — Anwar Kata Pengkritik Akan Serang Beliau Walau Apa Pun
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-06-10

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 10 Jun 2024, harga diesel di Semenanjung Malaysia melompat daripada RM2.15 kepada RM3.35 seliter — kenaikan 56% semalaman. Menteri Kewangan II Amir Hamzah Azizan mengumumkan pengapungan harga pada 9 Jun, berkuat kuasa keesokan harinya — satu hari notis untuk kejutan yang merambat melalui logistik, harga makanan, dan perniagaan kecil. Sabah, Sarawak, dan Labuan kekal pada RM2.15. Anwar memberitahu wartawan: “Kita juga perlu tahu bahawa apa sahaja yang kita buat, kita akan dikritik hebat dengan segala jenis fitnah dan pembohongan.” Tindak balasnya terhadap rakyat yang menghadapi kenaikan kos 56% adalah mengadu tentang kualiti kritikan yang diterimanya.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Bil subsidi diesel telah membengkak kepada RM14.3 bilion setahun, dan kebanyakan ahli ekonomi bersetuju subsidi menyeluruh itu regresif. Dasar ini mungkin perlu dari segi ekonomi, tetapi pelaksanaannya cuai dari segi politik. Kenaikan harga 56% dengan satu hari notis adalah kejutan kepada isi rumah, perniagaan kecil, nelayan, dan petani yang tidak boleh menyerap kenaikan kos semalaman. Pelaksanaan berperingkat selama 6-12 bulan akan mencapai matlamat fiskal yang sama dengan sebahagian kecil kesakitan.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Penolakan Anwar terhadap semua kritikan sebagai “fitnah dan pembohongan” bukan sahaja pekak nada — ia menghina. Ramai mereka yang bergelut dengan harga diesel yang lebih tinggi adalah pengundi PH. Mereka tidak memfitnah PM — mereka memberitahunya mereka tidak mampu mengisi lori mereka. Sistem harga dua peringkat mewujudkan masalah arbitraj segera — penyeludupan diesel dari Sabah dan Sarawak meningkat, menambah kos penguatkuasaan. Kerajaan menjanjikan penjimatan RM14.3 bilion akan membiayai pengangkutan awam dan bantuan bersasar. Tetapi PADU diketepi. Perluasan pengangkutan awam di luar KL kekal tidak ketara.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Apabila Najib cuba reformasi subsidi bahan api pada 2014, PH adalah antara pengkritik paling kuat. Apabila harga petrol turun naik di bawah sistem apungan terurus, PH menuduh BN membebankan rakyat. Kini berkuasa, PH melaksanakan potongan subsidi yang lebih agresif — 56% semalaman berbanding pengapungan beransur Najib — dan memanggil pengkritik sebagai pemfitnah.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda menaikkan diesel 56% semalaman dengan satu hari notis. Tindak balas anda terhadap kritikan adalah memanggilnya fitnah. Pelaburan semula yang dijanjikan — pengangkutan awam, bantuan bersasar — tidak terwujud. PADU diketepi. Bas tidak muncul. Jika rakyat mesti mengetatkan tali pinggang, tunjukkan anda mengetatkan tali pinggang anda dahulu. Terbitkan ke mana penjimatan RM14.3 bilion pergi. Sampaikan penambahbaikan pengangkutan awam yang anda janjikan. Laksanakan reformasi subsidi masa depan secara beransur. Atau terus mengenakan kejutan semalaman dan menolak kesakitan sebagai fitnah.

Sumber: Reuters | UCA News | The Diplomat

item.title: [BM] Harga Diesel Melompat 56% Semalaman Ketika PH Mansuh Subsidi — Rakyat Disuruh 'Ketatkan Tali Pinggang'
item.description:
Said by: Amir Hamzah Azizan, Finance Minister II; Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-06-10

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 10 Jun 2024, kerajaan PH memansuhkan subsidi diesel di Semenanjung Malaysia, menyebabkan harga melompat daripada RM2.15 kepada RM3.35 seliter semalaman — kenaikan 56%. Menteri Kewangan II Amir Hamzah Azizan membingkaikannya sebagai “rasionalisasi subsidi bersasar” dan mengumumkan Sistem Kawalan Diesel Bersubsidi untuk sektor yang layak. Kesannya serta-merta: kos pengangkutan melonjak, harga makanan naik, dan perniagaan memindahkan kos kepada pengguna. Kerajaan menjanjikan isi rumah B40 dan M40 akan dilindungi melalui bantuan tunai BUDI MADANI, tetapi bayaran dikritik secara meluas sebagai tidak mencukupi.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Reformasi subsidi perlu — Malaysia membelanjakan RM14.3 bilion untuk subsidi diesel pada 2023, kebanyakannya bocor kepada penyeludupan dan kenderaan asing. Kes fiskal kukuh. Tetapi pelaksanaan itu kejam. Kenaikan harga 56% semalaman memukul rakyat Malaysia yang bekerja dengan teruk, terutamanya dalam sektor yang bergantung kepada logistik. Penyelidikan menunjukkan perniagaan merentasi pelbagai industri menghadapi tekanan kos serta-merta, memindahkan kos terus kepada pengguna — tepat akibat yang PH janjikan untuk elakkan.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: PH menghabiskan bertahun-tahun menyerang dasar subsidi BN — kedua-duanya kerana terlalu murah hati (pembaziran fiskal) dan untuk sebarang percubaan mereformasinya (membebankan rakyat). Klasik pembangkang nak semua sekali. Kini berkuasa, PH memansuhkan subsidi diesel dengan kenaikan 56% semalaman dan memberitahu rakyat Malaysia ia untuk kebaikan mereka. Bantuan tunai BUDI MADANI sederhana berbanding kenaikan kos berturutan. Penjimatan sepatutnya membiayai pengangkutan awam dan pemindahan bersasar. Menjelang 2026, pengangkutan awam di luar KL kekal minimum, dan PADU — mekanisme penyasaran — telah diketepi. Wang telah dijimatkan. Pelaburan semula tidak disampaikan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Apabila Najib cuba reformasi subsidi bahan api pada 2014, PH adalah antara pengkritik paling kuat. Corak yang lebih luas konsisten: PH menjanjikan dasar populis dalam pembangkang, kemudian melaksanakan langkah ekonomi yang sama keras dalam kerajaan yang mereka serang pendahulu mereka kerananya.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Reformasi subsidi perlu. Tiada siapa mempertikaikan itu. Tetapi anda boleh melakukannya secara beransur-ansur, telus, dan dengan kusyen yang mencukupi. Sebaliknya, anda melakukannya dengan cepat dan keras, sementara menteri anda masih menikmati kad bahan api bersubsidi. Terbitkan ke mana penjimatan tahunan RM14.3 bilion pergi. Sampaikan penambahbaikan pengangkutan awam yang anda janjikan. Jika rakyat mesti mengetatkan tali pinggang, tunjukkan anda mengetatkan tali pinggang anda dahulu.

Sumber: MOF Malaysia | Fulcrum | LSE | AMRO

item.title: Diesel Price Jumps 56% Overnight — Anwar Says Critics Will Attack Him Regardless
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-06-10

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 10 June 2024, diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia jumped from RM2.15 to RM3.35 per litre — a 56% increase overnight. Finance Minister II Amir Hamzah Azizan announced the price float on 9 June, effective the next morning — one day’s notice for a shock that rippled through logistics, food prices, and small businesses. Sabah, Sarawak, and Labuan remained at RM2.15. Anwar told reporters: “We must also know that whatever we do, we will be severely criticised with all sorts of slander and lies.” His response to citizens facing a 56% cost increase was to complain about the quality of criticism he was receiving.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The diesel subsidy bill had ballooned to RM14.3 billion annually, and most economists agreed the blanket subsidy was regressive. The policy may be economically necessary, but the execution was politically negligent. A 56% price hike with one day’s notice is a shock to households, small businesses, fishermen, and farmers who cannot absorb overnight cost increases. Phased implementation over 6-12 months would have achieved the same fiscal goal with a fraction of the pain. The Diplomat noted that diesel increases have cascading effects — logistics, food transport, construction, agriculture, and fishing all depend on diesel.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar’s dismissal of all criticism as “slander and lies” is not just tone-deaf — it is contemptuous. Many of the people struggling with higher diesel costs are PH voters. They are not slandering the PM — they are telling him they cannot afford to fill their trucks. The two-tier pricing system created an immediate arbitrage problem — diesel smuggling from Sabah and Sarawak increased, adding enforcement costs. The government promised RM14.3 billion in savings would fund public transport and targeted aid. But PADU was sidelined. Public transport expansion outside KL remains negligible. The money was saved, but the promised reinvestment is largely invisible to the people who sacrificed.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: When Najib attempted fuel subsidy reform in 2014, PH was among the loudest critics. When petrol prices fluctuated under the managed float system, PH accused BN of burdening the people. Now in government, PH implemented an even more aggressive subsidy cut — 56% overnight versus Najib’s gradual float — and called critics slanderers. The hypocrisy of implementing tougher measures than those you attacked your predecessors for is the defining feature of PH’s economic governance.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You raised diesel by 56% overnight with one day’s notice. Your response to criticism was to call it slander. Your promised reinvestment — public transport, targeted aid — hasn’t materialised. PADU was sidelined. Buses didn’t appear. If the rakyat must tighten their belts, show them you’re tightening yours first. Publish where the RM14.3 billion in savings went. Deliver the public transport improvements you promised. Phase future subsidy reforms gradually. Or keep imposing overnight shocks and dismissing the pain as slander.

Sources: Reuters | UCA News | The Diplomat

item.title: Diesel Price Jumps 56% Overnight as PH Removes Subsidies — Rakyat Told to 'Tighten Belts'
item.description:
Said by: Amir Hamzah Azizan, Finance Minister II; Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-06-10

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 10 June 2024, the PH government removed diesel subsidies in Peninsular Malaysia, causing the price to jump from RM2.15 to RM3.35 per litre overnight — a 56% increase. Finance Minister II Amir Hamzah Azizan framed it as “targeted subsidy rationalisation” and announced a Subsidised Diesel Control System for eligible sectors. The impact was immediate: transport costs surged, food prices climbed, and businesses passed the costs on to consumers. The government promised B40 and M40 households would be protected through BUDI MADANI cash assistance, but payments were widely criticised as insufficient.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Subsidy reform is necessary — Malaysia was spending RM14.3 billion on diesel subsidies in 2023, much of which leaked to smuggling and foreign vehicles. The fiscal case is strong. But the execution was brutal. A 56% overnight price increase hits working Malaysians hard, especially in logistics-dependent sectors. Research showed businesses across multiple industries faced immediate cost pressures, passing costs directly to consumers — exactly the outcome PH promised to prevent. Sabah and Sarawak were exempted, creating a two-tier system that was as much about coalition management as regional sensitivity.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH spent years attacking BN’s subsidy policies — both for being too generous (fiscal waste) and for any attempt to reform them (burdening the rakyat). Classic opposition have-your-cake-and-eat-it. Now in power, PH removed diesel subsidies with a 56% overnight hike and told Malaysians it was for their own good. The BUDI MADANI cash assistance was modest compared to cascading cost increases. The savings were supposed to fund public transport and targeted transfers. By 2026, public transport outside KL remains minimal, and PADU — the targeting mechanism — was sidelined. The money was saved. The reinvestment was not delivered.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: When Najib attempted fuel subsidy reform in 2014, PH was among the loudest critics. The broader pattern is consistent: PH promises populist policies in opposition, then implements the same tough economic measures in government that they attacked their predecessors for. Subsidy reform, GST (abolished, replaced with less efficient SST), and toll abolition (still hasn’t happened) all follow the same trajectory. AMRO’s analysis recommended a phased approach — PH chose shock therapy instead.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Subsidy reform was necessary. Nobody disputes that. But you could have done it gradually, transparently, and with adequate cushioning. Instead, you did it fast and hard, while your ministers still enjoy subsidised fuel cards. Publish where the RM14.3 billion in annual savings went. Deliver the public transport improvements you promised. If the rakyat must tighten their belts, show them you’re tightening yours first. Or keep implementing overnight shocks while promising reinvestment that never arrives.

Sources: MOF Malaysia | Fulcrum | LSE | AMRO

item.title: [BM] Rangkaian Dwi 5G: Berbilion Dibazir Untuk Kompromi Politik — Satu Rangkaian Sudah Memadai
item.description:
Said by: Telecom Analysts / Consumers

Category: Economy

Date: 2024-06-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada pertengahan 2024, kerajaan PH mengubah pelancaran 5G Malaysia daripada rangkaian borong tunggal (Digital Nasional Berhad, DNB) kepada model rangkaian dwi — membenarkan rangkaian 5G kedua dibina bersama DNB. Langkah ini dilihat secara meluas sebagai kompromi politik untuk menampung pengendali telekomunikasi utama yang mempunyai hubungan politik dan yang menentang model rangkaian tunggal. Pendekatan rangkaian dwi menggandakan kos infrastruktur, memecah kecekapan spektrum, dan melambatkan perluasan liputan luar bandar — semuanya untuk menyelesaikan masalah politik, bukan masalah teknikal.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Infrastruktur 5G menelan kos berbilion. Rangkaian borong tunggal — model yang diguna pakai oleh negara-negara seperti Korea Selatan dan sebahagian Eropah — lebih cekap dari segi spektrum dan mengelakkan pertindihan infrastruktur mahal.
